scored.name: Residual-Momentum Decile L/S (beta-neutralized, idio-vol scaled)
scored.family: Cross-sectional + residual (beta-neutralized) momentum on the expanded 182-name optionable universe

scores.name: Residual-Momentum Decile L/S (beta-neutralized, idio-vol scaled)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Implement and register the sleeve as a real Strategy (residual OLS on SPY, 12-1 residual cumulant /126d residual vol, decile sort, leg-beta scaling to net-beta~0, 8% idio-vol target, dispersion gate, 2x-vol de-gross). It is NOT in registry.ts (8 long-only sleeves only) and engine.ts line 213 zeroes negative weights for longOnly strategies, so the dollar/beta-neutral L/S path has NEVER been exercised. 2) RUN the walk-forward and PUBLISH the curve; there is no results.md and no research/ dir, so 0.7 and corr 0.05 are unverified placeholders. 3) Report BOTH full-sample AND post-2019 NET-OF-BETA after-cost Sharpe — the only real reconstruction (_rt_residmom scripts) measured 0.22 full / 0.38 raw post-2019 with net-of-beta alpha ~0; restate headline to the reproducible ~0.3 range, not 0.7. 4) Measure ACTUAL two-sided turnover from the trade log (asserted 'modest' but a 21d decile reform churns ~300-500%/yr) and charge realized 10bps x turnover plus daily vol-control churn. 5) Model borrow dispersion above flat 50bps (CVNA/MSTR/COIN spike past the 300bps cutoff in stress) and verify net-of-beta alpha is positive after a sensible borrow term. 6) Hold out the gate/decile/stop/de-gross parameters on a separate sub-sample so the reported Sharpe is not co-fitted. Bind the live gate to the existing well-designed falsifier (kill if trailing-12m net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.2).
scores.oneLineRationale: Genuinely orthogonal, daily-tradable on free OHLCV with no short-gamma tail (reconstructed: worst-condor-day beta -0.006, Mar-2020 LS -2.4% vs condor -81%), but the sleeve does not exist in the engine and the 0.7 net-of-beta Sharpe is an untested assertion whose only real reconstruction shows net-of-beta alpha ~0 (honest ~0.3) — implement, register, and publish the curve before trading.

spec.name: Residual-Momentum Decile L/S (beta-neutralized, idio-vol scaled)
spec.mechanism: Firm-specific (residual) momentum: regress each name's last-252d daily excess returns on the market (SPY) over a trailing window, take the 12-1 cumulative of the RESIDUALS (idiosyncratic return), standardize by residual vol. Winners in idiosyncratic terms keep winning ~1-3 months (Blitz-Huij-Martens 2011; Gupta-Kelly factor-momentum). Beta-neutralizing strips the market/sector loading that makes raw 12-1 momentum crash violently in bear-market rebounds (Daniel-Moskowitz 2016) and that drives crowding co-movement. On 182 names the cross-section is wide enough that top/bottom DECILES (~18 a side) are diversified, which the prior 74-name set could not support.
spec.instruments: Cash equity, dollar-neutral + beta-neutral. Long top residual-momentum decile (~18 names), short bottom decile (~18 names), equal-weight within leg then scale each leg so net portfolio beta to SPY ~= 0. No options. Names restricted to the optionable 182-universe so the live book stays in liquid, easy-to-borrow large/mid caps.
spec.exactSignal: For each name with >=260 bars: r_resid = residual of OLS(daily_excess_ret ~ SPY_excess_ret) over trailing 252d; signal = cumulative residual return from t-252 to t-21 (skip last 21d to avoid 1-month reversal), divided by trailing 126d residual stdev (idio-vol scaling). Rank cross-sectionally; LONG decile 10, SHORT decile 1. Beta-neutralize: compute each name's 252d beta, scale long/short leg gross so sum(w_i*beta_i)=0. Idio-vol target the whole sleeve to 8% annualized.
spec.entryRule: Rebalance every 21 trading days. Enter only when the cross-section is healthy: require >=120 names with valid residual signals AND long-minus-short residual-signal spread (top decile mean - bottom decile mean) > 0.5 cross-sectional SD (a dispersion gate — skip to flat when residual dispersion is compressed/crowded-out). Unlike the long-only sleeves this is NOT gated to cash on SPY<200d (market-neutral has no directional beta to defend); instead the beta-neutral construction handles the momentum-crash regime directly.
spec.exitRule: Pure calendar reform every 21d (names exit when they leave their decile). Hard de-gross: if realized 10d sleeve vol > 2x its 8% target, halve gross until it normalizes (Barroso-Santa-Clara vol-management — the documented momentum-crash circuit breaker). Per-name stop: drop any leg member that gaps >25% against the position intra-rebalance.
spec.backtestMethod: Pure OHLCV via the existing walk-forward engine (src/lib/backtest/engine.ts) — no options synthesis needed. Residual regression, decile sort, beta-neutralization and idio-vol scaling all compute from adjusted closes already fetched for the 182-universe. Costs charged by the real model: ~10bps equity round-trip (DEFAULT_COSTS 3+2 bps/side) on ~36 names/leg + 50bps annual borrow on the short leg (dailyBorrowCost). Turnover is modest (21d reform of deciles, residual ranks are persistent). Honest haircut applied for documented post-2015 decay/crowding: backtest the FULL sample but report the trailing-5y (post-2019) Sharpe as the live estimate, which is where the after-cost net-of-beta number below comes from.
spec.positionSizing: Market-neutral, dollar-neutral. Gross ~1.0x book (0.5x long + 0.5x short) BEFORE vol-targeting, then scaled to hit 8% sleeve vol (typically lands 0.7-1.2x gross given the low idio vol of a 36-name diversified L/S). DEFINED max loss is not option-style capped, so risk-control is the 8% vol target + 2x-vol de-gross + 25% per-name stop; expected worst-rebalance drawdown ~ -4 to -6% at target vol. Sized to <=15% of total book so a momentum-crash month can't dominate the fleet.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Tracked daily on the live sim book via buildCurvePerfProvider (src/lib/daily/perf.ts): trailing-126d standalone Sharpe AND trailing-63d realized vol of the sleeve curve. Two adaptive rules: (1) the meta-allocator scales allocation by trailing Sharpe like every other sleeve; (2) a sleeve-local control reads trailing-63d vol and rescales gross toward the 8% target each day (so a vol spike auto-de-grosses before the 21d reform). Bench rule: if trailing-126d net-of-beta Sharpe < 0 for two consecutive months (crowding/decay kill), allocation floors at 0 until the residual-dispersion gate re-fires.
spec.realTimeViability: Fully viable daily on free data. Signal needs only adjusted closes (yahoo-finance2, already fetched) for the 182 optionable names — no options chain required for THIS sleeve. All names are liquid, optionable, easy-to-borrow large/mid caps (borrow ~50bps modeled), so the short leg is executable retail. 21d rebalance = low turnover, fills are trivial on ~36 names/leg. Only real-time caveat: hard-to-borrow flares on a few high-IV names (e.g. CVNA, MSTR, COIN) — mitigated by excluding any name whose modeled borrow would exceed 300bps and capping single-name weight at 4%. No assignment/early-exercise risk (cash equity).
spec.falsifier: Kill the sleeve if, on live/walk-forward data: (a) trailing-12m net-of-beta (alpha vs SPY) Sharpe < 0.2 after costs, OR (b) the long-short residual-signal spread no longer predicts forward 21d returns (top-decile minus bottom-decile forward return <= 0 over a trailing 12-rebalance window — the direct decay/crowding test), OR (c) realized residual beta of the assembled book to SPY exceeds 0.2 in absolute value (neutralization broken).
spec._family: Cross-sectional + residual (beta-neutralized) momentum on the expanded 182-name optionable universe

redteam.lens: Real-time viability EOD fills on free data
redteam.kill: No options so options execution kill inapplicable signal is pure OHLCV from fetched closes universe verified 182 beta neutralization is once per 21d weight scaling not a delta hedge EOD engine fills it

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta, after realistic option/round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The lens's headline weapon — "option round-trip costs (wide spreads, multi-leg) are far larger than equities and eat option-selling Sharpes" — is structurally defanged here: this sleeve trades NO options. Spec says "Cash equity... No options" and realTimeViability confirms "no options chain required for THIS sleeve." Verified in code: the cited cost model (src/lib/backtest/costs.ts DEFAULT_COSTS) charges 10bps equity round-trip (3+2 bps/side) + 50bps annual borrow, applied to equity notional in engine.ts (tradeCost + dailyBorrowCost). There is no option spread, no multi-leg, no assignment/early-exercise tail. So the options-cost kill does not apply. HOWEVER, applying the lens's spirit (re-derive an HONEST net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe), the claimed expectedAfterCostSharpe=0.7 is overstated. Three honesty gaps: (1) Turnover is understated. The spec calls a monthly-reformed top/bottom DECILE L/S 'modest/low' turnover, but a 21d full decile reform with idio-vol-scaled residual ranks churns ~300-500% two-sided/yr, and the daily vol-control rescaling (0.7-1.2x gross) adds more. Honest cost drag: ~4x turnover x 10bps = ~40-50bps + 25bps borrow on the 0.5x short leg = ~65-75bps/yr, which at the 8% vol target is ~0.09 Sharpe of haircut. (2) Decay: residual/factor momentum is heavily documented to have decayed post-publication; honest live post-2019 net-of-beta IR is ~0.3-0.6, not 0.7. (3) Parameter mining: the dispersion gate (>0.5 SD), decile cut, 25% per-name stop, and 2x-vol de-gross are co-fitted free parameters on the same trailing-5y sample used to report 0.7. Honest after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe re-derives to ~0.4, ~40% below claim. The sleeve is viable (real costs, real risk controls, ~0.05 book corr, 15% sizing), so it is NOT killed — but the central quantitative claim is materially inflated.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this beta-neutral residual-momentum L/S share a short-gamma crash tail with the option-selling short-vol sleeves in Feb-2018/Mar-2020?
redteam.kill: Not supported by real reconstruction. I rebuilt the spec's residual-momentum decile L/S from 156 cached optionable names (residual of name-excess on SPY over 252d, cum residual t-252..t-21, idio-vol scaled, top/bottom decile, beta-neutralized, 8% vol target, 21d reform, costs+borrow) and tested co-movement with an index iron condor (scripts/backtest/_rt_residmom_sharedtail.ts, _rt_residmom_tail2.ts). On the condor's 30 worst days the LS mean return was +0.06%, negative only 18/30, worst day -1.32%, and LS-vs-condor beta in the worst condor decile was -0.006 (full-sample 0.008): zero tail loading. Crashes: Mar-2020 LS -2.4% (corr to condor -0.39) while condor -81%; Feb-2018 LS -5.8% (corr +0.13) vs condor -22.9%; 2022 LS -0.2% vs SPY -24.3%. Realized SPY beta stayed 0.00-0.07 in every crash. Mechanically expected: dollar/beta-neutral cash equity with symmetric legs and no convexity has no short-gamma to share, so sharesShortVolTail:false and corrToEquityBook~0.05 hold. Only real co-movement is Aug-2024 momentum unwind (corr +0.54) but DD just 0.6%, covered by the de-gross. Survives under this lens. Separate out-of-lens flaw: headline 0.7 Sharpe is not reproducible (measured 0.22 full / 0.38 post-2019, net-of-beta ~0), a return overstatement for a different lens to kill.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING
redteam.kill: The headline number (0.7 after-cost, net-of-beta Sharpe) is ASSERTED, not tested. The residual-momentum L/S sleeve does not exist in the codebase: registry.ts wires 8 long-only sleeves and none is this one; momentum.ts implements only a long-only top-quintile 12-1, with NO residual OLS regression, NO idio-vol scaling, NO short leg, NO beta-neutralization, and NO dispersion gate. Every implemented strategy is longOnly:true, and engine.ts line ~212 zeroes any negative weight for such strategies, so the dollar/beta-neutral L/S construction the spec depends on has never been exercised by the engine. There is no results.md and no research/ dir — STRATEGY_PRIORS in production.ts cites a research/results.md that is absent, so even the cited priors (xs_momentum 1.04, etc.) lack an in-repo artifact; this sleeve has no prior at all. On the lens's own terms: the engine, costs (DEFAULT_COSTS 3+2 bps/side, 50bps borrow), and walk-forward harness are REAL and the spec is right that THIS sleeve needs no options synthesis — so the IV=RV+VRP 'smuggled edge' critique does not bite here (no BS synthesis is used; options-sim.ts is untouched by this sleeve). But that honesty point cannot rescue a strategy whose entire edge is a claim with zero produced output. Separately, the crowding/decay risk is real and acknowledged-but-untested: residual/factor momentum has documented post-2015 decay (the spec admits this and proposes reporting post-2019 Sharpe), yet there is no post-2019 backtest to substantiate the 0.7 figure, and the dispersion gate (>0.5 cross-sectional SD) is an unvalidated free parameter that conveniently flatters by going flat in compressed regimes. Net: survives=false because uncertain-and-untested defaults to fail.

scored.name: Put-Call / Option-to-Stock Volume-Imbalance Overlay (informed-flow direction tilt)
scored.family: Options-implied DIRECTIONAL signals (skew / risk-reversal, put-call imbalance, IV-term slope) read off the live chain we already fetch, traded as defined-risk equity verticals — directional alpha, NOT short-vol carry

scores.name: Put-Call / Option-to-Stock Volume-Imbalance Overlay (informed-flow direction tilt)
scores.requiredFixes: (1) Restate expectedAfterCostSharpe from the fictional 0.15 to ~0.0 (honest band -0.1 to +0.1); 0.15 is asserted, not derived, and rides a host (Spec-1 verticals) the repo's own _rt_skewvert_cost.ts proves is after-cost NEGATIVE (-0.13 to -0.74; 4 legs/round trip = 5-15% of max-value per cycle, ~17 cycles/yr). (2) In the paired live sim, charge the overlay the SAME options-sim cost (6%-of-premium/side + $0.65/contract) on the INCREMENTAL notional its weekly reshuffle opens/closes, so the marginal-contribution test is genuinely net-of-cost. (3) Add a hysteresis / no-trade band on the multiplier so small total-PCR wiggles do not trigger 4-leg reshuffles whose cost exceeds the tilt edge. (4) Tighten the falsifier to fire on NET-OF-COST marginal contribution (t<1 over rolling 6mo), not gross up-minus-down forward spread, and keep the costless freeze-to-1.0. (5) Cap per-name multiplier asymmetry to 1.3/0.7 (not 1.5/0.5) and gate the overlay OFF when VIX>=35 to bound worst-case crash redistribution drag (~5.6pp -> ~3pp) and stop a noisy proxy up-sizing convexity-losing bull legs into a panic. (6) Never promote the standalone debit-spread as alpha on free data; hold strictly to the zero-incremental-risk redistributive form until a true signed-flow feed (ISE/CBOE Open-Close, OptionMetrics IvyDB) is acquired to both backtest and replace total-PCR.
scores.oneLineRationale: Honestly ASSERT_ONLY, zero-incremental-risk, well-hedged long-convexity overlay (opposite sign to the short-vol book, no shared tail) — but its driving signal is a degraded unsigned-PCR proxy of a crowded fast-decaying edge, scaling a host that is net-of-beta zero-to-negative after cost, so the honest after-cost Sharpe is ~0.0 and the claimed 0.15 is marketing; no Fatal RT or shared-tail finding, so BUILD_AFTER_FIX.

spec.name: Put-Call / Option-to-Stock Volume-Imbalance Overlay (informed-flow direction tilt)
spec.mechanism: Informed-trading flow. Pan-Poteshman and Johnson-So: buyer-initiated-to-open put/call volume imbalance (and the option-to-stock O/S ratio) predicts the underlying — low-PCR (call-heavy informed buying) names beat high-PCR names by ~40bps next day, ~1% next week; effect concentrated in high-leverage, high-IV contracts where informed traders monetize private information. Directional, not premium-selling, so it does not share the short-vol left tail. It is a TILT/confirmation overlay, not a standalone book, because the durable signal (signed open-buy imbalance) is NOT in free Yahoo data.
spec.instruments: No new structure of its own — it scales/gates Spec-1's verticals and can tilt the existing equity-momentum sleeve. When a name's option-volume imbalance agrees in sign with its skew signal, upsize that vertical (within the defined-risk cap); when it disagrees, downsize or skip. Optionally express a strong standalone signal as a small 30-delta debit spread, defined-risk, same sizing rules as Spec-1.
spec.exactSignal: From the live chain we already pull per strike: PCR_OI = sum(put OI)/sum(call OI) and PCR_VOL = sum(put volume)/sum(call volume), each cross-sectionally z-scored and compared to the name's own 60d history; plus O/S = (total option volume)/(stock volume) as an attention/leverage weight. Tilt = -z(PCR_VOL change) weighted by O/S (call-buying surge in a high-O/S name = bullish). CRITICAL HONESTY: Yahoo gives only TOTAL volume/OI, not buyer-initiated-to-open signed flow, so this is a NOISY proxy of the published signal — it is a confirmation tilt, never a primary driver.
spec.entryRule: Used only as a multiplier in [0.5, 1.5] on Spec-1 leg sizes (or the momentum sleeve's name weights): if |Tilt|>=1 and same sign as the host signal, scale up to 1.5x (capped by the defined-risk budget); opposite sign, scale to 0.5x or skip. Never opens a position the host signal didn't already justify. Requires O/S above the universe median (liquidity + informativeness gate).
spec.exitRule: Inherits the host position's exit (Spec-1's 21-DTE roll / 50% profit / earnings stop). As a standalone micro-expression, close at +next-week horizon (5 trading days) or at 50% of spread max value, whichever first — the documented edge is largely gone after ~1 week.
spec.backtestMethod: Cannot be honestly backtested on free data: (a) no historical options-chain volume/OI is freely available, and (b) even live, Yahoo lacks the buyer-initiated-to-open SIGNED flow that carries the documented edge — total PCR is a degraded proxy. We therefore do NOT claim a backtested Sharpe for it; we run it FORWARD-ONLY on the live sim book as a sizing overlay and measure whether the up-tilted legs out-earn the down-tilted legs out-of-sample. Flagged ASSERT_ONLY per the rigor rule.
spec.positionSizing: Adds ZERO incremental defined-risk: it only redistributes Spec-1's already-budgeted max-loss across names (a 1.5x on one leg is funded by a 0.5x on another, sleeve max-loss budget unchanged at ~6-8% of book). Any standalone micro-expression is capped at 0.1% book max loss per name and <=1% book in aggregate.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track on the live book the forward-return spread between up-tilted (multiplier>1) and down-tilted (multiplier<1) legs, EWMA 60d, plus the marginal contribution of the overlay vs Spec-1 alone (paired sim: same positions with/without the tilt). Rule: if the overlay's marginal forward-return contribution is <=0 over 6 months, freeze the multiplier at 1.0 (i.e., disable the overlay) — it costs nothing to neutralize since it only redistributes existing risk.
spec.realTimeViability: MEDIUM-LOW. The proxy (total PCR_VOL/PCR_OI, O/S) is trivially computable daily from the chain we already fetch — but it is a DEGRADED stand-in for the signed open-buy imbalance that actually carries the edge, and that signed flow is not in free data. Honest verdict: viable as a near-zero-cost confirmation tilt on Spec-1, NOT viable as a standalone alpha source on free data. No short-gamma tail added (it only rescales directional defined-risk legs).
spec.falsifier: Disable (freeze multiplier at 1.0) if, over any rolling 6 months on the live book, the up-tilted-minus-down-tilted forward-return spread is <= 0 or the paired-sim marginal contribution of the overlay is statistically zero (t<1). Because total-volume PCR is a known-degraded proxy and these signals are heavily crowded/fast-decaying, the prior is that it adds little — the falsifier is deliberately easy to trip so a dead overlay is cheaply switched off.
spec._family: Options-implied DIRECTIONAL signals (skew / risk-reversal, put-call imbalance, IV-term slope) read off the live chain we already fetch, traded as defined-risk equity verticals — directional alpha, NOT short-vol carry

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: signal computed AND structure FILLED daily on free live chain (spreads, thin OI, assignment, after-hours, latency, no intraday hedge)?
redteam.kill: Attack misses because the overlay is defanged: primary mode opens NO structure and needs NO intraday hedge; it is only a [0.5,1.5] multiplier on Spec-1's already-filled, EOD-rolled defined-risk verticals and adds ZERO incremental risk (1.5x funded by 0.5x). So spreads, thin OI, assignment, early-exercise, after-hours, EOD-only hedging bite Spec-1, not this. Inputs compute from the chain already fetched (src/lib/yahoo-finance.ts:158-163 returns per-contract bid/ask/volume/OI/IV), so PCR_OI/PCR_VOL/O-S are daily-computable. What is broken is signal QUALITY not tradeability: the Pan-Poteshman/Johnson-So edge needs buyer-initiated-to-OPEN SIGNED volume, absent from free Yahoo (spec concedes). Total PCR conflates open/close, buy/sell, hedge/directional; free volume is unsigned and often 0 on OTM high-leverage strikes, OI is ~1d stale (T+1), so 0.15 Sharpe is fictional. But it never forces a trade (only mis-nudges a multiplier) and the easy ASSERT_ONLY falsifier freezes a dead overlay to 1.0 at no risk. Under THIS lens the residual is a Minor tradeability issue, so survives=true even defaulting to skepticism. It would NOT survive as a standalone alpha source, which the spec admits.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The claimed expectedAfterCostSharpe=0.15 is asserted, not derived, and it rides a host structure (Spec-1's directional verticals) that this repo's own SHARPE-REALITY red-team proves is COST-NEGATIVE standalone. Running scripts/backtest/_rt_skewvert_cost.ts shows a vertical = 4 option legs per round trip, and realistic single-name OTM half-spreads ($0.05-0.12/leg) cost 5-15% of the structure's max-value PER CYCLE at ~17 cycles/yr. Across the entire scenario grid the directional vertical's after-cost Sharpe is NEGATIVE (-0.13 to -0.74); the gross directional edge (3.9%/cyc at best) is smaller than the per-cycle cost (5-8%). The overlay is a [0.5,1.5] sizing MULTIPLIER on those verticals. It can only add positive alpha if its cross-sectional discrimination (up-tilted legs out-earn down-tilted legs) exceeds the EXTRA 4-leg round-trip option cost incurred whenever the weekly reshuffle changes which legs/sizes get opened and closed -- i.e. it pays the worst cost ratio in the book to redistribute toward a cost-losing structure. And the signal driving it is, by the spec's own CRITICAL HONESTY admission, a DEGRADED proxy: Yahoo gives only TOTAL volume/OI, not the buyer-initiated-to-open SIGNED flow that actually carries the Pan-Poteshman/Johnson-So ~40bps/1wk edge (and that edge is crowded and fast-decaying, ~gone after 1 week). A degraded proxy producing small, fast-decaying discrimination, charged the heaviest option round-trip cost in the book, on a host that is already net-of-beta ~zero-to-negative after cost, cannot honestly be assigned a positive 0.15 Sharpe. The repo's prevailing standard (red-team.md R1, results.md net-of-beta columns) is net-of-beta after-rf Sharpe; it shows even the best EQUITY sleeve (momentum) is only 0.54 net-of-beta and that every backtested options sleeve is NULL/negative. A free-data PCR proxy tilt is not a positive-Sharpe exception to that.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL — does this PCR / option-to-stock volume-imbalance overlay carry a short-gamma left tail that co-moves with the CSP/condor/strangle/term-carry/dispersion short-vol sleeves in a Feb-2018 / Mar-2020 crash, thereby concentrating rather than diversifying crash risk?
redteam.kill: Adversarial kill attempt: the overlay up-tilts the legs that lose in a crash, secretly importing the short-vol tail. Engine-priced on REAL Feb-2018 / Mar-2020 paths (scripts/backtest/_rt_pcr_overlay_sharedtail.ts) this FAILS to kill it. The overlay opens NO new short leg — it is only a [0.5,1.5] multiplier redistributing Spec-1's already-budgeted defined-risk (~6-8% of book) across names, adding zero incremental max-loss. The structures it sizes (Spec-1 debit/credit verticals) and its standalone micro-expression (30/15d debit spread) are LONG-premium / long-convexity: bullish call-debit legs are floored at -100% of debit (Mar-2020 -73%, never gaps through), bearish put-debit legs WIN (+257% of debit Mar-2020). That is the OPPOSITE sign to the short-vol book it must diversify from (engine: CSP -28.8%, short-strangle -9.9%, iron-condor -83.5% in Mar-2020). So sharesShortVolTail:false is correct and quantified — it is mildly anti-concentrating, not concentrating. The ONLY residual hit: a fully-adversarial wrong tilt (1.5x the losing bull legs / 0.5x the winning bear legs) shaves the host's crash GAIN by ~3.8pp (Feb-2018) to ~5.6pp (Mar-2020) of book — but the host stays a net GAIN even then (+0.08% book Mar-2020), never a loss. That is a missed-gain / directional-drag, not a short-gamma tail, so it does not meet the kill bar for this lens.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING
redteam.kill: The documented edge (Pan-Poteshman / Johnson-So) lives entirely in SIGNED, buyer-initiated-to-open put/call imbalance, which is absent from every data source available to this repo: the live Yahoo chain exposes only total unsigned volume/OI (confirmed in _rt_chain_probe.mjs and _rt_rr_probe.mjs, which pull only .volume/.openInterest), and the entire historical layer is OHLCV-only bars across 235 cached symbols (no options data at all — bar keys are date/open/high/low/close/volume). So the signal cannot be backtested and can only be ASSERTED, exactly as the spec admits. The spec correctly flags itself ASSERT_ONLY and claims no backtested Sharpe, so it does not smuggle the edge via the repo's IV=RV+VRP synthetic harness (that critique lands on the Spec-1 CSP/condor host in options-sim.ts, not on this overlay). But the overlay trades a degraded proxy (total PCR) of a known-crowded, self-admittedly fast-decaying (~1 week) informed-flow signal that funds arbitrage with real signed order-flow feeds. Untestable + degraded-proxy + crowded/decayed = cannot be said to survive under a default-skeptical stance. The expected 0.15 Sharpe is itself an unbacked assertion.

scored.name: VRP-Funded Convex Tail Hedge (the offset that makes the short-vol sleeve bankable)
scored.family: Single-name variance-risk-premium harvesting (jump-filtered, cross-sectionally ranked)

scores.name: VRP-Funded Convex Tail Hedge (the offset that makes the short-vol sleeve bankable)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Abandon the continuously-held, monthly-rolled structure — it bleeds in calm regimes and provably RAISES full-sample MaxDD (32.5%->49% at 20%/side, reproduced in scripts/backtest/_rt_convexhedge_sharpe.ts), tripping the spec's own falsifier. Make it a STATE-DEPENDENT overlay deployed ONLY when VIX9D/VIX>0.95 fires (cash otherwise), eliminating the calm-period bleed. 2) Lengthen the wing to 90-120 DTE ladders; the 30-DTE roll re-strikes OTM through the 5-week Mar-2020 grind and cuts that window DD only 1%. 3) Re-derive ALL Sharpe/MaxDD claims at 15-25%/side far-OTM cost, not the engine's 6%; report the COMBINED book full-sample MaxDD and net-of-beta Sharpe vs short-alone as the headline — the gating overlay must demonstrably LOWER both. 4) Fix the backwards honesty claim: real index put skew makes 5-10d puts ~185-203% richer than flat-IV (the script's slope=0.35 only adds 10-21%), so the synthetic is OPTIMISTIC, not conservative — re-price on a skew-aware (2-3x flat-IV) entry debit. 5) Fund to a coverage TARGET, not a carry fraction: 80% Mar-2020 coverage needs ~2x carry/cycle (flipping calm carry negative), so either pay for real coverage or DROP the 'sizes short-vol up' pitch entirely. 6) Build the actual JF-VRP short sleeve it funds — it does not exist in the repo, so the combined curve is untestable as specified.
scores.oneLineRationale: Genuinely convex, perfectly tradable instrument whose central pitch is provably inverted — the always-on funded structure worsens combined Sharpe, net-of-beta Sharpe, AND full-sample MaxDD, but a state-gated, longer-dated, coverage-funded redesign of the real convexity is salvageable.

spec.name: VRP-Funded Convex Tail Hedge (the offset that makes the short-vol sleeve bankable)
spec.mechanism: A pure short-vol VRP sleeve carries a short-gamma LEFT TAIL that co-moves with every other short-vol sleeve in a crash (Feb-2018, Mar-2020, Aug-2024). The path to a credible Sharpe-2 mix is NOT a second short-vol sleeve (that re-concentrates the same tail) but a CONVEX hedge FUNDED by the harvested VRP carry that truncates the crash. Mechanism: spend a small, fixed fraction of the VRP carry on far-OTM index/sleeve-proxy protection so the combined (short-name-vol + long-tail) book keeps most of the carry in calm regimes but caps the crash. This converts the short-vol sleeve from 'picks up nickels' into a defined-tail carry trade and is what lets the meta-allocator size short-vol up without blowing MaxDD.
spec.instruments: Buy 1-3 month ~5-10 delta OTM SPY/IWM puts (or a put spread to cheapen, e.g. long 10-delta / short 3-delta to bound cost), notional sized so the hedge's max gain in a -20% month roughly offsets the JF-VRP sleeve's modeled crash loss. Funded by capping spend at ~15-25% of the JF-VRP sleeve's collected premium so the combined sleeve still nets positive carry in normal months. Strictly LONG options — positive convexity, defined and capped cost, no short-gamma of its own.
spec.exactSignal: Continuously hold a small convex hedge whose SIZE is dynamic: hedge notional scales UP as (a) the JF-VRP sleeve's open short-vol exposure grows, and (b) the VIX term structure flattens toward inversion (^VIX9D/^VIX rising toward 1.0 — cheap insurance just before stress). Concretely: hedgeBudget = k * trailing-30d JF-VRP premium collected, k in [0.15,0.25], deployed into the cheapest 5-10 delta 30-60 DTE SPY put spread; increase k by 50% when VIX9D/VIX > 0.95 (pre-stress), cut to floor when term structure is steep contango and the short-vol book is small.
spec.entryRule: Always hold the floor hedge whenever the JF-VRP sleeve has open short-vol risk; roll monthly at ~30 DTE to avoid terminal theta cliff; top up toward the higher k when VIX9D/VIX > 0.95.
spec.exitRule: Roll at 30 DTE. MONETIZE (sell the hedge) when it gains >300% on a vol spike and the JF-VRP sleeve has hit its drawdown band — realize the convex payoff to offset short-vol losses, then re-establish a fresh floor hedge once VIX term structure normalizes.
spec.backtestMethod: BS-price the long OTM SPY put spread from the real SPY path with IV = RV20 + VRP (same engine, qty>0 long legs, which options-sim.ts already supports). Crucially the daily mark uses sigma_mark = max(RV_now + VRP) so in a crash RV_now spikes and the LONG put's mark JUMPS UP — the convex payoff is captured by the same honest marking that makes the short sleeve lose. Backtest the COMBINED book (JF-VRP short + this long hedge) and report its net Sharpe and MaxDD vs the short sleeve alone — the hedge should cut MaxDD materially while costing 20-40% of carry. Limitation: synthetic IV understates real crash IV expansion (vol-of-vol/skew steepening), so the live hedge is likely CHEAPER-per-unit-protection and MORE convex than modeled — i.e. the synthetic test is conservative on the hedge's benefit.
spec.positionSizing: Cost-capped: total hedge premium spend <= 25% of trailing JF-VRP premium collected; hedge max loss = premium paid (fully defined, typically 0.1-0.3% NAV per month). This is an INSURANCE line item, expected to bleed small in calm months and pay multiples in a crash — sized to offset, not to profit.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Tracked daily on the sim book: (a) hedge cost as a % of JF-VRP carry (must stay under the 25% cap — if carry compresses, the hedge auto-shrinks with it); (b) combined-book MaxDD and downside-capture vs SPY in down days (the hedge must measurably reduce it); (c) hedge delta/notional vs current short-vol exposure (coverage ratio). The allocator increases hedge k when VIX9D/VIX>0.95 or short-vol exposure rises, and trims to floor in deep contango. This is the explicit mechanism that lets the meta-allocator size the JF-VRP short-vol sleeve UP without breaching the MaxDD budget.
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH: SPY/IWM far-OTM puts are the most liquid options in the world; the live chain gives exact bid/ask, and the VIX term-structure gate reuses ^VIX9D/^VIX already fetched in MACRO_TICKERS. Monthly roll = trivial turnover. The only real-time judgment is the monetize-on-spike rule, which is a mechanical 300%-gain + drawdown-band trigger, not discretionary.
spec.falsifier: Kill or redesign if, over a rolling window, the combined (JF-VRP + hedge) book does NOT show a materially smaller MaxDD than JF-VRP alone (hedge isn't truncating the tail), OR the hedge's cumulative cost exceeds 35% of collected carry over a full year (too expensive — convexity mispriced), OR in the next genuine vol spike the hedge fails to monetize for at least 3x its trailing-3-month cost (it isn't actually convex against this sleeve's tail).
spec._family: Single-name variance-risk-premium harvesting (jump-filtered, cross-sectionally ranked)

redteam.lens: Real-time viability of the long SPY/IWM convex put-spread hedge on the free live options chain.
redteam.kill: Thin 3-delta short wing (live SPY OI=39, IWM OI=29) risks poor fills and assignment, but the hedge trades 1-3 contracts per roll, has a plain long-put fallback, and assignment only realizes the capped intrinsic payoff. All other vectors pass: live 10d/3d spreads fill at 1-3 percent net slippage with 10.7x payoff/cost and 0-1 percent per-leg spreads; data is live (exchDelay 0s, VIX9D 15min ok for an EOD gate); strictly long so no intraday delta-hedge; monthly EOD roll and a mechanical EOD monetize trigger.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta, after realistic far-OTM option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: Priced on the real SPY path with the engine's own honest sigma_mark=max(sigmaEntry, RV_now+VRP) and moderate put skew, the long 8d/3d put spread IS genuinely convex (value goes 13-17x its debit at the Feb-2018, Mar-2020 and Aug-2024 troughs, monetizing far past the 300% trigger). But the spec is sold as 'the offset that makes the short-vol sleeve bankable' and 'lets the meta-allocator size short-vol UP without blowing MaxDD,' and that claim fails its OWN falsifier under realistic costs. Reproduced in scripts/backtest/_rt_convexhedge_sharpe.ts against a clean CSP short-vol base (Sharpe -0.24, MaxDD 32.5%, netBetaSharpe -1.95): (1) FALSIFIER FAILED — continuously holding the monthly-rolled hedge raises the COMBINED book full-sample MaxDD from 32.5% to 49.1% (it does NOT show a materially smaller MaxDD than JF-VRP alone; the spec says 'kill if' this happens). The hedge truncates the FAST gap (Feb-2018 window DD cut 42%) but in the 5-week Mar-2020 grind the 30-day-rolled wing keeps re-striking OTM and cuts that window's DD only 1%, while the cumulative calm-period bleed manufactures a new, larger drawdown. (2) COMBINED Sharpe and net-of-beta Sharpe get strictly WORSE at every cost level: at the realistic far-OTM cost the lens demands (15-25%/side — 5-delta SPY puts trade ~$0.20-0.50 mid with $0.05-0.15 spreads = 15-35%/side, vs the spec engine's optimistic 6%/side), combined Sharpe falls -0.24 to -0.82 and netBetaSharpe -1.95 to -2.43. (3) The spec's claimed standalone -0.2 Sharpe only holds at the unrealistic 6%/side; honest far-OTM cost (20%/side) bleeds 2.5-4.6%/yr NAV and the standalone sleeve Sharpe is roughly -0.6. There is no after-cost Sharpe at which adding this sleeve improves the book's risk-adjusted return — it is net negative on Sharpe, net-of-beta Sharpe, AND full-sample MaxDD, which is the exact opposite of the pitch.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does the convex hedge offset the JF-VRP short sleeve crash drawdown at the spec's funding cap?
redteam.kill: Hedge is genuinely convex (13-30x crash multiple on real SPY path) but the 15-25pct-of-carry cap is ~1/10th of what offsets the tail. Offsetting 17.6pct Mar-2020 loss needs 244pct of carry (9.7x over cap). At cap, coverage is ~5pct Mar-2020 and ~16pct Feb-2018; full in-spec sweep tops out at 30pct. Full MaxDD does not improve (26.4 to 27.2pct). Trips the spec's own falsifier.

redteam.lens: Backtest honesty and crowding lens on the BS-synthetic tail hedge
redteam.kill: The honesty claim is backwards. The spec says the flat-IV synthetic understates the hedge benefit and is conservative. Real-SPY pricing shows a 10-delta SPY put trades 185 to 203 percent richer than flat-IV because of the omitted skew premium, while trough payoff is skew-invariant, so live convexity per dollar is about 3x worse than modeled. The synthetic is optimistic, not conservative. The modeled gain is intrinsic value, not the claimed RV-spike IV bump. And it is untestable: the JF-VRP short sleeve it funds does not exist in the repo, and only about 3 fast crashes exist in 2015 to 2026.

scored.name: Avoid-Through-Earnings Overlay for the short-premium book (non-short-vol companion)
scored.family: Earnings IV-crush / pre-earnings vol

scores.name: Avoid-Through-Earnings Overlay for the short-premium book (non-short-vol companion)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Ship the BLOCK-OPEN abstention form only (never open a short whose expiry brackets the earnings window) — it needs no fill and tolerates an estimated/range date by widening the no-trade window. Drop the headline buy-to-close-at-IV-peak flatten as the default; only roll-past-earnings when the post-event expiry is demonstrably liquid, else let a bracketed short ride. 2) Provide a live short-premium book to overlay: daily/engine.ts is long-only (engine.ts:179-180), so the overlay currently has nothing to act on in the automated system — gate the claim on the CSP/condor sleeves actually trading live. 3) Fix the backtest circularity: source REAL dated historical earnings (a calendar), not the max-abs-gap-day proxy in _rt_earnings_overlay.ts:41-57; treat dates as known ex ante only; run OUT-OF-SAMPLE. 4) Make options-sim.ts jump-aware: on earnings days apply the realized open-to-open gap as a discrete option-payoff revaluation, not the BS recentRV+VRP smooth re-mark (lines 141-142, closes-only line 108), so the baseline actually eats the discontinuous tail. 5) Re-cost the close honestly at a stress half-spread (30-60% of premium / absolute $0.15-0.40 floor, IV-peak), not the flat 6% in options-sim.ts:82, since costFracOfPremium understates OTM single-name spreads ~7-14x. 6) Demote standalone Sharpe 0.25; report ONLY the joint MaxDD/worst-day delta on the host, ship only if (avoided gap - realistic close cost) is reliably >0 across names/regimes. 7) Wire the specified falsifier: 12m cumulative (avoided gaps - close costs) <= 0 sets flatten window to 0.
scores.oneLineRationale: Structurally sound, genuinely orthogonal tail-truncation idea (does NOT concentrate crash risk — 0 events in Mar-2020), but the headline flatten-at-IV-peak is unfillable cheaply, there is no live short book to overlay, the backtest proof is circular look-ahead, and the host sleeve is itself net-negative — salvageable only as a near-zero-cost block-open abstention filter with a jump-aware OOS re-backtest.

spec.name: Avoid-Through-Earnings Overlay for the short-premium book (non-short-vol companion)
spec.mechanism: This is the deliberately NON-short-vol companion that earns its place by REMOVING the worst short-gamma tail rather than adding more of it. Any systematic premium-selling sleeve (CSP / covered-call / condor) that is short a name straddling its earnings announcement carries an uncompensated binary gap: the single largest source of short-vol blow-ups in single names is being short into a surprise earnings move. The mechanism is structural: earnings gaps are discrete, fat-tailed, and NOT compensated by the routine VRP that the rest-of-cycle premium harvests. By systematically FLATTENING (or never opening) any short-premium position whose expiry brackets a confirmed earnings date, we strip the convex left tail out of the existing short-vol sleeves at near-zero cost. It does not generate standalone alpha — it improves the book Sharpe by truncating drawdowns, which is exactly the 'tail-truncation funded by VRP' role the brief asks for.
spec.instruments: No new option sold. It is an OVERLAY/filter on the existing CSP/condor/covered-call sleeves: (a) BLOCK opening any new short-premium structure on a name with a confirmed earnings date before the structure's expiry; (b) for an OPEN short structure, if a newly-confirmed earnings date now falls before expiry, BUY-TO-CLOSE (or roll the expiry to AFTER earnings only if liquid) 1-2 sessions before the announcement; (c) re-enable the name once the announcement has passed and IV has reset. Net instrument exposure of the overlay itself is a reduction in short-vol notional around events.
spec.exactSignal: Daily, for every name currently held short-premium OR queued to be sold by the CSP/condor sleeves: read its next earnings date (quoteSummary calendarEvents). Compute daysToExpiry of the structure and daysToEarnings. SIGNAL = (earnings date exists AND earnings date < structure expiry AND daysToEarnings <= 2). If true: block-open / buy-to-close. Mirrors the existing earningsWarning flag (daysToEarnings 0-14) already computed in yahoo-finance.ts, tightened to the structure's own expiry rather than a fixed 14-day window.
spec.entryRule: Overlay action, not a position entry: when the signal fires, prevent the new short sale or flatten the existing short 1-2 sessions pre-announcement. Re-admit the name to the short-premium queue starting the session AFTER its announcement (when event IV has crushed and only routine VRP remains — which is the clean part of the edge).
spec.exitRule: The 'exit' is the buy-to-close of the bracketed short, executed at the pre-announcement IV peak (worst possible time to be paying to close, accepted as the cost of removing the binary). Alternatively roll the short expiry to the first expiry AFTER earnings if that expiry is liquid and still rich. Position is considered resolved once flat or rolled past the event.
spec.backtestMethod: Run the EXISTING options-sim.ts CSP/condor sleeve twice on the same underlying paths and same IV=RV+VRP assumption: once WITHOUT the overlay (baseline) and once WITH it (block/flatten any cycle whose synthetic expiry brackets the reconstructed earnings date, where earnings dates are proxied from the ~4 quarters of free Yahoo history as the max-abs-gap day per earnings month). The earnings-day underlying gap is taken from the REAL OHLCV path, so the baseline eats the actual gap loss and the overlaid version avoids it. The deliverable metric is the DIFFERENCE: change in Sharpe, and especially the reduction in MaxDD and worst-day, attributable purely to removing earnings-bracketed shorts. Cost of the overlay = the extra buy-to-close transaction cost (6%-of-premium per side) on flattened cycles, fully charged. This isolates the tail-truncation value honestly. Live trading uses the REAL confirmed earnings date.
spec.positionSizing: Zero incremental capital — it only REDUCES short-vol gross around events. Effect on sizing: frees the collateral of flattened CSPs into cash for the days around each event, slightly lowering average short-vol exposure. No defined-max-loss of its own; it shrinks the max-loss of the sleeves it overlays.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: On the live sim book, log every avoided/flattened event and counterfactually mark what the bracketed short WOULD have lost (using the realized gap). Track the running sum of 'gap losses avoided minus close-costs paid'. ADAPT: if over a rolling window the overlay's net contribution is positive (avoided losses > extra costs — the expected case given fat earnings tails), keep it always-on; if it ever turns net-negative (earnings gaps stopped being fat / IV stopped pre-ramping, i.e. the crowding regime), relax the flatten window from 2 sessions toward 0 so the book keeps harvesting the now-benign event premium. The overlay's own P&L-vs-counterfactual is the metric.
spec.realTimeViability: VERY HIGH. Pure filter on data already fetched: earnings date (quoteSummary calendarEvents) and the open short structures the book already holds. No new chain analytics, no new fills beyond an occasional buy-to-close that the book already knows how to do (rollroute exists). The only real-time risk is an UNSCHEDULED or date-revised earnings announcement — mitigated by the 1-2 session buffer and by re-reading the calendar daily. It is the most operationally robust sleeve in the family because it removes risk rather than adding a position.
spec.falsifier: Falsified as a value-add if, over a rolling 12-month window of live events, the cumulative (avoided gap losses - extra close costs) is <= 0 — i.e. earnings gaps no longer punish short premium enough to justify flattening, meaning the IV pre-ramp now fully compensates the event. In that world the overlay is benched (window -> 0) and the book simply harvests through earnings. Also falsified if buy-to-close slippage at the pre-announcement IV peak systematically exceeds the avoided gap (the close is too expensive), which the counterfactual log measures directly.
spec._family: Earnings IV-crush / pre-earnings vol

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the signal be computed AND the structure actually FILLED daily on the free live options chain?
redteam.kill: The signal input is computable on free EOD data — yahoo-finance.ts (lines 332-360) already fetches quoteSummary calendarEvents and derives daysToEarnings/earningsWarning, exactly as the spec claims. But the overlay cannot be reliably ACTED ON daily on free data with realistic fills, for four reasons. (1) Stale/estimated trigger: Yahoo calendarEvents.earningsDate is frequently an UNCONFIRMED estimate or a [start,end] range; the code blindly takes eDates[0] (line 354) and cannot distinguish confirmed from estimated. Estimates drift by days and confirmations/revisions land intraday or after-hours, so the EOD daily read can miss them — and the spec's '1-2 session buffer' is not a mitigation but the precise failure mode (an estimate off by >2 sessions defeats it). (2) Worst-moment fill: the required buy-to-close executes at the pre-announcement IV peak, the single worst spread/liquidity moment, on OTM 0.16-delta strikes with thin OI; real spreads there run 10-30%+ of premium, far above the modeled costFracOfPremium=0.06 (options-sim.ts:83), so the charged cost is systematically understated for the exact moment the overlay always trades into. (3) No live book to overlay: the actually-automated daily loop (daily/engine.ts:runDay) is a long-only EQUITY book that 'never shorts' (engine.ts:179-180); the CSP/condor short-premium sleeves exist only in the BS_SYNTHETIC backtest (options-sim.ts) and a manual simulated_trades sandbox, so the overlay has no live short-premium positions to flatten in the automated system. (4) Look-ahead-flavored backtest: the earnings proxy is defined as 'the max-abs-gap day per earnings month' from real OHLCV — i.e. it labels the realized worst-gap day as the earnings day, then credits the overlay with avoiding it by construction, inflating the MaxDD/worst-day reduction beyond anything achievable live where only the (estimated) calendar date is known ex ante. Under default-to-skepticism this does not clear 'fillable daily on free data with realistic fills.'

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The overlay's marketed expectedAfterCostSharpe=0.25 is asserted with no derivation, and the host sleeve it modifies is already net-negative at realistic costs, so there is no positive Sharpe to defend. Running the repo's own red-team harness (scripts/backtest/_redteam_sharpe.ts) on cached 2015-2026 data: the baseline CSP sleeve prints Sharpe=-0.38 / netBetaSharpe=-2.34 / CAGR=-2.54% at the spec's OWN 6%-of-premium cost, and -0.74 to -1.23 Sharpe at a realistic 12-20% cost. The 6% cost assumption (costFracOfPremium in options-sim.ts/costs.ts) understates real single-name OTM option cost by ~7-14x: the harness's sanity print shows a 16d 45-DTE put collects only ~$0.24-0.71/sh, so a $0.10 half-spread/side is 41% of premium and $0.20 is 83%. The overlay's signature action is a buy-to-close at the PRE-ANNOUNCEMENT IV PEAK -- the widest-spread, highest-vol moment -- yet it is charged only 6%/side, the most under-costed trade in the whole book. Structurally it pays a certain peak-IV close cost on EVERY earnings-bracketed cycle to dodge a tail that hits a minority: a 16d put is ~1sigma OTM, and a probe shows even an 8% down-gap on a 25%-IV name leaves the short still OTM (net +$0.71/sh); you need ~12%+ to lose meaningfully. So (avoided-gap - realistic close-cost) is not robustly positive -- exactly the failure the spec's own falsifier concedes. Finally the proof cannot be produced: options-sim.ts has ZERO earnings logic (grep confirms no earnings/gap/calendarEvents in the backtest layer), and the proposed proxy -- defining the earnings day as the max-abs-gap day -- is circular look-ahead that mechanically guarantees an avoided move in-sample, over only ~4 quarters of history. Removing a rare left-tail barely changes the 6.27% annualized vol (theta-mark dominated), so the Sharpe delta is tiny while the cost is continuous; net contribution is near zero or negative, and the combined after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe stays negative.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this sleeve carry a short-gamma left tail that co-moves with the other short-vol sleeves (CSP/condor/strangle/term-carry/dispersion) in a Feb-2018 / Mar-2020 crash, thereby concentrating rather than diversifying crash risk?
redteam.kill: The strongest case that it shares the tail: the overlay's one P&L drag is buying-to-close at the pre-announcement IV peak, and if a confirmed earnings date lands during an elevated-IV crash regime the book pays inflated premiums to close exactly when the other short-vol sleeves are bleeding -- a small same-direction cost. But this fails to be a shared tail on both structure and data. STRUCTURE: the overlay sells no option and opens no position; it only BLOCKS or BUY-TO-CLOSES an existing short. Removing/blocking a short can only reduce short-gamma notional, so by construction it cannot create a new convex left tail that co-moves positively with the other sleeves' losses -- its crash-window contribution sign is >=0 (loss-reducing), never additive. DATA (11y, 30 liquid names, scripts/backtest/_rt_earnings_overlay.ts): proxied earnings resolve to 4.0 events/name/yr (clean quarterly). Mar-2020 COVID window (Feb19-Mar31) contains ZERO earnings events -- it sits in the earnings desert, so the overlay does literally nothing in the worst short-vol crash. Feb-2018 contains only 9 events across all 30 names (0.7% of all overlay actions over 11y), and those are genuine idiosyncratic earnings (4.25x larger gap than ambient crash-day moves, so not crash-beta misattribution). Only 0.5% of the overlay's lifetime value falls inside the two crash windows; the close-cost drag in Feb-2018 was 0.81% summed across 9 names versus 11% of avoided down-gap -- a rounding error against the host sleeves' crash drawdown. The earnings tail it truncates is orthogonal to (near-zero beta with) the systematic crash tail, consistent with the spec's corrToEquityBook=-0.05 and sharesShortVolTail=false.

redteam.lens: Backtest honesty & crowding
redteam.kill: The BS-synthetic backtest cannot honestly demonstrate this overlay's value, and the proposed test smuggles in its own edge. Verified in options-sim.ts: simulateUnderlying reads ONLY closes (line 108) and marks positions EOD with sigmaMark = trailing-20d-RV + VRP (lines 141-142) — it never consumes open/high/low. An earnings gap therefore enters the sim only as one close-to-close return that bumps trailing RV by ~gap/sqrt(20)*sqrt(252); the 'loss the baseline eats' is a smooth Black-Scholes re-mark at an elevated vol, NOT the discrete fat-tailed assignment payoff the entire thesis rests on. BS systematically understates the true binary gap (deep-ITM short put -> near-intrinsic), so the deliverable ΔMaxDD/Δworst-day measures an artifact of how the marker smooths a jump, not the tail it claims to truncate. The elevated RV also persists 20 days, contaminating the overlaid arm's re-entry, so the 'difference' is confounded. Worst: the earnings-history reconstruction does not exist in the repo (only the NEXT earnings date is fetched via calendarEvents), and the proposed proxy — earnings day = max-abs-gap day per month — is CIRCULAR: defining the event as the biggest gap and then measuring loss avoided on that day guarantees by construction that the overlay 'avoids the biggest gap.' That is a definitional/look-ahead edge baked into the backtest. The crowding/decay dimension (event IV pre-ramp now fully compensating the gap — the single-name analogue of SVXY-2018/0DTE) plus the expectedAfterCostSharpe 0.25 and corr -0.05 can only be ASSERTED; there is no historical IV surface, IV-ramp data, or capacity model in the project to test them.

scored.name: SPX Put-Ratio Backspread funded by a near-dated put-credit-spread (PRBS-VRP)
scored.family: Convex tail hedge FUNDED by the VRP

scores.name: SPX Put-Ratio Backspread funded by a near-dated put-credit-spread (PRBS-VRP)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Build the structure in production: add put_ratio_backspread and put_credit_spread to OptionStructure (options-sim.ts:53 currently only csp/covered_call/iron_condor/short_strangle) — none of the load-bearing legs exist; the +0.25 was never produced by this code. 2) Add a real per-strike SKEW surface to structureValue/sigmaMark (currently flat single sigma at L100/L142) sourced from an EXTERNAL dated series (features.ts iv_skew_25d), NOT a hand-tuned slope. The funding/self-funding thesis lives entirely in throwaway scripts (_rt_prbs_vrp2.ts) where slope=0.35 is calibrated until the backspread opens for ~zero credit — circular; under flat BS the 1x2 opens for a DEBIT. 3) Charge per-leg proportional costs by delta (12-25%/side for 5-15d wings, not flat 6%) on every leg of every weekly roll; re-derive net. 4) Wire funding+convex into the SAME book and report capmStats.netOfBetaSharpe + Calmar (metrics.ts:150 exists) — the funding leg's positive return is disguised short-put equity beta. 5) Drop the intraday 'monetize on VIX>40/-5% spike' rule (impossible on EOD loop); replace with EOD re-strike accepting the haircut. 6) Mark long wings symmetrically (remove the max(entryIV,...) floor at L142) so calm IV-crush bites — current floor produces implausible positive calm carry, contradicting the spec's own negative-carry admission. 7) Walk-forward MUST weight Feb-2018/Mar-2020/Aug-2024 at full weight AND include compressed-skew 2023-2025 to prove funding still covers carry in the live regime.
scores.oneLineRationale: Coherent funded-convex-hedge thesis whose convex leg is genuinely long the crash tail (net-negative beta in fast gaps), but the structure and the skew that funds it do not exist in the engine — the 0.25 Sharpe is asserted via a tuned calibration constant, not backtested; honest net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe reconciles to ~0.

spec.name: SPX Put-Ratio Backspread funded by a near-dated put-credit-spread (PRBS-VRP)
spec.family: Convex tail hedge FUNDED by the VRP
spec.mechanism: Two structurally-linked legs on the SAME index (SPY/SPX-proxy). FUNDING leg = systematic short-dated (7-14 DTE) 16-delta/5-delta put-credit-spread harvesting the variance risk premium (IV>subsequent RV on average) and the steep, crowded near-the-money put skew that dealers overpay for. CONVEX leg = a 45-60 DTE put RATIO BACKSPREAD (sell 1 ~30-delta put, buy 2 ~12-15-delta puts) financed by that skew: you SELL the rich belly and BUY two cheap, deep-OTM wings, so the structure is opened for ~zero-to-small net credit yet is LONG 1 extra deep put -> long gamma/vega that explodes in a fast crash. Economic basis: VRP + put-skew convexity (behavioral over-pay for near-strike protection vs under-pay for the deep tail) funds positive crash convexity. In calm markets the funding leg's theta covers the backspread's small theta; in a Volmageddon/Mar-2020-style gap the extra long deep put goes deep ITM and pays multiples, offsetting the book's short-gamma sleeves (CSP/condor/strangle) which lose simultaneously.
spec.instruments: Per unit: FUNDING = sell 7-14 DTE 16-delta SPY put / buy 5-delta SPY put (defined-risk put credit spread, ~$5-10 wide), rolled weekly. CONVEX = sell 1x ~30-delta 45-60 DTE SPY put, buy 2x ~12-15-delta 45-60 DTE SPY puts (1x2 put ratio backspread), opened for ~0 to small net credit; rolled at 21 DTE. Net book carries ONE extra long deep-OTM put = the convexity.
spec.exactSignal: Sizing/entry driven by named, free, daily data. (1) Hedge notional target = current net short-gamma of the live short-vol sleeves (CSP+condor+strangle gross short-put delta x100, read off the sim book) so payoff offsets the crash loss. (2) Funding aggressiveness scales with VRP proxy = max(0, VIX/100 - trailing 20d realized vol of SPY) and with skew steepness = (25-delta put IV - ATM IV)/ATM IV from the live chain; richer skew/VRP => widen/roll funding more aggressively, lean skew => shrink funding (avoid paying up). (3) Convex leg always-on while any short-vol sleeve is live; long-put strikes set by live-chain delta (12-15 delta) via the BS IV solver. Threshold: only open funding credit spread when VRP proxy>0 AND VIX9D/VIX term ratio<1.0 (contango); when ratio>1.0 (backwardation=stress) STOP selling funding puts and let the convex leg run.
spec.entryRule: Daily: keep one 7-14 DTE put-credit-spread open (roll when <5 DTE or 50% max profit) sized by the VRP/skew signal; keep one 45-60 DTE put ratio backspread open per unit of book short-gamma, rolled at 21 DTE. Enter convex leg whenever short-vol sleeves are net short puts; scale up the long-put ratio if book short-gamma rises or term structure starts inverting.
spec.exitRule: Funding spread: close at 50% of max profit, at 5 DTE, or immediately if VIX term structure inverts (backwardation) — then it has done its job and you stop feeding the short tail. Convex backspread: roll at 21 DTE to keep gamma fresh; in a crash, monetize the deep long puts on a VIX>40 or SPY -5% day spike (take 30-50% of the convexity gain, re-strike to keep protection). Stop on funding leg = defined by the 5-delta long wing (max loss = spread width - credit).
spec.backtestMethod: Use the existing options-sim.ts: BS-price every leg from the real SPY/SPX-proxy path with IV = trailing 20d realized vol + VRP spread (vrpVolPoints=0.035 base), and ADD a skew term so OTM put IV = ATM IV x (1 + skewSlope*(K_moneyness)) calibrated so 25-delta put IV sits ~3-5 vol pts over ATM (matches observed SPX skew). Mark daily with sigma_mark=max(entryIV, recentRV+VRP) so a vol spike raises BOTH the short funding put (loss) AND the two long deep puts (larger gain) — net convexity shows up honestly. P&L = funding credit captured minus close cost, PLUS backspread mark-to-market (negative small theta in calm, large positive on gap-downs). Validate over 2018-02 (Volmageddon), 2020-03, 2022 grind, 2024-08 yen-carry spike. Honest caveat: BS can UNDERSTATE the deep-put payoff in a real jump (skew/vol-of-vol explode beyond RV+VRP), so the synthetic Sharpe is a conservative LOWER bound on crash payoff.
spec.positionSizing: Capital base = the short-vol sleeves' collateral. Convex+funding combined sized to ~3-6% of total book NAV in defined max loss (funding spread width net of credit, x number of spreads). Convex leg targets a crash payoff of ~1.0-1.5x the modeled short-vol crash loss at SPY -20% over 1 month. Gross options notional may exceed book (long puts are cheap) but DEFINED max loss = funding-spread max loss + backspread net debit (near zero) is capped at the 3-6% budget. No naked short legs (every short put has a long wing or is the inner of a backspread).
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Tracked on the live sim book: (a) net book gamma & vega (sum of all sleeves' BS Greeks from black-scholes.ts) — the convex leg's long-vega must keep total book vega >= a floor (book never net-short-vega by more than X). (b) Hedge efficiency = realized convex-leg P&L on down days / short-vol-sleeve P&L on those same days (target offset ratio 0.8-1.2 in the worst-5% days). (c) Carry drag = trailing 20d net theta of (funding+convex). Allocation rule: if 60d carry drag exceeds the funding budget (hedge is bleeding) -> lean the long ratio toward 1x1.5 and widen funding; if offset ratio<0.8 on recent down days -> add a long put (raise to 1x2.5); if VRP proxy and skew both rich -> let funding fully cover and even contribute carry. Auto-benches the convex leg only if short-vol sleeves are all flat.
spec.realTimeViability: High. Signal is computable daily from free data already in the app: live SPY/SPX option chain (delta, IV, OI via yahoo-finance2 /api/options), VIX & VIX9D term ratio (already in regime engine), realized vol from OHLCV, and the live sim book's own Greeks. Fills: SPY/SPX options are the most liquid options on earth — 7-14 DTE 16-delta put spreads and 45-60 DTE ratio backspreads fill on tight markets daily. Assignment/early-exercise: funding short put is European-style on SPX (cash-settled, no assignment) or, if SPY is used, the long 5-delta wing caps loss and 21-DTE management avoids gamma-week pin risk. Main real-world frictions, stated honestly: (1) the convex leg has NEGATIVE carry in long calm stretches even after funding (Sharpe is LOW by design — it is insurance that pays for itself ~most months, not an alpha engine); (2) in a slow grind-down (2022) the deep long puts may not go ITM enough to offset — backspread helps less than in a fast gap; (3) skew can compress (2024-25) making funding thinner. These are why net Sharpe is modest, not high.
spec.falsifier: Over a walk-forward including >=2 crash episodes, the sleeve is falsified if EITHER (a) on the worst-5% SPY days the convex leg fails to offset >=60% of the short-vol sleeves' loss (it is not actually convex/co-moving), OR (b) the all-in net carry drag over calm periods exceeds 1.5% of book NAV per year after funding (the VRP/skew does NOT pay for the convexity, so it is just expensive insurance), OR (c) adding the sleeve does not raise the COMBINED book's Sharpe/Calmar (its negative correlation in crashes fails to improve risk-adjusted return). Any of these kills the 'funded convex hedge' thesis.
spec._family: Convex tail hedge FUNDED by the VRP

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the signal be computed AND the multi-leg structure actually FILLED/monetized daily on the free live options chain with realistic fills?
redteam.kill: The SIGNAL is genuinely computable daily from free data — verified in code: VIX9D/VIX term ratio is wired live (src/lib/backtest/engine.ts:104; src/app/api/forecast/route.ts) and historically (cache back to 2015), realized vol comes from OHLCV, and the options route (src/app/api/options/route.ts -> getOptionsChain) returns live SPY chain WITH bid/ask/openInterest/volume/IV per contract. BUT the STRUCTURE cannot be filled/monetized daily on free data, and that is what this lens kills. (1) The entire P&L thesis is the RELATIVE price of a ~30-delta belly vs deep-OTM 12-15 delta wings in a 1x2 ratio backspread, opened for a precise ~zero net credit across three strikes at two deltas, on TWO simultaneous expiries (7-14 DTE funding + 45-60 DTE convex). Yahoo's free chain is 15-min-delayed, EOD-quality: bid/ask on the deep-OTM SPY puts the convexity depends on are routinely 0.00 or 30-100%+ of mid, with thin/zero OI/volume at exactly those strikes, and IVs that are Yahoo's own crude solve — you cannot know an executable net-credit mid for a three-leg ratio from a delayed snapshot, so 'fills on tight markets daily' is unsubstantiated for the load-bearing legs. (2) The backtest engine cannot even MODEL the edge: src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts supports only csp/covered_call/iron_condor/short_strangle — NO ratio backspread, NO put-credit-spread, and NO SKEW (every leg priced at one sigmaMark=max(sigmaEntry, rvNow+vrp) at line 142-143, identical across strikes). The spec admits the skew term must be ADDED, meaning the entire 'sell rich belly skew to fund cheap deep wings' economic basis is currently invisible and unvalidated. (3) Monetization is intraday-impossible on an EOD loop: the exit rule says monetize the deep puts 'on a VIX>40 or SPY -5% day spike' and re-strike — but an EOD-only system cannot act until next close, by which point the convex spike it is supposed to capture has mean-reverted (the exact short-gamma-week / fast-gap regime where deep-OTM put quotes blow out and a free delayed feed is least trustworthy). Egress to Yahoo is also blocked and node_modules absent in this checkout, so I could NOT empirically confirm fillable deep-OTM quotes; under the lens's skeptical default (uncertain => survives=false) plus the verified gaps, the structure is not tradeable daily on free data at the prices the thesis assumes.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The claimed expectedAfterCostSharpe of 0.25 is fictional under this lens on three independent grounds, each fatal. (1) STRUCTURE NOT BUILT: the PRBS put-ratio-backspread + funding put-credit-spread does not exist in code. src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts OptionStructure enum is only "csp"|"covered_call"|"iron_condor"|"short_strangle"; grep for backspread/ratio/skew/funding-leg/convex-leg/put-credit-spread returns ZERO hits in any sim file, and the only runnable backtest (scripts/backtest/options.ts) tests csp/condor/strangle. So 0.25 was never produced by this code for this strategy. (2) THE FUNDING SOURCE IS UNIMPLEMENTED AND IMPOSSIBLE UNDER THE MODEL: the whole thesis is that steep put skew makes the belly rich and the wings cheap, letting the backspread open for ~zero credit. But structureValue()/openStructure() price every leg at a single flat sigma (sigmaMark/ivEntry, options-sim.ts L97-104, L163-213) — there is NO skew surface. The backtestMethod's promised skewSlope term is not in black-scholes.ts or options-sim.ts. Under flat BS vol a 1x2 backspread opens for a net DEBIT, not a credit, so the 'self-funding' claim is false in the very engine cited to validate it. (3) COST REALITY KILLS THE CARRY: re-deriving with realistic 8%/side option spreads (the 5-delta and 12-15-delta strikes this lives on are penny-wide on $0.20-0.40 mids = 12-25%/side, not 'tightest on earth'), the funding leg round-trip eats ~27% of net credit and net edge goes NEGATIVE (~ -$0.02/cycle); rolled weekly that is a large drag, and the convex leg is pure cost rolled ~17x/yr on 3 legs. Honest net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe re-derives to ~ -0.1 to +0.1, because the funding leg's apparent positive return is disguised short-put equity beta (long-market in calm) that vanishes when beta is stripped via capmStats netOfBetaSharpe. The 0.25 figure is pre-realistic-multileg-cost, pre-skew-reality, and not net of beta — exactly the three errors this lens targets.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does PRBS-VRP carry a short-gamma left tail that co-moves with the book's other short-vol sleeves (CSP/condor/strangle) in Feb-2018/Mar-2020, concentrating crash risk rather than diversifying?
redteam.kill: Half-true, not fatal. The FUNDING leg (7-14 DTE 16d/5d put-credit-spread) IS short the tail and co-moves 1:1 with the other short-vol sleeves (same structure). On the real SPY path the weekly-rolled funding leg summed -96% of one cycle max-loss in Feb-2018 and -195% in Mar-2020 (_rt_prbs_vrp.ts sec D); peer index condor/CSP lose -83.5%/-28.6% in Mar-2020. BUT the CONVEX leg (1x2 put backspread) is genuinely LONG the tail: the extra deep put goes ITM and pays multiples in a gap (+12x wing premium Feb-2018, +31x Mar-2020 at trough; _rt_prbs_vrp2.ts sec 1). Net, the deep wings dominate: at 4% NAV the unit offsets ~170% of a 60%-NAV short-vol book crash loss in both episodes (NET +7.6% Feb-2018 / +24.0% Mar-2020 of NAV; _rt_prbs_vrp2.ts sec 4). So the sleeve is net-long crash convexity, co-moving NEGATIVELY with the rest of the short-vol book in a fast gap. sharesShortVolTail=false survives because the convex leg over-compensates the funding leg's inherited short-gamma in exactly the fast-crash episodes the lens names.

redteam.lens: Backtest honesty & crowding — is IV=RV+VRP (plus the added skew term) defensible, or does it smuggle in the edge; is the VRP/skew premium crowded/decayed; capacity at retail size; what is testable vs only assertable.
redteam.kill: The funding edge is smuggled into the backtest via a calibration constant, and the existing sim physically cannot test the convexity thesis. (1) The whole economic claim is "sell the rich near-strike belly, buy two skew-cheap deep wings, open for ~zero credit." But options-sim.ts prices every leg at ONE flat sigma (line 100 structureValue; line 143 sigmaMark = rvNow + vrp). There is no skew surface. The spec's own backtestMethod admits the skew term does not exist and must be ADDED and "calibrated so 25-delta put IV sits ~3-5 vol pts over ATM." That calibration constant IS the edge: you tune skewSlope until the 1x2 backspread opens for zero credit, then 'discover' it opens for zero credit. Circular — the sim cannot independently confirm funding works. (2) Marking all strikes at a single sigmaMark means the deep-put 'explosion' in a crash is driven only by the underlying going ITM plus a uniform vega bump, NOT by the skew/vol-of-vol steepening that is the real payoff source; the spec's 'conservative LOWER bound' framing is one-sided (flat-vol marking simultaneously UNDERstates how expensive the wings are to buy/roll, so it is an UPPER bound on the funding edge — the two biases do not both point conservatively). (3) The funding leg (7-14 DTE put-credit-spread harvesting VRP/near-strike skew) is the crowded 2018-Volmageddon / 0DTE short-vol archetype and is structurally the SAME short-gamma as the CSP/condor/strangle sleeves it claims to offset — on a gap it loses to max-loss SIMULTANEOUSLY with those sleeves. Net convexity = backspread gain MINUS funding crash loss, and that netting depends on the exact skew the sim does not model. (4) The named entry/stop signals (VRP proxy = VIX/100 - RV; VIX9D/VIX term-ratio contango gate) are not wired into options-sim.ts at all — VIX never sets entry vol there — so the gating logic is asserted, untested. The convexity, the funding, and the gate can only be ASSERTED, not tested, in the existing tooling.

scored.name: Skew-Slope Directional Tilt (cross-sectional risk-reversal), traded as defined-risk verticals
scored.family: Options-implied DIRECTIONAL signals (skew / risk-reversal, put-call imbalance, IV-term slope) read off the live chain we already fetch, traded as defined-risk equity verticals — directional alpha, NOT short-vol carry

scores.name: Skew-Slope Directional Tilt (cross-sectional risk-reversal), traded as defined-risk verticals
scores.requiredFixes: Three blockers, each independently disqualifying as specified. (1) COST/SHARPE (Fatal, reproduced via _rt_skewvert_cost.ts): a defined-risk vertical is 4 option fills per round trip across single-name OTM bid-ask; round-trip cost is 3.7-14.8% of structure max value per cycle (the spec's own return denominator) at 30% IV, and after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe is NEGATIVE (-0.13 to -0.74) across the entire scenario grid because the ~0.22-delta vertical captures only ~3.9% return-on-risk/cycle vs a 5-8% cost/cycle. FIX: trade the underlying delta-1 (equity round-trip ~10bps per costs.ts, 25-50x cheaper; replace the defined-risk cap with a stop) OR go monthly + hold-to-expiry + 20-30pt-wide spreads on only AAPL/MSFT/NVDA-tier names hard-gated at bid-ask<=5%. (2) BACKTEST HONESTY (reproduced independently): no free historical IV surface exists in the repo (data.ts is Yahoo OHLCV only), so the actual 25-delta risk-reversal can never be tested. The realized-skew proxy the spec substitutes fails its own pre-registered bar: my build on 230+ cached names gives pooled rank-IC -0.002 to -0.010 (WRONG SIGN vs the spec's bullish claim) and gross long-short Sharpe -0.12 to -0.21 BEFORE any cost. FIX: source OptionMetrics/ORATS 25d risk-reversals and re-run the real signal; until then the priorSharpe must be ~0, not 0.5. (3) SHARED TAIL (Serious): the offered bull-put CREDIT spread is net short gamma/vega and co-loads the CSP/condor/strangle left tail (cross-name corr -> ~1 in crashes), contradicting sharesShortVolTail:false. FIX: delete the credit-spread variant, force debit-only on both deciles, add a book-level non-negative net-gamma guard, and size the summed max-loss budget at tail-correlation=1.
scores.oneLineRationale: Well-motivated directional/orthogonal idea, but the tradable 4-leg vertical has negative after-cost Sharpe in every scenario, the implied signal is untestable on free data and its only proxy is near-zero/wrong-sign and negative gross, and the credit-spread variant reintroduces the shared short-vol tail — CUT until traded delta-1 on a real IV surface.

spec.name: Skew-Slope Directional Tilt (cross-sectional risk-reversal), traded as defined-risk verticals
spec.mechanism: Behavioral + informed-flow, NOT variance-risk-premium. Xing-Zhang-Zhao (JFQA) and Cremers-Weinbaum: the steepness of a single name's volatility smirk (OTM-put IV minus ATM/OTM-call IV at matched delta) negatively predicts its future return — steep-smirk names underperform flat-smirk names by ~1.9%/mo risk-adjusted, persistent up to ~6 months, robust to size/BM/vol/skewness/volume. Economic source: option market prices jump-to-downside risk and informed traders buy protective/directional puts ahead of bad news, so a steepening relative skew is a leading bearish tell and a flattening / call-rich skew (positive risk-reversal) is bullish. This is a DIRECTIONAL cross-sectional signal — its return stream is structurally different from selling premium, so it does not stack the short-gamma left tail of the CSP/condor/strangle book.
spec.instruments: Trade the UNDERLYING via DEFINED-RISK equity verticals on the optionable universe (~80-150 liquid names already screened). Bullish decile (flattest / most call-rich skew, top risk-reversal): buy a 40-45 DTE ~30-delta bull call DEBIT spread OR sell a 16-delta bull put credit spread (5-10pt wide). Bearish decile (steepest smirk, most negative risk-reversal): buy a 40-45 DTE bear put DEBIT spread. Verticals (not outright shares) cap max loss, are capital-efficient, and keep gross option notional defined; net position is roughly delta-balanced long-vs-short so it is closer to market-neutral than the long-only equity sleeves.
spec.exactSignal: Daily from the live yahoo-finance2 chain we already fetch: for each name pick the 30-45 DTE expiry; using the Black-Scholes engine (analytic delta + IV solver already in black-scholes.ts) interpolate IV at exactly 25-delta put and 25-delta call. SKEW_t = IV(25d put) - IV(25d call) (the 'risk reversal', sign-flipped). Normalize within the universe to a cross-sectional z-score AND to each name's own trailing 252d skew percentile (to strip structural sector skew). Composite signal = -0.6*z(SKEW) - 0.4*pctile(SKEW) so that LOW/negative skew (call-rich) = bullish, HIGH skew (steep smirk) = bearish. Optional confirmation gate: only take a name if the iTransformer 60d forecast concordance agrees in sign (re-uses the existing concordance gate). Long the top quintile, short the bottom quintile.
spec.entryRule: Weekly rebalance (the signal is a 1-6 month predictor — daily churn is pure cost). Rank the universe by the composite skew score; require |z|>=0.8 and 25-delta put AND call both quoted with bid>0, OI>=200, and bid-ask <= 15% of mid (liquidity gate). Open the bullish vertical on top-quintile names and the bearish vertical on bottom-quintile names, equal DEFINED-RISK budget per leg. Skip any name with earnings inside the holding window (earnings repricing dominates the skew signal — re-uses existing earnings gate).
spec.exitRule: Hold to ~21 DTE then close/roll the survivors to the next 40-45 DTE expiry (re-evaluate the skew rank at the roll — if a name has left its quintile, drop it). Take profit at 50% of max value on debit spreads / 50% of credit on credit spreads; hard stop if the underlying gaps through the short strike of a credit spread (defined max loss already caps it). Forced exit on any earnings announcement entering the window.
spec.backtestMethod: Two-part honest backtest. (a) SIGNAL VALIDITY on REAL OHLCV: there is no free historical IV-surface, so proxy each name's daily 25d risk-reversal with a realized-skew proxy = sign-corrected trailing return skewness + a downside/upside realized-semivariance ratio over 60d (these are the physical-measure analogue the smirk prices in; Xing-Zhang-Zhao show the smirk loads on jump risk which IS observable in realized downside semivariance). Rank-sort the universe weekly on this proxy, long-short quintile, hold 21d, conservative equity-vertical cost (model the vertical's debit/credit and ~1-2 vol-pt round-trip slippage via BS from IV=trailing-RV+VRP spread of ~3-4 pts). (b) STRUCTURE P&L: price the actual verticals each day with BS using IV = trailing 20-60d RV + a 3-4 vol-pt VRP term-structure (single-name VRP wider), debit/credit and Greeks from black-scholes.ts, realize P&L on the historical underlying path. Report the long-short proxy Sharpe AND haircut it ~30-40% for the proxy-vs-true-IV basis. State assumption explicitly: IV(K)=RV_60d*(1+VRP) with a linear skew slope calibrated so 25d-put-minus-call ≈ the realized downside-semivariance tilt.
spec.positionSizing: Capital base shared with the ARMS book. Per name, DEFINED max loss = spread width minus net credit (or net debit) per contract * 100 * qty. Size each leg so its max loss = 0.25-0.5% of book; whole sleeve max loss budget ~6-8% of book if every position hits max loss simultaneously (it won't — names are decorrelated post-beta). Long-short construction keeps net delta near zero and net beta small (target |beta|<0.15). Gross option notional can exceed 1.0x book via the verticals but the SUMMED defined max loss is the true risk and is held <= ~8% of book. No naked legs, no assignment surprise beyond the defined-risk spread (manage/close ITM short legs before expiry).
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: On the live sim book track (1) rank-IC: Spearman correlation between yesterday's composite skew score and the realized next-day/next-week underlying return, EWMA over 60 trading days; (2) realized long-short decile spread vs the backtest prior; (3) hit rate of the bullish vs bearish legs separately. Allocation rule (re-uses allocator.ts benching): if the 60d rank-IC EWMA drops below +0.02 (signal-decay / crowding tripwire) the sleeve is benched to a floor weight; if IC stays >0 but spread compresses, the allocator's light-Sharpe-tilt shrinks it toward the equal-risk base. Because the signal is documented to decay ~5-10%/yr, the IC tripwire is the primary defense against crowding.
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH for signal, MEDIUM for fills.
