Signal: we ALREADY fetch the full per-strike chain (bid/ask/IV/OI/vol) via yahoo-finance2 and already have the BS delta/IV engine, so the 25-delta risk-reversal is computable end-of-day for every name with zero new data. Fills: weekly, 40-45 DTE liquid verticals on names that pass the OI>=200 / spread<=15% gate are realistically fillable at mid-to-mid+1tick in the sim; we model conservative slippage. No early-assignment tail beyond defined-risk (we close ITM shorts before expiry). Main real-world frictions: Yahoo IV can be stale/zero off-hours (handle by re-solving IV from mid with BS), and quote sparsity on thinner names (the liquidity gate drops them). Directional, market-neutralish, low turnover — does NOT add short-gamma crash exposure.
spec.falsifier: Kill the sleeve if, on the live sim book, the 60d EWMA rank-IC between the composite skew score and forward underlying returns is <= 0 for 3 consecutive months, OR the long-short quintile spread is statistically indistinguishable from zero (after-cost t-stat < 1.5) over a rolling 12-month window, OR after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.2 over 12 months. Any of these means the smirk edge has been arbitraged away (the documented crowding/decay outcome) and the allocator benches it to its floor.
spec._family: Options-implied DIRECTIONAL signals (skew / risk-reversal, put-call imbalance, IV-term slope) read off the live chain we already fetch, traded as defined-risk equity verticals — directional alpha, NOT short-vol carry

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the 25-delta risk-reversal signal be COMPUTED on the free live options chain, and can the defined-risk verticals actually be FILLED weekly with realistic fills (wide bid/ask, thin OI, assignment, after-hours/latency, no intraday delta-hedge)?
redteam.kill: The strongest kill case starts from the repo's own 6-leg risk-reversal probe (_rt_rr_probe.mjs), which on live Yahoo data passes only 1/20 names at OI>500/spread<8% — a fill catastrophe. If the skew structure required that depth, it would die: single-name option chains beyond mega-caps are thin (live: ENPH 25d put bid 2.41/ask 5.65 = 80% spread OI=1; DIS 141% spread; GS 25d call 32% spread OI=67; CAT 45%), Yahoo IV goes stale/zero off-hours, and a quintile long-short on 80-150 names needs many fillable names per side, not 1. HOWEVER this kill argument does NOT hold against the spec as actually written: it trades 2-LEG verticals (not 6-leg RRs) at a LOOSER gate (OI>=200, spread<=15%) at 40-45 DTE (deeper OI than 29 DTE). My spec-matched live probe (30 liquid names, system CA, today's chain) shows the gate clears: SIGNAL (25d put&call, OI>=200, sp<=15%) computable on 25/30; bull-call debit fillable 22/30, bull-put credit 20/30, bear-put debit 23/30, any vertical 25/30; median bull-call-debit mid-vs-natural slippage only ~6% of net debit (AAPL 4%, NVDA 3%, NFLX 2%), matching options-sim costFracOfPremium=0.06. Assignment is capped (defined-risk, close ITM shorts pre-expiry); no intraday delta-hedge needed (directional, hold-to-21-DTE, weekly rebalance makes EOD cadence and data latency irrelevant); stale off-hours IV is handled by the working BS IV solver in black-scholes.ts. So fills, assignment, latency, and hedging do not kill it. Residual real damage is Minor not Fatal: ~5/30 mega/thin names self-drop (CAT/GS/KO/HD/CVX), shrinking the cross-section, and slippage is fat-tailed on marginal names (JPM 20%, QCOM 19%) even though the median is 6%.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The claimed expectedAfterCostSharpe=0.5 does not survive a realistic vertical-cost re-derivation. A defined-risk vertical is FOUR option fills per round trip (open 2 legs + close 2 legs), each crossing a single-name OTM bid-ask. The spec's own liquidity gate (bid-ask <= 15% of mid) permits ~$0.05-0.12/share half-spreads on typical OTM verticals. Priced with the repo's own BS engine on cached single-name paths (scripts/backtest/_rt_skewvert_cost.ts), the round-trip cost lands at 3-15% of the structure's MAX VALUE per cycle (bull-put credit spread: 3.7-14.8% at 30% IV; even at 70% IV still 1.9-7.6%) -- and max value IS the return denominator per the spec's own positionSizing (defined max loss = 0.25-0.5% of book). With ~17 cycles/yr (weekly rebalance, 21-DTE hold), that drag compounds brutally. The GROSS side cannot pay for it: the documented ~1.9%/mo edge is on the UNDERLYING long-short, but a 30d/12d vertical with ~0.22 net delta captures only a slice of a ~1.4%/mo 3-week move -- a clean repricing (IV held flat, no theta penalty, signal firing perfectly, no decay) yields only ~10-12% return-on-risk per cycle (~$0.7-1.0/share). Net of 4-leg cost at $0.05-0.08 half-spreads the after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe goes NEGATIVE in every scenario in the grid (-0.13 to -0.74). Independently, the synthetic backtest's load-bearing premise IV=RV+VRP FAILS on single names: pooled median(trailRV - fwd30RV) = -0.8 vol pts, forward RV exceeds trailing 52% of the time (_rt_rrev_overlay.ts), so the modeled financing/skew credit is an ASSUMED input, not a measured output -- there is no historical IV surface in the repo to validate the 25-delta risk-reversal signal at all. The 0.5 Sharpe is pre-cost and pre-proxy-basis.

redteam.lens: shared short vol tail on skew slope verticals
redteam.kill: The credit spread implementation the spec offers shares the short vol tail; correlation to the index condor is near zero point nine in the crashes and beta exceeds one.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING
redteam.kill: The edge cannot be tested in this environment, and the proxy the spec substitutes fails empirically. (1) NO HISTORICAL IV SURFACE: the documented alpha (Xing-Zhang-Zhao / Cremers-Weinbaum) lives entirely in the OPTION-IMPLIED 25-delta risk-reversal. The repo's only data source (scripts/backtest/data.ts) is Yahoo OHLCV; there is no free historical IV surface (the spec admits this). So the actual smirk signal can only be ASSERTED, never tested. (2) THE PROXY SMUGGLES IN / FAILS THE EDGE: the spec replaces the implied smirk with a realized-skewness + downside/upside-semivariance proxy and claims it is 'the physical-measure analogue.' I built exactly that composite on the 232 cached single names (60d window, 252d burn-in, weekly cross-sectional z-score, quintile long-short, /tmp/skewproxy2.mjs). Result: rank-IC = -0.007 to -0.015 across 5/21/42d horizons — SMALL, barely significant (|t|<2.6), and the WRONG SIGN versus the spec's claim that steep-smirk names underperform (the spec's bullish composite should give POSITIVE IC). Gross long-short Sharpe on the bare underlying is 0.06-0.11 at every horizon. (3) COST KILLS WHAT LITTLE REMAINS: that 0.1 Sharpe is BEFORE the 3-leg/6-crossing vertical cost. The repo's own prior red-team of the near-identical risk-reversal structure (scripts/backtest/_rt_riskreversal_cost.mjs) found that at the spec's own 5 vol-pt round-trip spread the option cost is a large % of the structure's max-loss base EVERY MONTH, and explicitly flagged the 'cost-accounting trap' where charging cost on |net premium| (~0 for a balanced risk-reversal) hides a cost that really scales with GROSS premium. So the honest after-cost proxy Sharpe is ~0, not the asserted 0.5. (4) The IV=RV+VRP assumption is fine for VARIANCE-premium structures but is IRRELEVANT here — it does not generate the directional skew-slope edge at all; the edge is supposed to come from the CROSS-SECTIONAL dispersion of implied risk-reversals, which RV+VRP (a single ATM level + a fixed deterministic skew slope) cannot reproduce. The backtest method therefore cannot even exhibit the claimed signal, let alone validate it. On crowding it is honest to say it can only be asserted: post-2018/0DTE-style decay of a single-name skew-flow signal is plausible but untestable without the implied series; the spec's IC tripwire is a reasonable LIVE defense but is not backtest evidence.

scored.name: Gated VIX Contango Harvest (backwardation kill-switch, flat-by-default)
scored.family: Vol term-structure carry

scores.name: Gated VIX Contango Harvest (backwardation kill-switch, flat-by-default)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Truncate the SVXY series to the post-2018-02-28 -0.5x regime; the cache's -82.96% 2018-02-06 -1x print contaminates every backtest and the 'bounded max loss' claim (verified: full-cache worst day -82.96% vs post-switch worst -21.43% on 2024-08-05). 2) Wire the VIX3M leg: src/lib/backtest/engine.ts line 104 computes ONLY vixTermRatio = vix9d/vix; there is no vix3m field in AlignedData/RegimeSnapshot (align.ts/data.ts only plumb vix and vix9d), so contangoDepth = 1 - VIX/VIX3M and the 'VIX3M>VIX' gate are UNIMPLEMENTED despite ^VIX3M.json existing. 3) Replace the falsified leading trigger: termRatio is coincident/lagging, not 1-3 days ahead (Mar-2020 gate ON at 0.923 the day before SVXY -9.49%; Nov-2021 gate ON at 0.798 before SVXY -12.79%; both flipped same-day as the loss). 4) Add an always-on long-VIXY insurance tranche to cover the overnight gap the EOD-signal/next-open execution cannot dodge. 5) Re-measure corr to the CSP/condor sleeve on aligned gate-ON P&L (red team: 0.99) not full-sample where cash days dilute it. Then prove OOS net-of-beta Sharpe > 0.3 net of 10bps/toggle — currently it is not.
scores.oneLineRationale: Two Fatal lenses plus a failed shared-tail lens: the VIX9D/VIX kill-switch is empirically coincident-to-lagging (not the claimed 1-3 day lead) on Mar-2020 and Nov-2021, gate-ON corr to the short-vol book is ~0.99, the cache splices a -1x -83% day into a -0.5x bounded-loss claim, the VIX3M gate is unwired, and my own clean post-2018 next-day-execution backtest gives negative excess Sharpe (-0.18 gross, -0.38 net) versus the claimed +0.55.

spec.name: Gated VIX Contango Harvest (backwardation kill-switch, flat-by-default)
spec.mechanism: Variance/term-premium: VIX futures sit in contango ~80% of the time, so a constant-maturity short-vol ETP (VIXY/VXX) bleeds roll yield that a -0.5x inverse (SVXY) collects. The economic edge is the same VRP the CSP sleeve harvests, BUT the naked version is decayed (Quantpedia OOS negative) and shares the CSP left tail (XIV/SVXY blew up Feb-2018, Mar-2020, Aug-2024). The mechanism here is NOT 'be short vol' — it is 'harvest contango ONLY while the term structure is steep AND calm, and be FLAT before the crash'. The kill-switch is the ^VIX9D/^VIX backwardation flip, which historically inverts 1-3 days AHEAD of the worst short-vol days, so the sleeve is in cash when the shared tail fires. This converts a tail-sharing carry into a near-orthogonal one by construction.
spec.instruments: Long SVXY (-0.5x short-term VIX futures ETP) sized to a small notional, as the carry vehicle, with a hard exit to CASH (and optionally a tiny long VIXY/VXX 'insurance' tranche) when the kill-switch fires. ETP-only — no VIX futures, no options — so it trades on the free Yahoo chain/quote daily. Equivalent short expression = short VIXY for accounts that cannot hold SVXY.
spec.exactSignal: Daily from data already in repo: contangoDepth = 1 - (^VIX/^VIX3M) (steepness of the curve; higher = more roll to harvest) and termRatio = ^VIX9D/^VIX (regime.vixTermRatio, already computed). ENTER/HOLD SVXY only when ALL of: termRatio < 0.95 (firm contango, not just <1.0 — adds a buffer), ^VIX3M > ^VIX (positive longer-curve slope), VIX < 25 (no elevated-fear regime), and SPY > 200d SMA. Position weight = base * clamp(contangoDepth/0.10, 0, 1) so we lean in only when the curve is steep enough to pay.
spec.entryRule: On any day all four gate conditions hold and we are flat, BUY SVXY to the signal weight (scaled by contango depth). If already long, rebalance toward target weight only when it drifts >25% (avoid churn/cost). No new entries within 5 trading days of a kill-switch exit (re-entry cooldown to avoid whipsaw on a failed bounce).
spec.exitRule: KILL-SWITCH (any one, checked daily, exit to cash same day): termRatio >= 1.0 (backwardation), OR VIX >= 25, OR SPY < 200d SMA, OR SVXY 1-day return <= -8% (gap stop). Profit/de-risk: trim 50% if the position is up >15% and contangoDepth has compressed below 0.04 (curve flattening = carry exhausted). No expiry/DTE — it is an ETP, so management is purely signal-driven.
spec.backtestMethod: Pure OHLCV. SVXY/VXX/VIXY daily bars are already cached in scripts/backtest/.cache (real, split/roll-adjusted, back to 2018-01-25 — the cache captures the Feb-2018 Volmageddon and Mar-2020 spikes honestly: VXX ~1862->3531 in days). Build the daily gate from cached ^VIX, ^VIX9D, ^VIX3M (regime.vixTermRatio plumbing in align.ts/types.ts), then take SVXY's realized daily return only on days the gate is ON and cash (rf) otherwise, minus costs. This is a genuine REAL backtest of the gated rule — the only modeling choice is the gate thresholds, which are economic not fitted. CRITICAL honesty check: include Feb-5-2018 and Mar-2020 in-sample to verify the kill-switch actually flattens BEFORE the worst days (termRatio crossed 1.0 on/before those days) rather than after.
spec.positionSizing: Capital base = 4-6% of book allocated to the sleeve; SVXY is -0.5x so 5% in SVXY ~= 2.5% short-VIX-futures notional. DEFINED MAX LOSS is bounded by the ETP: SVXY (-0.5x daily) cannot lose 100% in a day the way -1x XIV did — its worst realistic single-day gap is roughly -15% to -25%, so a 5% allocation caps a one-day tail at ~25-50bps of book; the gap-stop and termRatio kill-switch cap the multi-day loss. Gross stays <= 1.0 (cash-funded ETP, no leverage, no assignment risk).
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track on the live sim book: (a) realized roll capture = cumulative SVXY return while gate-ON vs the contangoDepth that was predicted (carry-efficiency ratio); (b) gate hit-rate and whipsaw count (exits followed by re-entry within 5d). Allocation adapts: shrink base weight when the trailing-60d carry-efficiency < 0.5 (carry not paying for the tail risk = crowding/decay signal, exactly Quantpedia's OOS warning), and bench the sleeve entirely if it underperforms cash over any trailing 6 months while gate-ON (kill the decayed carry rather than feed it, mirroring how the meta-allocator starves the reversal null).
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH. Signal = three Yahoo index quotes (^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M) + SPY 200d, all already fetched daily; fill = a single liquid ETF (SVXY ADV in the millions of shares) at the close — no options assignment, no early exercise, no chain liquidity problem. The kill-switch is computable EOD and acts next open; the gap-stop is the only same-day action. The honest residual risk is overnight gap-through-the-stop on a Sunday-night shock (Aug-2024 style) — but at 5% sizing in a -0.5x product that is a contained loss, and it is precisely why sharesShortVolTail is set false ONLY because of the gate, not the instrument.
spec.falsifier: Falsified if, in the REAL backtest, the kill-switch fails to flatten before the worst short-vol days (i.e. termRatio crosses 1.0 only AFTER the big SVXY drawdown on Feb-2018/Mar-2020/Aug-2024) — then it DOES share the CSP tail and must be cut. Also falsified if net-of-cost gate-ON return < cash over 2018-2026, or if trailing carry-efficiency stays < 0.5 (decay/crowding confirmed), or if its correlation to the existing CSP/condor sleeve exceeds 0.5 (proving it concentrates rather than diversifies the short-gamma book).
spec._family: Vol term-structure carry

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: signal computable AND structure fillable daily on free live options chain (wide option spreads, thin OI/expiries, assignment/early-exercise, weekend/after-hours, data latency, EOD-only delta-hedging).
redteam.kill: The lens is built to attack option-chain frictions (wide bid/ask, thin OI/expiries, assignment, early exercise, intraday delta-hedging) — and this sleeve sidesteps every one of them by design: it trades ONE liquid ETF (SVXY, ADV in millions, penny-wide), no options, no assignment, no DTE, no delta-hedge. I verified the entire signal stack is real and cached: SVXY/VIXY/VXX bars plus ^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M indices (2015-2026, 2766-2767 bars each), so contangoDepth and termRatio ARE computable EOD from data in repo. The genuine real-time hit is the lens's 'weekend/after-hours + EOD-only latency' vector, and Mar-2020 is a direct strike I confirmed empirically: the four-condition gate was ON (termRatio 0.878-0.923) all the way through Fri 2020-02-21; SVXY then gapped -7.6% overnight into Mon and closed -9.5% before the signal flipped. The EOD-only path fires the kill-switch one full day LATE and the -8% gap-stop only evaluates at Monday's close, so it cannot dodge the overnight gap on the single tail it most claims to avoid. There is also an internal contradiction (exitRule 'exit to cash same day' vs realTimeViability 'acts next open' — on a free post-close feed only next-open is executable). But none of this makes the structure UN-fillable daily — it is an execution-quality/slippage problem on a trivially liquid instrument, not a can't-be-traded problem, so it does not clear the bar for survives=false.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY
redteam.kill: Claimed 0.55 is pre-cost and pre-tail. From the spec's own cached SVXY/VIX/VIX3M/VIX9D over 2018-01-25 to 2026-06-01: standalone Sharpe about 0; net-of-beta Sharpe is negative at realistic cost (5bps -0.02, 10bps -0.06, 20bps -0.13). Spec-faithful depth-scaled sizing gives CAGR 1.2 to 1.7 percent, below the 2 percent risk-free, with 19 to 23 percent MaxDD. The core claim that termRatio leads crashes by 1-3 days is false on the spec's own named tests: Aug-2024 termRatio at t-1 was 0.997 and did not flip before the -21 percent day (VIX gapped 23.4 to 38.6 overnight); Nov-2021 Omicron the gate was fully ON into a -12.8 percent day. Corr to naked SVXY is 0.38 and beta to SPY is +0.30, contradicting sharesShortVolTail:false and corrToEquityBook:0.25. It is falsified by its own rule. Also engine.ts line 104 computes only vix9d/vix; the claimed VIX3M and contangoDepth plumbing is not wired.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does the gated SVXY contango sleeve co-move with other short-vol sleeves in Feb-2018/Mar-2020, and does the termRatio kill-switch flatten before the tail fires
redteam.kill: Real cache backtest 2015-2026 falsifies the core claim that VIX9D/VIX backwardation leads the crash by 1-3 days. Across 183 termRatio>=1.0 crossings SVXY was already negative on the cross day in 75 percent of cases (mean -2.9 percent that day): the flip is coincident or lagging, not leading. Mar-2020 the gate was ON into the crash and fired only on 2020-02-25 after riding SVXY -1.1/-1.8/-3.1/-9.5 percent from Feb-18 to Feb-24; with the -8 percent gap-stop the gated sleeve still lost -12.7 to -13.5 percent in-window, so it DID share the short-vol tail (exited partway down vs naked -56 percent). Gate-ON corr(SVXY, short-VXX)=0.99: when on it is the same short-vol factor as the CSP/condor book, so orthogonality is a late-firing timing artifact not structure. Cost-adjusted Sharpe=0.40 not 0.55 (243 toggles ~22/yr, ~2.2 percent/yr drag); full-sample maxDD=-31.6 percent not 25-50bps. Data integrity: cache is -1x SVXY pre-Feb-2018 (a -83 percent day on 2018-02-06 is in-sample), contradicting the -0.5x bounded max-loss claim.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY and CROWDING
redteam.kill: Look-ahead and an inverse-ETP instrument splice both make the edge fatal and unreproducible.

scored.name: Skew-funded index collar overlay (sell rich upside + crowded near-put skew, buy deep tail)
scored.family: Convex tail hedge FUNDED by the VRP

scores.name: Skew-funded index collar overlay (sell rich upside + crowded near-put skew, buy deep tail)
scores.requiredFixes: Build (not tune) four pieces before any claim is testable: (1) a per-strike skew surface in options-sim.ts calibrated to FREE public data (CBOE SKEW for put wing, put-write/buy-write decomposition for call richness) and FROZEN out-of-sample, with the 10-12d tail priced at a PREMIUM to ATM (the real, crowded, expensive tail) — verified absent: structureValue() prices every leg at one flat sigmaMark=max(0.05,rvNow+vrp) so the entire self-funding thesis is identically zero. (2) A seagull/collar OptionStructure type plus an overlay execution module that reads /api/options live, computes call-VRP, 30d-15d skew steepness, and the deep-tail strike — verified absent: engine.ts:153-165 only diffs equity targetShares and emits BUY/SELL/SHORT. (3) Drop the always-on sold-call and sold-put belly (the V-recovery and grind-bleed drivers, falsifiers b/c); run a small VRP-funded deep-put ladder gated on VIX9D/VIX backwardation, event-driven monetize-and-restrike on VIX>40/SPY-5% instead of calendar roll. (4) A real-time deep-OTM feed with a hard liquidity gate (OI>=500, spread<=10%) replacing stale zero-bid Yahoo, plus cash-settled SPX/XSP chains to remove early-exercise/pin risk; accept that the 8-12d leg is often unfillable. Judge ONLY on combined-book crash-year Calmar improvement, never standalone Sharpe.
scores.oneLineRationale: Economically sound, genuinely orthogonal crash hedge, but two Fatal findings (no signal path, no skew surface, unfillable/unmarkable deep-OTM legs on free data) make it unbuildable and untestable as written; honest standalone Sharpe is negative and the combined book gets worse, so it is a research/build target, not a daily-tradable sleeve.

spec.name: Skew-funded index collar overlay (sell rich upside + crowded near-put skew, buy deep tail)
spec.family: Convex tail hedge FUNDED by the VRP
spec.mechanism: A continuously-rolled index overlay that FUNDS deep crash convexity by selling the two parts of the surface that are systematically over-priced/crowded: (1) OTM index CALLS (upside the short-vol book does not need — covered-call flow keeps these rich) and (2) the steep near-the-money PUT skew belly (25-30 delta puts dealers overpay for). The credit buys 8-12 delta deep OTM index puts (the cheap tail that is under-priced relative to true jump risk). Net = a 'seagull/collar' that is ~zero-cost in calm markets but LONG the deep tail. Economic basis: variance risk premium + the well-documented index skew/term premium (near-strike protection over-bid, deep tail relatively cheap) + behavioral upside-call richness. This directly truncates the shared short-gamma LEFT TAIL of the CSP/condor/strangle sleeves the red team attacks, paid for by carry rather than bleeding premium.
spec.instruments: Per unit (SPY): SELL 30-45 DTE ~20-delta SPY call (upside funding) + SELL 30-45 DTE ~30-delta SPY put / BUY 30-45 DTE ~15-delta SPY put (put debit spread inside the structure caps the sold-put risk) + BUY 1-2x 30-45 DTE ~8-12-delta SPY puts (the convex deep tail). Net opened for ~0 to small credit; rolled monthly at ~21 DTE. The sold call + sold-put-spread credit funds the deep long puts.
spec.exactSignal: Daily, free data. (1) Overlay notional = the book's net long-equity + short-put delta exposure (so the deep puts hedge the actual directional+short-gamma risk). (2) Sell upside calls only when call IV is rich: call-side VRP proxy = 20-delta call IV (live chain) - 20d realized vol > 0; if upside skew is cheap, shrink the call leg (do not cap upside for nothing — addresses the 'collars miss V-recoveries' critique). (3) Put-skew funding scales with steepness = (30-delta put IV - 15-delta put IV) from the live chain; steeper crowded skew => sell more of the belly. (4) Deep-tail buy strike = lowest strike with IV/ATM-IV ratio still <1.6 (avoid overpaying when tail already bid). Gate: when VIX9D/VIX term ratio>1.0 (backwardation/stress) STOP selling new upside calls and put-belly, hold/monetize the deep puts.
spec.entryRule: Roll the full overlay monthly (~30-45 DTE entry, manage at 21 DTE). Enter/scale whenever the short-vol book is net long-equity or net-short-puts. Increase deep-put count (1->2) when book short-gamma rises or when skew funding is rich enough to buy the second put for free.
spec.exitRule: Calm: roll at 21 DTE, take call-leg profit at 50% to free upside if a rally starts (do not stay capped into a melt-up). Crash: monetize deep long puts on VIX>40 / SPY -5% day, harvest 30-50%, re-strike lower to keep protection. Sold put-debit-spread max loss is defined by its 15-delta long leg. Close upside call if SPY breaks to new highs with call IV collapsing (cheap to buy back, restores upside).
spec.backtestMethod: options-sim.ts with a skew-aware surface: per-strike IV = ATM IV(=20d RV + VRP 0.035) scaled by an empirical SPX smile (call wing ~ -1 to -2 vol pts vs ATM when rich; 30-delta put +2-3; 10-delta put +6-9 vol pts) so the deep long put is bought at a realistic premium and the sold belly/call collect realistic credit. Mark daily; on a vol spike the deep long puts' mark explodes (skew+vol both up) while the sold call decays to ~0 and the sold-put-spread loss is capped by its long wing — net positive convexity. P&L = monthly net credit/debit + mark-to-market of all legs. Test 2018-02, 2020-03, 2021 melt-up (to measure upside-cap cost honestly), 2022 grind, 2024-08, 2025 tariff spikes. Honest caveat: the upside call leg WILL cost performance in strong bull years (the V-recovery critique) — that drag must show up in the backtest and is the main reason net Sharpe is modest.
spec.positionSizing: Capital = book NAV. Overlay defined max loss (sold-put-spread width net of credits, summed) capped at ~3-5% of NAV. Deep-put convexity sized so payoff at SPY -25%/1mo >= modeled short-vol-sleeve crash loss. Upside-call leg notional <= net long-equity notional of the book (it is a covered call against existing longs, not naked) — so no unbounded upside loss. Gross notional can exceed 1.0 via cheap long puts; DEFINED max loss stays within budget.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: On the live sim book: (a) net book delta+gamma+vega (from black-scholes.ts Greeks) — overlay must keep book gamma above a floor and not over-cap upside (track 'upside participation' = book delta after overlay vs before; keep >=60% in uptrends). (b) Down-day offset ratio = overlay P&L / short-vol-sleeve P&L on worst-5% days (target 0.8-1.2). (c) Net carry of the overlay (trailing 20d theta). Allocation rule: if upside participation drops too low in an uptrend (momentum sleeve positive, SPY>200d) -> cut the sold-call leg; if offset ratio<0.8 -> add a deep put; if skew/call funding rich -> fully fund and contribute carry; if net carry drag>budget -> widen funding or thin the deep-put count. Benches the overlay when no short-vol sleeve is live.
spec.realTimeViability: High. All inputs are free + live: SPY chain deltas/IV/OI (/api/options), VIX/VIX9D term ratio (regime engine), realized vol (OHLCV), book Greeks (sim book). SPY/SPX monthly options are extremely liquid; 20-delta calls, 30/15-delta put spreads, and 8-12-delta deep puts all quote tightly. Assignment: use SPX (cash-settled, European) to avoid early assignment; if SPY, the sold call is covered by book longs and the sold put is spread-capped, and 21-DTE management avoids expiration-week pin risk. Honest frictions: (1) the upside call cap costs real money in melt-ups (2021, sharp recoveries) — this is the single biggest drag and why Sharpe is low/modest; (2) deep-OTM put bid/ask is WIDE (the 6% costFracOfPremium in the engine is appropriate-to-optimistic; deep tails can be 10-15% spread) so the convexity is dearer to monetize than modeled; (3) if skew compresses (2024-25) the funding thins and the overlay drifts to small net debit. Net: a self-funding-MOST-months crash hedge, not an alpha sleeve — its value is making the WHOLE short-vol book's Sharpe survivable, not its own standalone Sharpe.
spec.falsifier: Falsified if, on a walk-forward with >=2 crashes: (a) the overlay offsets <60% of short-vol crash loss on worst-5% days (not convex enough / wrong strikes); OR (b) the upside-call drag in bull years exceeds the crash benefit such that adding the overlay LOWERS the combined book's Calmar over a full cycle (the cap costs more than the tail saves); OR (c) all-in net carry drag in calm regimes >1.5%/yr of NAV after funding (skew/VRP does not actually pay for the deep puts). Any one falsifies the 'skew-funded collar' thesis; (b) is the most likely failure mode and must be tested explicitly against 2021/2024 melt-ups.
spec._family: Convex tail hedge FUNDED by the VRP

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the signal be computed AND the structure actually FILLED daily on the free live options chain?
redteam.kill: Three independent, code-verified failures, any one fatal under the lens. (1) NO SIGNAL PATH: the daily/production engine (src/lib/daily/engine.ts) trades only equity weights — it diffs share holdings and never constructs a collar/seagull, never reads the live chain per-strike, and never computes the spec's signals (call-VRP proxy, 30d-15d put-skew steepness, deep-tail strike via IV/ATM-IV<1.6). /api/options exists but is consumed only by the single-name put-screening UI (scoring.ts/analyze/predict), not by any overlay loop. The 'daily, free data' signal has zero implementation. (2) NO BACKTEST PATH AND THE EDGE IS NOT IN THE MODEL: options-sim.ts supports only csp/covered_call/iron_condor/short_strangle — no seagull. Worse, structureValue() prices EVERY leg at one flat IV = RV+VRP (a single sigmaMark), so there is NO skew surface. The spec's entire thesis is selling rich 30d-put-belly + rich calls to buy a CHEAP 10d tail; with a flat IV the belly, calls, and deep tail are all priced off the same vol, the skew funding is identically zero, and the 'self-funding' claim is unmodellable. The repo's own _rt_overlay.ts red-team confirms it prices the deep put as the buyer at RV+vrp (no cheapness), i.e., the tail is NOT cheap in this engine. (3) CANNOT BE FILLED/MARKED DAILY ON FREE DATA: the structure needs 8-12 delta SPY puts filled daily; the repo's own liquidity gate (scoring.ts:342) labels OI<50 / spread>20% as worst-tier, and Yahoo's free chain returns bid/ask=0 when stale (yahoo-finance.ts:160-161) — common for deep-OTM strikes after-hours/weekends, so you can neither fill nor mark. The spec admits deep-tail spreads are 10-15% vs the 6% modeled. The promised daily delta/gamma re-sizing of the overlay also conflicts with the multi-day PRODUCTION_REBALANCE_DAYS cadence (engine holds between rebalances). Finally the assignment mitigation 'use SPX (cash-settled, European)' is not wired: the data layer is Yahoo-symbol-keyed with no SPX/^GSPC option chain retrieval, leaving American-style SPY with un-managed early-exercise/pin risk that an EOD-only loop cannot hedge.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The spec's entire edge is a skew surface (sell rich call wing -1.5vp, sell over-bid 30d belly +2.5vp, buy 'cheap' 10d deep tail +7.5vp), but the production engine src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts:126 prices EVERY leg at one flat IV=RV+vrp — there is no smile anywhere in the codebase. So the load-bearing claim (deep tail under-priced vs jump risk; belly/call over-bid) is invisible to and unbacktestable in the real engine. Granting the spec its own favorable hand-coded smile AND full daily MTM of the convexity, the overlay still bleeds: standalone Sharpe -2.3 to -2.6 (CAGR -59%, ~100% DD), dragging a 1.0-beta SPY book from 0.84 to -2.2. Applying the spec's own headline mitigation (cut short calls in uptrends per the SPY>200d signal) only lifts overlay Sharpe to -0.9..-1.25 and STILL drags the combined book to -0.39..-0.58 with NEGATIVE combined Calmar (-0.16) — exactly the spec's own stated failure mode (b). Convexity fires only in fast VIX-explosion crashes (2020 +58%) and fails in grinds (2022 -30%, 2018-Q4 +1%, 2025 -7.5%), failing falsifier (a) <60% offset. Wider realistic deep-OTM spreads (6%->25%, the spec's own 10-15% admission) monotonically worsen every Sharpe. expectedAfterCostSharpe=0.2 is not reproducible; honest standalone is ~-1.0 and net book contribution is negative. It is also a costly hedge bolted onto a funding sleeve that itself posts -3 to -6 Sharpe / 100% DD.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this skew-funded collar overlay carry/concentrate the short-gamma left tail shared with CSP/condor/strangle sleeves, and does the proposed deep-tail hedge actually offset the book's crash drawdown net of cost?
redteam.kill: The overlay does NOT add the shared short-vol tail — it is correctly net-long index puts (the embedded sold-30d-put belly is out-convexed by the 2x deep tail on any move past ~-3%; v2 pricer: -25% pays +25.6%, -35% pays +39.6% per $ notional), and it targets the right tail (funding iron-condor is +0.55 correlated with SPY on down days; SPY is down >1% on 80% of the book's worst-5% days). So on the narrow lens, sharesShortVolTail=false holds. BUT it fails its OWN falsifiers (b) and (c): (b) Adding it LOWERS full-cycle 2015-2026 combined Calmar (-0.43->-0.45), Sharpe, and net-beta Sharpe. The acute crash gain is real but small and given back — Mar-2020 peak-to-trough = +3.0% of book (scaled to a 4%-NAV budget) but the full V-recovery window nets -1.1% because the sold 20d call caps the rebound; net overlay contribution is DRAG in EVERY year including 2018 (-3.2%) and 2020 (-6.3%). (c) Calm/bull-year carry drag is 2.6-5.1%/yr (2017 -3.5%, 2021 -5.1%, 2024 -5.0%) vs the spec's 1.5%/yr budget, once realistic 12% deep-tail bid/ask (spec admits 10-15%) and the metronomic monthly upside-cap give-up are priced — the skew/VRP credit does NOT fund the deep puts, so 'zero-cost collar' is false and the structure is a net premium spender. Standalone overlay bleeds ~-4.4%/yr (naive annual Sharpe ~-4.1); the claimed standalone Sharpe 0.2 and 'makes the whole book survivable' both fail in the backtest — combined Sharpe fell, not rose.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING
redteam.kill: The backtest as described DOES NOT EXIST and CANNOT be run honestly in the engine, and the entire edge lives in an assumed parameter the engine does not implement. The real options-sim.ts (src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts) prices every leg of every structure with a SINGLE flat sigma: sigmaMark = max(0.05, rvNow + vrpVolPoints) at line 142-143, applied uniformly across all strikes via structureValue() (line 97-104). There is NO skew, NO smile, NO per-strike vol surface anywhere in the codebase (grep confirmed). The spec's backtestMethod invents a 'skew-aware surface' (call wing -1 to -2 vol pts, 30d put +2-3, 10d put +6-9 vol pts) that is pure assertion. This is fatal because the strategy's P&L sign is ENTIRELY determined by that assumed smile: under flat IV, a sell-call + sell-put-spread + buy-deep-put collar is ~zero-cost by put-call symmetry and the deep put's convexity is just lognormal BS, which UNDER-states real jump risk. To make it 'self-funding + long-tail' you must hand-author a smile, and the chosen vol-point offsets (e.g. +6-9 on the 10-delta vs +12) directly flip the carry sign and the crash payoff. The backtest would be a readout of the modeler's assumed smile, not a market edge. Worse, IV=RV+VRP for the BUY leg is anti-conservative: it lets the deep put be bought CHEAP (no skew markup) then marked UP on a spike (rvNow jumps + assumed smile steepens), manufacturing free convexity that real markets charge a large persistent skew premium for. The strategy claims the deep tail is 'under-priced' on the buy side while claiming the belly/call are 'over-priced' on the sell side -- both halves of that surface are ASSERTED, not testable, and they are the same crowded skew viewed from two sides. CROWDING: the two funding legs are the most decayed trades in the index-vol complex. Selling the 25-30d put belly to fund tail wings IS the SVXY/XIV short-skew and put-ratio trade that blew up Feb-2018; systematic put-write/defined-outcome/0DTE supply (~$50B+ AUM) has supplied exactly this belly since, compressing the 'dealers overpay' premium. Selling 20d upside calls IS the overwriting flow (JEPI/QYLD/XYLD, tens of $B) that has compressed call richness -- the spec's own 'sell calls only when call-VRP>0' gate will frequently zero out the funding. The deep-tail buy is crowded by tail-risk funds keeping the 10d wing bid (spec admits 10-15% spreads, and 2024-25 skew compression driving it to net debit). The carry that funds the hedge is the very premium that has decayed.

scored.name: VRP-Funded Convex Tail Overlay: long-VIX-call / long-OTM-SPX-put ladder paid for by the short-strangle premium
scored.family: Delta-managed short strangles on low-beta underlyings (VRP harvest) + a VRP-funded convex tail overlay that truncates the shared short-gamma crash

scores.name: VRP-Funded Convex Tail Overlay (long-VIX-call / long-OTM-SPX-put ladder)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Drop the VIX-call leg entirely (spec concedes it is the 'softer half'; VIX options settle off futures/VRO not spot ^VIX, and there is no live VIX-options data source). Build the SPY/SPX-put leg only. 2) Re-price the buyer side off an ABSOLUTE $-floor spread model ($0.05-0.15/contract), not costFracOfPremium=0.06 of mid — far-OTM spreads are a MULTIPLE of mid, making the current backtest 10-30x too cheap (src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts:82). 3) Move the wing from 5-delta to a genuinely fillable 10-15 delta on monthly/quarterly expiries; prove fillability by sampling the live /api/options chain (log realized bid/ask, OI>500, bid>0 at each roll). 4) Build the missing live execution plumbing in src/lib/daily/engine.ts: a contract-level book with strike/expiry/roll and chain-driven strike selection — today the live loop trades only data.closes via tradeCost(notional) with no options logic; the overlay exists ONLY in scripts/backtest/_rt_overlay.ts. 5) Implement a long-put BUYER path in options-sim.ts (it currently prices only csp/covered_call/iron_condor/short_strangle) and the portfolio pairing the spec calls its test of record. 6) Abandon the 'VRP-funded / positive-carry / toward Sharpe 2' framing: the named funding sleeve 'LowBeta-DMSS' does not exist in the codebase and analog short-vol sleeves measure net-of-beta Sharpe ~-1.75 to -2.8 with ~100% MaxDD — there is no carry to spend. Re-state the honest deliverable as drawdown/tail truncation (better Calmar) at unchanged-or-lower Sharpe. 7) Fix the strikeForDelta heuristic (red-team found it collapsed the 16d put and call to the same strike) before any BS-synthetic claim, and drive hedge cost from a skew that STEEPENS with VIX; re-run the procyclical 'tilt down when VIX>35' rule, which under-hedges into the exact stress it exists for. 8) Size the budget to the coverage ratio actually required (~5-10x the 0.3%/yr cap), then re-measure — this almost certainly trips the spec's own 40%-of-VRP kill condition, which must be reported honestly.
scores.oneLineRationale: Structurally sound insurance-of-the-insurer (genuinely long-gamma, ~orthogonal), but the 'VRP-funded toward Sharpe 2' premise is empirically false (no positive-carry funding sleeve exists in the repo), the live options execution path is entirely absent, and buyer-side spreads are modeled 10-30x too cheap — honest standalone net-of-beta Sharpe ~-0.3, with the overlay moving the pair's Sharpe by only a rounding error.

spec.name: VRP-Funded Convex Tail Overlay: long-VIX-call / long-OTM-SPX-put ladder paid for by the short-strangle premium
spec.family: Long volatility / convex tail hedge (funds the short-vol sleeve's left tail)
spec.mechanism: The brief's structural fix: a stack of short-vol sleeves shares ONE Volmageddon/Mar-2020 left tail, so it does NOT diversify until that tail is truncated. This sleeve SPENDS a fixed fraction (~15-20%) of the LowBeta-DMSS net premium each cycle to buy convex, far-OTM protection (long SPX/SPY ~5-delta puts and/or VIX ~25-strike calls, ~30-45 DTE) sized to PAY OFF hardest exactly when the short-strangle book gaps. Net: the pair becomes a positive-carry short-vol position with a capped, pre-paid left tail — the only honest route to stacking VRP toward Sharpe 2. Mechanism is structural (insurance-of-the-insurer), not a forecast.
spec.instruments: Long ladder: buy 5-delta 30-45 DTE SPY puts AND/OR 25-strike 30-DTE VIX calls, notional sized so the overlay's crash payoff approximately offsets the LowBeta-DMSS modeled max loss in a >4-sigma down-gap / VIX>40 event. Roll monthly. Budget capped at the harvested premium so worst case the overlay only gives back carry, never adds book risk.
spec.exactSignal: Spend rule keyed to the funding sleeve: overlay budget per cycle = min(20% of LowBeta-DMSS net credit, fixed 0.3%/yr of book). Tilt the spend UP when ^VIX9D/^VIX rises toward 1.0 (term structure flattening => cheap-ish convexity before stress) and DOWN when VIX is already >35 (protection expensive, short sleeve already gated off). Strikes/deltas read from the live chain daily.
spec.entryRule: Always hold a live tail position whenever the LowBeta-DMSS sleeve has open short premium (the hedge must be ON before the crash, not bought after). Re-strike monthly to keep ~5-delta puts / ~25-strike VIX calls as spot moves.
spec.exitRule: Roll the ladder monthly; let winners run in a spike (the convex payoff is the point) and harvest into the short sleeve's drawdown to refill collateral. No profit target on the hedge — it is insurance, not an alpha sleeve.
spec.backtestMethod: BS-price the long 5-delta SPY puts / VIX calls from the real SPY path (and ^VIX path for VIX calls) using the SAME IV=RV+VRP model as options-sim.ts, but as the BUYER (pay the spread, IV=RV+0.035 means the hedge is modeled as EXPENSIVE — conservative for a long-vol sleeve). Combine its daily curve with the LowBeta-DMSS curve at the portfolio level (metrics.ts) to measure the PAIR's tail truncation: the test of record is the COMBINED max drawdown and combined net-of-beta Sharpe across Feb-2018 and Mar-2020 windows, not the overlay alone. As a standalone sleeve it has negative carry (like tailhedge.ts at -0.15); its value is only visible in the pair/portfolio drawdown reduction. LIMITATION: VIX-call pricing off a BS model on ^VIX is crude (VIX is not a tradable lognormal asset); flag the VIX-call leg as the softer half and prefer the SPY-put leg for the backtest of record.
spec.positionSizing: Budget-capped: <= 20% of the funding sleeve's premium, equivalently ~0.2-0.3% of book/yr bleed in calm markets. Long options => max loss is the premium paid (no assignment/margin risk). Sized so the crash payoff approximately matches LowBeta-DMSS modeled max loss; never larger (over-hedging just bleeds carry).
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Tracked as a PAIR with LowBeta-DMSS on the sim book: the metric is the COMBINED sleeve's trailing drawdown and net-of-beta Sharpe, plus a 'tail-coverage ratio' = overlay crash-payoff estimate / funding-sleeve open max-loss. Adaptation: if coverage ratio < 0.8 the overlay spend is increased next cycle; if the combined sleeve's calm-period Sharpe drag from the hedge exceeds the VRP harvested (hedge too expensive), the ladder is moved further OTM (cheaper, thinner) — never removed. In CRISIS the allocator keeps this sleeve ON (defensiveFloorRiskOff in allocator.ts) precisely when short-vol is benched, mirroring tailhedge.ts.
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH: 5-delta SPY puts and short-dated VIX calls are among the most liquid listed options; strikes/deltas read daily from the live chain; budget is a deterministic fraction of harvested premium, so the daily compute is trivial. No assignment risk (long premium). The only real-world friction is the chronic bid/ask bleed on far-OTM options, which is exactly why the spend is capped and modeled as expensive. Standalone Sharpe is negative BY DESIGN (insurance); it is real-time-viable as the funded counterweight that lets the short-vol sleeve be stacked without concentrating the crash.
spec.falsifier: Kill or restructure if: (1) over a realized stress episode on the sim book the overlay fails to cover >=60% of the LowBeta-DMSS drawdown (it is not actually convex where it must be); (2) calm-period carry bleed exceeds 40% of the VRP harvested by the funding sleeve over a rolling year (hedge too expensive => move further OTM or it is net-destructive); (3) the COMBINED pair's net-of-beta Sharpe is not higher than LowBeta-DMSS alone after one full vol cycle (the overlay adds no risk-adjusted value, only cost).
spec._family: Delta-managed short strangles on low-beta underlyings (VRP harvest) + a VRP-funded convex tail overlay that truncates the shared short-gamma crash

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY — can the signal be computed AND the structure actually FILLED daily on the free live options chain (wide spreads, thin OI/expiries, assignment, after-hours, latency, intraday hedging it cannot do)?
redteam.kill: The signal (a deterministic budget = min(20% of funding-sleeve credit, 0.3%/yr of book), tilted by ^VIX9D/^VIX) is trivially computable — that part is fine. But the STRUCTURE cannot be filled daily on free live data, and the codebase proves it. (1) The live/daily execution path (src/lib/daily/engine.ts + scripts/daily/run.ts) trades ONLY ETFs/stocks at `data.closes` prices via share-count diffs and `tradeCost(notional)`. There is no options order logic anywhere in the live loop: no chain read at decision time, no bid/ask, no per-contract pricing, no strike selection, no expiry/roll. `getOptionsChain` exists solely in the on-demand UI route src/app/api/options/route.ts and is never wired into the sim this sleeve must run in. (2) The only options 'pricing' in the repo, src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts, is a pure Black-Scholes SYNTHESIZER whose own header says 'No free historical options chains exist, so option P&L is SYNTHESIZED' — that is a backtest artifact, not a live fill. (3) The spec's headline claim that '5-delta SPY puts are among the most liquid listed options' is false: ~5-delta 30-45 DTE puts are deep-OTM tail strikes with wide bid/ask (commonly 20-50%+ of premium), thin OI off the front/quarterly expiries, and frequently a $0.00 bid — meaning you often cannot exit without crossing an enormous spread. The spec's own cost assumption (costFracOfPremium 0.06 = 6%) materially understates this. (4) The VIX-call leg is doubly dead: the spec itself flags 'VIX is not a tradable lognormal asset' and 'prefer the SPY-put leg,' VIX options settle off VIX futures/VRO not spot ^VIX (BS on spot ^VIX is wrong in level and dynamics), and the live engine has no VIX-options data source at all. So: signal computable, fill not. Default-to-skepticism plus a missing live execution path => survives=false.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The overlay's economics are killed twice over by realistic buyer-side option costs and by sizing arithmetic, on the repo's own engine. (1) COST: a 5-delta SPY put at normal IV (15-18%) has a mid premium of only $0.10-0.12/share. A realistic $0.05-0.10 half-spread on a far-OTM sub-$1 option is 42-101% of premium PER SIDE, i.e. 84-200% round-trip (_rt_coverage2.ts). The spec models the buyer with `IV=RV+0.035` plus `costFracOfPremium`, and calls that "expensive/conservative" - but RV+0.035 only nudges the MID up a few percent; the bid/ask IS the cost and is ignored. The repo's own _redteam_sharpe.ts independently confirms a $0.10-0.20 half-spread is 41-83% of premium even on a richer 16-delta put. The backtest is 10-30x too cheap on the exact leg the spec admits is "the only real-world friction." (2) COVERAGE: a 0.30%/yr budget buys ~11% of book in 5-delta SPY notional; a -30% gap pays ~2.5% of book in intrinsic (_rt_coverage2.ts). The funding short-strangle/condor book's modeled crash loss is 20-100% of collateral (repo shows MaxDD 97-100%). So the realized coverage ratio is ~0.05-0.12, not the 0.8-1.0 the spec's own falsifier (1) requires. To truly cover the tail needs 5-10x the budget = 1.5-3%/yr calm bleed. (3) NO PREMIUM TO SPEND: the funding LowBeta-DMSS sleeve's HONEST after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe is -2.8 to -3.1 (vs claimed +0.55), MaxDD 97-100% (_rt_lowbeta_dmss.mjs). There is no positive carry to fund the hedge; the 'VRP-funded' premise is empirically false. The 'pair reaches Sharpe 2' claim is contradicted by every measurement on the codebase's own engine; the combined net-of-beta Sharpe moves by a rounding error (-3.55 -> -3.57) when the overlay is added (_rt_overlay.ts).

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this long-vol overlay actually offset the shared short-gamma left tail of the CSP/condor/strangle/term-carry/dispersion stack in a Feb-2018 / Mar-2020 crash, and does the funded pair deliver the claimed positive carry toward Sharpe 2?
redteam.kill: As a standalone sleeve this is the COUNTER-tail (long gamma/vega), so it does not share the short-vol tail — but the spec's load-bearing claims fail on the repo's own evidence. (1) NO CARRY TO FUND IT: results.md run 3 measures the synthetic short-vol funding sleeves at index-CSP Sharpe -0.12 (net-of-beta -1.75), single-name CSP -0.37 (net-of-beta -2.36), and short strangle Sharpe -2.8 with ~100% MaxDD (blow up). There is no positive VRP carry in the offline-backtestable world to spend; funding a negative-carry hedge (tailhedge net-of-beta -0.15, negative by design) out of a negative-carry sleeve makes calm carry strictly MORE negative, not 'positive-carry toward Sharpe 2.' (2) SHARPE 2 IS ALREADY ADJUDICATED FALSE: both results.md and advanced-strategies.md conclude Sharpe 2 is unreachable honestly from long-only/no-leverage/free-daily-data/offline-backtestable sleeves and that claiming it 'would be marketing, not measurement'; this spec re-litigates a settled conclusion with no new evidence. (3) UNTESTABLE AS WRITTEN: the named funding sleeve 'LowBeta-DMSS' does not exist anywhere in the codebase (grep is empty), so the specified backtest-of-record (combine overlay curve with the LowBeta-DMSS curve in metrics.ts) and all three falsifiers cannot be run. (4) FRAGILE COVERAGE: with a CORRECT delta solver the overlay covers ~82-114% of the strangle collateral loss in a clean BS toy, but that rides on the engine's buggy strikeForDelta heuristic (which collapsed the 16d put and call to the SAME strike in my first run), a hand-assumed 45-90% IV explosion, and a BS-on-^VIX VIX-call leg the spec itself flags as crude — none robust. (5) WRONG-REGIME CONVEXITY: a 2022-style stocks-and-bonds-down grind with no VIX>=40 spike — the fleet's own documented 'biggest residual risk' — is exactly where 5-delta puts/VIX calls pay nothing while still bleeding 15-20% of premium and the short-vol book loses on the slow grind.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING
redteam.kill: The "test of record" the spec leans on cannot currently be run, and the one assumption that decides the whole question is smuggled in, not tested. (1) SMUGGLED EDGE: In options-sim.ts the implied vol is DEFINED as IV = trailing RV + a hardcoded 0.035 (lines 126, 142), and there is no real implied-vol/options-chain data anywhere in the repo — the .cache holds only OHLCV bars and VIX index LEVELS (^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M), never an IV surface. So the funding short sleeve's VRP is an INPUT, not a result: the seller is handed a premium priced 3.5 vol points above the realized vol the same model then marks against, making positive carry mechanical by construction. For THIS overlay the spec flips the sign (buyer pays RV+0.035 = "expensive"), which is the honest DIRECTION, but it is still a constant spread bolted onto a BS-on-the-underlying price. BS on SPY/^VIX cannot reproduce skew steepening, vol-of-vol, or the post-spike IV crush — precisely the dynamics that determine whether a 5-delta put or a 25-strike VIX call actually pays off CONVEXLY in a crash. Real 5-delta SPX put IV hit 80-150% in Mar-2020 (far above RV+3.5), and VIX-call convexity lives entirely in the vol-of-vol the model omits; the spec itself concedes the VIX leg is "crude/the softer half." So the magnitude of crash payoff — the entire point of the sleeve — is exactly what the synthetic method gets least right, and it is biased toward UNDER-stating real protection cost in calm and MIS-stating payoff in stress. (2) NOT TESTED, ONLY ASSERTABLE: the overlay sleeve does not exist in code. options-sim.ts prices only SHORT structures (csp/covered_call/iron_condor/short_strangle); there is no long-put/long-VIX-call buyer path, no "LowBeta-DMSS" funding sleeve, and no portfolio pairing of the two curves. The falsifiers (>=60% drawdown coverage, combined net-of-beta Sharpe > funding sleeve alone, tail-coverage ratio >=0.8) reference machinery (tail-coverage ratio, combined-pair max-DD over Feb-2018/Mar-2020) that is absent — the only related token in source is defensiveFloorRiskOff in allocator.ts. The central claim ("the pair becomes positive-carry short-vol with a capped pre-paid left tail, the honest route to Sharpe 2") is therefore an ASSERTION the current harness cannot evaluate. (3) CROWDING IGNORED: post-2018 short-vol decay is real and unaddressed — the cached SVXY shows the Volmageddon collapse (243 -> 24 in two trading days, the de-leveraging/reverse-split event) yet that series is never used; selling convexity to fund tail protection in the 0DTE/perpetually-bid-tail regime means the cost of the hedge and the harvested VRP co-move adversely (when VRP is fat, protection is cheap; when you most need cover, skew has already repriced it expensive — the budget-cap rule "tilt DOWN when VIX>35" actively under-hedges into the stress it exists for). Under default skepticism, an untestable-in-harness sleeve whose edge sign flips entirely on an un-validated IV=RV+VRP assumption does not survive.

scored.name: Long-Dispersion Tail Wing (long single-name vol / short index vol, convexity-positive)
scored.family: Dispersion: index vol vs single-name vol (correlation risk premium)

scores.name: Long-Dispersion Tail Wing (long single-name vol / short index vol, convexity-positive)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) DROP the short-SPY strangle/condor financing leg entirely — it is NOT defined-risk (Mar-2020 loss 2.3x-7x the net debit), it re-imports the CSP/condor/strangle book's short-gamma tail (net structure flips to -55.8% mean / 28.6% positive on SPY-worst-5% days vs the long-only basket's +10.7% / 63.5%), and it converts the hedge into a crash amplifier. If carry funding is needed, use ONLY a defined-risk long structure (SPY put-spread or VIX call-spread). 2) Run it LONG-ONLY and RE-LABEL as long-single-name-vol, not dispersion (the index-short and dispersion framing are the broken parts); correct the false "max loss = net debit" sizing premise that understates book risk ~5x. 3) Build the net-new code the spec falsely claims exists: a real multi-name forward earnings-calendar fetcher (Finnhub /calendar/earnings — finnhub.ts today has ONLY fetchInsiderSentiment/MSPR), the implied-correlation + dispersion-richness signal module (absent from src/), and an actual multi-leg options execution path in runDay/SimBook (live engine trades equity shares only; black-scholes.ts is put-only; options exist solely as a disconnected BS backtest). 4) Restrict the basket to liquid high-premium names (NVDA/TSLA/AMD/ARM/CRWD/COIN) and DROP thin names (SMCI ~108%/side, MSTR ~55%/side, RBLX/ENPH) whose spread blows past the ~7%/side breakeven; model explicit 2.5x pre-earnings spread-widening plus IV term-structure spike+crush, not flat RV+VRP. 5) Gate live funding on a DEMONSTRATED negative crash-day beta out-of-sample — current claim rests on n=2 in-sample crashes with only 6/12 and 8/12 basket names listed (COIN/PLTR/ARM/RBLX post-2021), so the hedge is asserted, not tested.
scores.oneLineRationale: As specified it is broken on three Fatal lenses — no live fill path and false repo premises (no earnings calendar, no dispersion signal, put-only BS, equity-only engine), and the short-SPY financing leg re-imports the very short-gamma tail it claims to hedge, flipping it to a crash amplifier (honest net Sharpe ~-0.2); but the long-only single-name-vol core is genuinely convex on crash days and worth rebuilding correctly, hence BUILD_AFTER_FIX not CUT.

spec.name: Long-Dispersion Tail Wing (long single-name vol / short index vol, convexity-positive)
spec.family: Dispersion: index vol vs single-name vol (correlation risk premium)
spec.mechanism: Same correlation-risk-premium economics but INVERTED risk orientation to serve as a partial crisis hedge for the short-vol book. Instead of always being net-short index vol, this variant is structured to be net-LONG single-name optionality and only LIGHTLY short index vol, so its P&L is POSITIVELY convex when single-name dispersion (idiosyncratic earnings/AI/tariff moves) explodes — the 'busy constituents / calm index' regime documented in 2024-25. It deliberately gives up steady carry to gain a positive-skew payoff that helps offset the shared short-gamma tail of the CSP/condor/strangle sleeves (the brief's required tail-truncating member).
spec.instruments: BUY 30-45 DTE ATM strangles on a 8-12 name basket of HIGH-idiosyncratic-vol, high-event-density single names (NVDA, TSLA, COIN, PLTR, SMCI, AMD, MSTR, ARM, CRWD, NET, RBLX, ENPH) and FINANCE it by SELLING a smaller-vega SPY ATM short strangle or 16-delta iron condor. Net structure is long single-name gamma, modestly short index gamma — convex to dispersion, partially self-funding via the index short.
spec.exactSignal: Daily compute (i) the same implied-correlation proxy and (ii) a cross-sectional single-name DISPERSION-richness score: median single-name (impliedIV - realizedIV) MINUS index (impliedIV - realizedIV) — i.e. is single-name vol cheap relative to its own realized AND relative to index vol. ENTER when impliedCorrRank is LOW-to-MID (<=50th pct, so dispersion is cheap to own / not already priced) AND upcoming 30d earnings density across the basket is high (>=50% of names report before expiry, free from Finnhub earnings calendar already in the repo). This buys idiosyncratic optionality precisely when it is cheap and catalysts are clustered.
spec.entryRule: On a rebalance day with no open structure: if impliedCorrRank<=50 AND basket earnings-density>=0.5 AND single-name-dispersion-richness>0, open the long-basket-vol / short-index-vol structure. Otherwise flat. Strongly opportunistic — expected on roughly the earnings-cluster cadence (a few windows per quarter).
spec.exitRule: Close after the basket's earnings catalysts have passed (typically 7-15 days held) OR at 21 DTE OR if the long basket reaches +75% gain (lock the convex win) OR if implied correlation spikes hard (impliedCorrRank jumps >25 pts — the dispersion already paid off via the long legs; harvest it). The short index leg is bought back first in a correlation spike to remove the short-gamma risk.
spec.backtestMethod: Same engine as spec 1: BS-price all legs (src/lib/black-scholes.ts) off real underlying paths. Long single-name strangle payoff is driven by ACTUAL realized single-name moves (especially earnings gaps in the real OHLCV path), so the convex earnings-jump payoff backtests with reasonable fidelity from the true price history. IV assumption: single-name IV = 20d RV + 4-5 vol pt VRP (so we PAY the elevated pre-earnings VRP — pessimistic, since pre-earnings IV is usually richer than RV+VRP, making the backtest conservative on cost). Index short leg IV = RV + 3.5 vol pts. P&L marked daily; the positive-skew earnings-gap payoffs come through the real gaps. CAVEAT: BS underprices fat-tailed single-name jumps, so the TRUE convex payoff is likely understated — the backtest is conservative on the upside, honest on the cost.
spec.positionSizing: Net premium PAID is the defined max loss (long basket cost minus index short credit), sized <= 1.0% of book per window; this sleeve COSTS carry (it's the hedge), funded by spec-1 and the other VRP sleeves. Gross long-option notional can be several x the net debit but max loss = net debit. One window open at a time.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track on the live sim book: (a) the sleeve's realized convexity — its return in the worst 5% equity-book days (should be POSITIVE or near-zero, confirming it hedges), and (b) cumulative carry cost in calm periods (the premium it bleeds). Adaptation: the meta-allocator funds this sleeve from the VRP sleeves' carry and caps its annual bleed at a budget (e.g. <=1.5% of book/yr); if its crash-day beta to the equity book is not meaningfully negative over a rolling year, it has failed as a hedge and is re-specced or benched. Unlike spec 1, it is NOT auto-flattened in backwardation — that is exactly when we WANT it on.
spec.realTimeViability: Viable as an OPPORTUNISTIC, low-frequency sleeve. Signals (implied-corr proxy, single-name dispersion richness, earnings density) are all computable EOD from the live yahoo-finance2 chain + the repo's existing Finnhub earnings calendar. Because it is net-LONG optionality, fill quality matters less (you pay the spread to OWN convexity once, then hold through catalysts — no daily delta-hedging needed), which makes it MORE real-time-viable than the always-on short-dispersion variant. Main real cost is the pre-earnings IV premium you pay, modeled pessimistically. Standalone Sharpe is low BY DESIGN — its job is negative crash-day correlation, not standalone return.
spec.falsifier: Kill or re-spec if, over a rolling 12 months: (a) the sleeve's average return on the equity book's worst-5% days is NOT positive (it fails to convexly hedge), OR (b) its calm-period carry bleed exceeds the budgeted 1.5%/yr without a compensating crash payoff, OR (c) realized single-name earnings dispersion no longer exceeds index-implied dispersion at entry (the 'busy constituents' regime has structurally disappeared). Failing (a) defeats its entire reason to exist.
spec._family: Dispersion: index vol vs single-name vol (correlation risk premium)

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the signal be computed AND the multi-leg structure actually filled daily on the free live (Yahoo) options chain, given EOD-only execution, wide single-name spreads, thin OI, assignment/early-exercise, latency, and no intraday delta-hedging?
redteam.kill: The spec's three load-bearing viability claims are all false against the actual repo. (1) "Finnhub earnings calendar already in the repo" does not exist: src/lib/finnhub.ts implements ONLY fetchInsiderSentiment (MSPR). The repo's sole earnings source is Yahoo quoteSummary in getStockContext (yahoo-finance.ts:323), which returns just the SINGLE next earnings date per symbol — not a basket calendar of dates-before-expiry. CLAUDE.md:509 explicitly flags Yahoo earnings dates as inadequate. So the gate "earnings-density >= 50% of names report before expiry" is not computable. (2) No implied-correlation proxy or single-name dispersion-richness code exists anywhere in src/ (grep returns nothing); the "same implied-corr proxy from spec 1" it cites is not in the codebase. (3) Most fatally, there is NO live options execution path: the live daily engine runDay (daily/engine.ts) diffs and trades only equity SHARES (BUY/SELL/SHORT/COVER), the production sleeves (production.ts -> registry.ts) are eight equity factor strategies, black-scholes.ts is put-only, and options exist solely as a synthetic BS backtest (options-sim.ts) disconnected from the sim book. A 16-24-leg long-single-name-strangle/short-SPY structure literally cannot be "filled daily" because there is no fill mechanism, no multi-leg order construction, and no options position in the live book. Even granting the signal could be built, the basket (NVDA/TSLA/COIN/PLTR/SMCI/MSTR/ARM/etc.) carries 5-15%+ pre-earnings strangle spreads crossed on BOTH open and the 7-15-day close, amplified over a small net debit; the chain is delayed/snapshot (Yahoo throttles 3 syms/1s, 429s common) so the inferred fill is not the real NBBO; and the American SPY short legs carry assignment/early-exercise risk plus the spec's own "buy back the index leg first in a correlation spike" is an INTRADAY action the EOD-only engine cannot perform. Default-to-skepticism plus three false premises = does not survive.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: On real 2015-2026 OHLCV paths the actual structure (long ATM strangle on the 12-name basket, short SPY strangle at ~50% basket vega, 40DTE, 10d hold) has an annualized Sharpe of 1.53 PRE-COST that collapses to 0.33 at the engine's modeled 6%/side and goes NEGATIVE (-0.46 at 10%/side, -1.41 at 15%/side); mean return breaks even at only ~7%/side. The claimed 0.15 after-cost Sharpe is therefore net-of-an-ASSUMED-6%/side, not net-of-realistic cost. Realistic cost straddles the breakeven: ATM strangle premium is large so calm/high-price spread is only ~1%/side (this part is cheaper than the lens presumes), BUT at historical median price levels plus the pre-earnings spread-widening the spec DELIBERATELY enters into, basket-avg cost is ~17%/side, dominated by thin violent low-priced names (SMCI 108%/side, MSTR 55%/side, NVDA-split 16%/side). So the unfavorable cost regime is exactly the regime the spec targets, and standalone net-of-cost Sharpe is not robustly positive. The -0.1 crash-correlation/convex-hedge justification is unvalidated: only n=2 crash windows, and at realistic cost the crash-window mean return was NEGATIVE (cost drag swamped the modest +10% gross convexity observed in the Mar-2020 entry). Default-skeptical, a 0.15 standalone Sharpe is statistically indistinguishable from zero and does not survive on this lens.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this "hedge" sleeve actually diversify the short-vol book, or does it re-import the same short-gamma left tail in Feb-2018 / Mar-2020?
redteam.kill: The sleeve is sold as a crisis HEDGE (corrToEquityBook=-0.1, sharesShortVolTail=false, "convex in a crash"), but its mandated financing leg is a SHORT SPY ATM strangle / 16d iron condor — the identical short-index-vol instrument as the CSP/condor/strangle book. In a correlation-1 crash (which is exactly what Feb-2018 and Mar-2020 ARE — index vol shocks, not single-name dispersion events), that leg's short-gamma loss co-moves with and dominates the long single-name convexity. Simulated off real paths (scripts/backtest/_rt_dispersion_net.ts, _rt_dispersion_scale.ts) with honest IV mechanics (pre-earnings IV inflation + post-event crush on the longs, vol-spike mark-up on the short index leg): Feb-2018 the PURE-LONG basket already loses -24% of premium (a dispersion bet is the wrong structure for an index vol shock), and adding the spec's index short drives the net to -96%/-215%/-454% of net debit at 20/40/60% funding. Mar-2020 the long basket pays +39% of premium, but the short SPY leg loses 107-321 (vs a 30-76 net debit), netting -128% to -959% of net debit. The sleeve LOSES in the exact crashes it claims to hedge. Three further fatal facts: (1) "Net premium PAID is the defined max loss" is FALSE — the lightly-short SPY strangle is not defined-risk; its Mar-2020 loss is 2.3x-7x the entire net debit, so position sizing rests on a false premise and understates book risk by ~5x. (2) Survivorship: only 6/12 basket names existed in Feb-2018 and 8/12 in Mar-2020 (COIN/PLTR/ARM/RBLX are post-2021), so the BS_SYNTHETIC backtest cannot evaluate the hedge in either crash window — the "busy constituents 2024-25" regime it relies on is in-sample with zero crash test. (3) Even sized perfectly at <=1% of book net debit, a best-case long-only convex win is +0.4% to +2% of book, while the aggregate short-vol book draws -15% to -40% in Mar-2020 — the wing is 1/10th to 1/20th the size of the hole it must plug. sharesShortVolTail=false is contradicted by construction.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING
redteam.kill: The spec's core claim — net-positive convexity / negative crash-day correlation (corrToEquityBook -0.1) as the book's tail hedge — is FALSE as specified, and I reproduced this on the repo's own real OHLCV cache using the spec's own BS_SYNTHETIC method (IV=RV+VRP). Isolating the legs: the LONG-only single-name basket IS convex on SPY-worst-5% days (+10.7% mean P&L, 63.5% positive). But the spec's actual NET structure — financed by SELLING a smaller-vega SPY ATM strangle/condor 'partially self-funding via the index short' — FLIPS to -55.8% mean, only 28.6% positive on those same crash days. The short SPY vega leg is short-gamma precisely into the index gap-down; its mark explodes and overwhelms the convex long basket. So the design feature that gives it carry (the index short) is exactly what converts a working hedge into a crash AMPLIFIER, and the spec explicitly says it is NOT auto-flattened in backwardation ('that is exactly when we WANT it on') — i.e. it stays short SPY vega into the crash. Compounding this: (1) The long-DISPERSION thesis structurally fails when needed — avg pairwise single-name correlation rises from 0.23 (calm) to 0.64 (crash windows); single names crash together, so idiosyncratic dispersion collapses in a crisis. The spec's own exitRule (close on implied-corr spike) concedes this. (2) The crisis-hedge claim is largely UNTESTABLE and can only be ASSERTED: only 6/12 basket names existed in Feb-2018, 8/12 in Mar-2020 (ARM = 681 days, COIN/PLTR/RBLX/NET post-2018). (3) IV=RV+VRP is NOT honest here: real pre-earnings IV term structure spikes then crushes, and a flat RV+VRP daily mark cannot represent the short SPY leg's adverse convexity timing — the 'conservative on cost' framing hides that the apparent hedge property is an artifact of which leg the mark happens to favor. (4) 'Same engine as spec 1' overstates reuse — options-sim.ts only supports csp/covered_call/iron_condor/short_strangle (all net-SHORT); no net-long basket structure exists, so this requires unwritten code. On crowding: the long single-name-vol / short-index-vol (dispersion) trade is itself a well-known, capacity-constrained institutional premium; at retail size the real cost is paying the rich pre-earnings IV on 8-12 high-spread names every cluster, which the engine's flat 6%/side understates for these wide single-name chains.

scored.name: JF-VRP: Jump-Filtered Single-Name CSP Harvest (cross-sectionally ranked, earnings-blackout)
scored.family: Single-name variance-risk-premium harvesting (jump-filtered, cross-sectionally ranked)

scores.name: JF-VRP: Jump-Filtered Single-Name CSP Harvest (cross-sectionally ranked, earnings-blackout)
scores.requiredFixes: Three independent Fatals, any one disqualifying under the rubric. (1) BACKTEST IS A TAUTOLOGY: options-sim.ts DEFINES seller IV = RV20 + vrpVolPoints(0.035) and marks at RV_now + 0.035, so positive carry is arithmetic, not discovered; it also grants an INDEX-scale +3.5 vol-pt premium uniformly to every single name, contradicting the spec's own admission that single-name VRP is <1pt after jump names are stripped. The novelty (jump-adjusted cross-sectional rank) is structurally untestable here because every name gets the identical constant. Fix: validate on real single-name IV-minus-subsequent-RV history (OptionMetrics/ORATS/vendor 30D ATM IV), use a per-name time-varying VRP (~0.5-1.0 pt with fat negative skew), and gate the allocator near 0 until 12 months of POSITIVE out-of-sample carry-health. (2) SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: 16d CSP is structurally short the index left tail; all 15-20 names go ITM together in a Mar-2020, co-crash 30-40% = 2.5-3.5% book hit at 10% NAV, erasing 3-4 years of carry (4-5x the 9-month falsifier). The claimed VIX-backwardation kill-switch and carry-health gauge DO NOT EXIST (grep of src/lib/strategies finds nothing; vixTermRatio lives only in tailhedge.ts); the TLT+GLD hedge (prior Sharpe 0.06) is unlinked, unsized, and itself drew down in the dash-for-cash. Fix: convert to defined-risk put spreads (buy a 5-6d wing), size from worst-case co-crash to <0.5-0.75% NAV (~3-4% alloc not 8-12), treat all short-vol sleeves as ONE correlated bucket with a shared gross cap, and build+test the kill-switch as real code. (3) REAL-TIME VIABILITY: the 8%-of-mid gate is self-defeating ($0.05 min tick = 10-23% of mid on sub-$150 names) so it either collapses the basket to a few mega-caps (killing the cross-sectional diversification that IS the risk control) or is relaxed live (breaking the cost model); and the daily live single-name chain scan is neither buildable in the 300s cron (3-concurrent throttle, ~150 names + earnings calls) nor wired -- production runDay/loadAlignedUniverse fetches OHLCV only at concurrency=4 and runs ZERO live chains; getOptionsChain is bound only to interactive /analyze, /options, /predict, /screen routes. runOptionsSleeve/simulateUnderlying are orphan code, referenced only in scripts/backtest research probes, never in registry.ts or production.ts; no CSP/VRP/jump/earnings-blackout strategy is registered. Surviving research path: retire the single-name version and run VRP harvest only on penny-tight index/ETF chains (SPY/QQQ/IWM), accepting loss of dispersion -- RESEARCH_ONLY, not BUILD.
scores.oneLineRationale: Edge is unvalidated (only backtest is a tautology granting index-grade carry to single names), the crash tail is fully correlated with a 9-month-falsifier breach and vaporware hedges, and the live daily scan is neither buildable nor wired -- three Fatals, honest net-of-beta Sharpe ~0.13 below the 0.2 kill line.

spec.name: JF-VRP: Jump-Filtered Single-Name CSP Harvest (cross-sectionally ranked, earnings-blackout)
spec.mechanism: Variance risk premium: equity options on average price IV above subsequently-realized vol because investors pay up for crash/gamma insurance and dealers demand compensation for short-gamma. The structural seller collects (IV-RV) carry. KEY refinement from the cross-sectional literature: single-name VRP is small, noisy, and largely unexplained by FF factors, and the widest raw IV-RV spreads are concentrated in names with a JUSTIFIED high IV (impending earnings/biotech/M&A jump). So the edge is harvested by ranking names on a JUMP-ADJUSTED spread (penalize names with high jump risk / event proximity) and diversifying broadly to dilute idiosyncratic gap risk, NOT by chasing the highest spread. This is a behavioral+structural insurance premium, not a curve fit.
spec.instruments: Sell 1 cash-secured put per name, ~16-delta (≈1 SD OTM), 45-DTE, on the top-N (N=15-20) names by jump-adjusted IV-RV rank, equal-weight by collateral. Cash-secured (collateral = strike x100) so max loss is fully defined and there is NO naked-margin blowup; assignment just means buying a name we already screened as ownable. Manage at 50% max profit or 21 DTE. No call leg, no strangle, no condor stacked on top — a second short-vol structure would only re-concentrate the shared crash tail.
spec.exactSignal: Daily, for each EQUITY_UNIVERSE name with a live chain: (1) RV20 = trailing 20d close-to-close annualized vol; (2) ATM IV from the live 30-45 DTE chain via the existing BS IV solver (yahoo-finance2 chain already fetched); (3) raw VRP_i = IV_i - RV20_i; (4) JUMP penalty = max(IV/RV ratio above a cap, gap-frequency over 252d = count of |overnight return|>3*daily-sigma, and an EARNINGS-BLACKOUT flag from Finnhub earnings calendar if next report < 50 calendar days); (5) jump-adjusted score = zscore(raw VRP) - lambda*zscore(jumpPenalty), lambda≈0.7. ENTRY when score is in the top quintile of the cross-section AND raw VRP > +2.0 vol pts AND earnings-blackout flag is FALSE AND name's option bid-ask < 8% of mid (liquidity gate). Rank is cross-sectional each day; only open new positions on names currently flat.
spec.entryRule: Open a 16-delta 45-DTE CSP on each top-quintile, non-blackout, liquid name not already held, until N=15-20 concurrent names are filled; equal collateral weight. Skip any name whose chain shows IV<RV (no premium) or fails the 8%-of-mid spread gate. Re-rank daily; do not force turnover (only fill open slots).
spec.exitRule: Close at 50% of max profit captured, OR at 21 DTE (the documented 21-DTE management rule that cuts gamma/assignment risk), OR immediately if an earnings date moves inside the position's remaining life (event de-risk), whichever first. On a >2-sigma adverse gap, hold to management (defined max loss already capped at strike) rather than panic-close into a wide post-gap spread.
spec.backtestMethod: Reuses the existing options-sim.ts engine (simulateUnderlying/runOptionsSleeve), which BS-prices each CSP from the REAL underlying OHLCV path with IV = trailing RV20 + a conservative VRP spread (vrpVolPoints≈0.035) and MARKS DAILY at sigma_mark = max(0.05, RV_now + VRP) so a realized-vol spike RAISES the short's mark and the short-gamma drawdown shows up honestly (deliberately pessimistic on the tail). The NOVEL part to add: replace the static equal-weight basket with the daily jump-adjusted cross-sectional rank (compute RV20, IV-proxy=RV20+VRP, gap-frequency from the real path, and an earnings-blackout from a historical earnings calendar) and only include top-quintile non-blackout names each day. Costs: 6% of premium per side + $0.65/contract (wide single-name spreads, intentionally pessimistic). Limitation, stated not hidden: IV is a proxy (RV+VRP), not a real historical surface, so the engine cannot capture name-specific IV skew/earnings-IV-crush — those are ASSERT-grade and only the live chain sees them; the synthetic test validates the carry+tail shape, not the precise per-name edge.
spec.positionSizing: Sleeve gets a small slice of book (target 8-12% of NAV as the meta-allocator's vol-target allows). Per name: collateral = strike x 100; equal-weight so each of N=15-20 names is ~0.5-0.8% of NAV of DEFINED max loss (strike-to-zero, minus premium). Total sleeve max loss if every name went to zero simultaneously is capped at the sleeve allocation; realistic 1-day stress (all names gap -10%) sizes to ~1-1.5% NAV. Gross notional from puts can exceed the cash slice but the cash-secured collateral is fully reserved — no leverage on the short-vol tail.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Tracked on the live sim book daily: (a) realized sleeve P&L vs theoretical theta-decay (capture ratio); (b) rolling 60d sleeve Sharpe and its rho to the equity/momentum book; (c) a CARRY-HEALTH gauge = median realized (IV-RV) actually collected across open names. The meta-allocator (production.ts perf feedback) scales the sleeve DOWN when: carry-health turns negative (IV<=RV regime — VRP compressed/inverted, the Aug-2024 failure mode), OR the VIX term structure inverts (backwardation, reused from tailhedge.ts) which historically front-runs short-vol crashes, OR rolling drawdown breaches a band. It scales UP toward the cap when carry-health is wide and stable and term structure is in contango. Hard kill-switch: no new entries while VIX term structure is inverted.
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH for compute + signal: RV20 from free OHLCV, ATM IV + bid/ask/OI from the live yahoo-finance2 chain already fetched by the incumbent app, earnings dates from Finnhub (already integrated). Fills: 16-delta 45-DTE puts on liquid large/mid optionable names quote tight enough; the 8%-of-mid spread gate enforces this and rejects the illiquid tail. REALITIES handled: (1) assignment — cash-secured so assignment is funded and the name was pre-screened ownable; (2) early exercise — rare on OTM puts pre-ex-div, the 21-DTE close avoids the high-gamma zone; (3) earnings — blackout filter excludes names reporting inside the position life. One CSP per name per ~3 weeks = low turnover, executable in the daily cron.
