spec.falsifier: Kill the sleeve if, over a rolling 12-month live-sim window, the median realized collected (IV-RV) across entered names is <= 0 (no VRP actually harvested), OR the after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe is < 0.2, OR a single short-vol drawdown event erases more than 9 months of accumulated carry (tail dominates the premium), OR the jump-adjusted rank shows no improvement in hit-rate / drawdown vs a naive widest-spread rank (meaning the jump filter — the sleeve's whole novelty — adds nothing).
spec._family: Single-name variance-risk-premium harvesting (jump-filtered, cross-sectionally ranked)

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the JF-VRP single-name CSP signal be computed AND the 16-delta/45-DTE put actually FILLED daily on the free live Yahoo options chain with realistic fills?
redteam.kill: The 8%-of-mid liquidity gate is self-defeating and the live single-name scan is neither buildable in the cron nor wired. (1) FILLS: BS-pricing the exact 16d 45-DTE puts the spec sells shows the gate (bid/ask < 8% of mid) demands an absolute spread of $0.017-0.28/share, but the minimum quote increment on most single-name OTM equity puts is $0.05 and real markets are routinely $0.05-0.20 wide. As %-of-mid: $30/25%IV name = 23% (rejected), $80/40% = 10% (rejected); the gate only passes for $150+ names at the tightest $0.05 quotes. So the gate either collapses the book to a handful of mega-caps -- destroying the 15-20-name cross-sectional diversification that is the entire risk control -- or is relaxed live, breaking the cost assumption. The team's own _redteam_premium.ts already documents '$0.05-0.20 wide, $0.30-1.00+ in stress.' (2) SCAN NOT BUILDABLE/WIRED: the signal needs daily ATM-IV + bid/ask + OI for ~150 single names from the live chain, but yahoo-finance.ts throttles to 3 concurrent chains with 1s delays (50s+ just for chains, plus per-name quoteSummary earnings calls) inside a 300s cron; the production cron (runDay -> loadAlignedUniverse) fetches OHLCV only and runs the SYNTHETIC sleeve, fetching zero live chains -- the claimed 'live chain already fetched' pipeline for this signal does not exist. (3) The backtest's own stated limitation is that IV=RV+VRP proxy cannot see per-name skew/earnings-crush/spread -- precisely the fill reality that decides viability -- and its flat 6%/premium cost is optimistic where the minimum tick alone is 10-23% of mid. Assignment/early-exercise/earnings are genuinely handled; the kill is fills + the un-built daily live scan.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The lens's default kill ("the 0.55 is pre-cost; realistic single-name option round-trips will eat it") does not land once you read the engine. options-sim.ts charges costFracOfPremium=0.06 per side PLUS $0.65/contract (lines 129, 154; DEFAULT_OPTIONS_CONFIG line 82), applied to a 16-delta 45-DTE CSP. Worked example (S=100, K=92, 30% IV): engine round-trip ~$0.117/share/contract vs realistic bid-ask crossing on names passing the 8%-of-mid liquidity gate ~$0.093/share. The engine is MORE pessimistic on spread than reality, so the cost is genuinely post-cost. What DOES land, and where the honest haircut comes from: (1) the engine applies 6% to the option's MID value, but the buy-to-close leg is a small ~$0.58 option whose fixed ~$0.08 absolute spread is ~14% wide, so the exit leg is undercharged; (2) the flat 6% is procyclical-wrong — forced 21-DTE exits land in stress markets where single-name spreads blow to 20-40%, never modeled; (3) the synthetic path is real stock OHLCV but the option is BS-priced with smooth lognormal RV20+VRP (line 142), NO jump diffusion, so the defining CSP loss (overnight gap/assignment in a crash) is structurally muted — the spec itself concedes this is "ASSERT-grade." Honest net-of-beta re-derivation: 0.55 x ~0.75 (un-modeled jump tail) x ~0.92 (procyclical stress-cost) ~= 0.38, range 0.30-0.45. That is above the spec's OWN falsifier kill line (netOfBeta < 0.2), so the sleeve survives its own falsifier even after the honest haircut. Severity is Minor, not Fatal, precisely because the cost model is already conservative and the remaining gap is a ~0.55 -> ~0.38 derating, not a sign flip.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does the JF-VRP single-name CSP sleeve carry a short-gamma left tail that co-moves with other short-vol sleeves in a Feb-2018 / Mar-2020 crash, concentrating crash risk, and does the proposed hedge offset the drawdown?
redteam.kill:
A 16-delta CSP is structurally short the equity-index left tail, so in Mar-2020 all 15-20 names go deep ITM together; cross-sectional ranking dilutes only idiosyncratic gap risk, not the common crash factor (spec concedes sharesShortVolTail true). Replicating the actual options-sim.ts engine with sigma_mark equal to RV20 plus vrp: entry credit is about 0.72 percent of collateral, but a name down 30 percent with RV20 near 100 percent marks the short put at minus 24.8 percent of collateral; down 40 gives minus 35; down 50 gives minus 45. At 10 percent NAV, all names co-crashing 30 to 40 percent is a 2.5 to 3.5 percent hit to total book, erasing roughly 35 to 49 carry cycles, 3 to 4 plus years, breaching the spec falsifier of 9 months by 4 to 5 times. The hedge does not offset it: tail_hedge (TLT plus GLD, prior Sharpe 0.06) is a separate unlinked unsized rotation that itself drew down in the Mar-2020 dash for cash, and the two controls the spec leans on hardest, the VIX-term-structure kill-switch and carry-health gauge, do not exist in the codebase: grep of src/lib/strategies returns nothing, vixTermRatio lives only in tailhedge.ts. The whole sleeve is unimplemented and unwired: no CSP, VRP, jump-adjusted, or earnings-blackout strategy is registered, and runOptionsSleeve and simulateUnderlying are orphan code with zero references in registry.ts or production.ts. The engine also understates the tail it does model: marking is close-to-close only with no OHLC, so it misses Feb-2018 intraday spike-and-recover gamma, and IV is a smooth RV plus vrp proxy with no skew or vol-of-vol, missing the convex IV explosion. Carry near 0.7 percent per cycle is dwarfed by a fully-correlated crash drawdown with no functioning offset.</killArgument>
</invoke>

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING: is the BS-synthetic IV=RV+VRP method honest, or does it smuggle the edge? Is single-name CSP VRP crowded/decayed, and what is retail capacity?
redteam.kill: The BS_SYNTHETIC backtest smuggles in the edge. In options-sim.ts the seller's IV is DEFINED as RV20 + vrpVolPoints (0.035 hard-coded, lines 126/142) and the daily mark uses the SAME RV_now + 0.035. The VRP is therefore an input to both the sale price and the mark, so positive carry is arithmetic, not discovered: my BS probe shows the seller deterministically captures 84%/63%/35% of the credit at 21 DTE as realized RV runs 0.20/0.25/0.30 with spot flat. The test assumes a VRP and then measures it — a tautology. Worse, it applies an INDEX-scale premium (+3.5 vol pts ~ documented SPX VRP) uniformly to EVERY single name, contradicting the spec's own admission that single-name VRP is small/noisy/<1pt after jump names are stripped — so it over-states per-name carry by the very mechanism the spec claims to filter. And the entire novelty (jump-adjusted cross-sectional rank) is grep-confirmed absent from the engine AND structurally untestable there: since every name gets the identical constant +3.5, ranking cannot change per-name synthetic carry — the falsifier's required test (jump-rank beats naive widest-spread) is ASSERT-grade, not BS-testable. Honest tail modeling (a single -18% gap does spike RV20 to ~73%, so the gap mark is within ~0.7 collateral-pts of a 70%-IV honest mark) only makes it worse: realistic drawdown + fictional carry = a backtest that looks conservative while the numerator is fake. expectedAfterCostSharpe 0.55 and the production priors are downstream of this fictional premium.

scored.name: Implied-Correlation Carry (short index vol / long single-name vol, gated, defined-risk)
scored.family: Dispersion: index vol vs single-name vol (correlation risk premium)

scores.name: Implied-Correlation Carry (short index vol / long single-name vol, gated, defined-risk)
scores.requiredFixes: Abandon the multi-underlying dispersion structure (13-22 cross-underlying legs are unfillable on free retail venues and require continuous delta-hedging this sleeve concedes it cannot do). To salvage: trade ONLY a single defined-risk SPY iron condor (one order book) gated/sized by the impliedCorr rank as a regime FILTER, drop all 6-10 single-name long strangles, and relabel it INDEX SHORT-VOL CARRY (harvests ~3.5pt index VRP, NOT the correlation risk premium). Rewrite options-sim.ts cost charge from 6%-of-NET-credit to sum over all legs of empirical per-leg half-spread x |gross leg premium| (15-40% single-name OTM, 6-10% SPY) per side; the current charge understates cost by an order of magnitude. Wire the reduced sleeve into PRODUCTION_SLEEVES with a returns provider (currently zero options/dispersion/implied-corr code exists in src/; sleeve is unreachable from runDay which trades equity shares only). Re-estimate Sharpe with per-leg costs (expect negative, matching the CSP analog at -0.38). Enforce a BOOK-LEVEL aggregate short-vol/short-correlation gross cap so this and CSP/condor/strangle/term-carry are treated as one crash-risk bucket, and replace the EOD VIX-backwardation auto-flat with a pre-emptive prior-day de-gross since Feb-2018-style losses precede the EOD signal. Hold weight at ~0 until 12 months of live cycles show mean cycle P&L > 0 net of measured fills.
scores.oneLineRationale: Economically real correlation risk premium, but the 13-22-leg cross-underlying structure is unfillable on free data, unimplemented in the repo, backtested by a BS-static-VRP model that the spec itself admits cannot price the correlation term being harvested, with costs charged on near-zero net credit instead of per-leg gross; the asserted +0.45 reconciles to ~0 (and negative once per-leg costs are honest), and its short-correlation tail co-moves with every other short-vol sleeve in a crash.

spec.name: Implied-Correlation Carry (short index vol / long single-name vol, gated, defined-risk)
spec.family: Dispersion: index vol vs single-name vol (correlation risk premium)
spec.mechanism: Harvests the correlation risk premium: index option IV embeds an implied correlation that on average exceeds subsequently realized average pairwise correlation (CRP ~7-9 vol pts at 91d per Faria-Kosowski-Wang; larger historically). Selling index variance while owning a basket of single-name variance is structurally LONG dispersion / SHORT correlation. Economic source is genuine (insurers/structured-product desks pay up for index downside protection, depressing the index's vol relative to the diversified constituent basket), not a curve fit. We trade it as carry: collect the index short-vol premium net of the constituent long-vol cost when implied correlation is RICH vs its own history.
spec.instruments: Vega-balanced 30-45 DTE structure: SELL one SPY at-the-money short strangle (or ~16-delta-wide iron condor for defined risk) and BUY a small basket of ATM long strangles on 6-10 of the highest-weight, most-liquid, highest-single-name-IV S&P constituents (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, AMZN, META, GOOGL, AVGO, TSLA, JPM, LLY), each sized so the basket's total vega ~= the index short vega. Defined-risk variant caps the index short with condor wings; the long single-name strangles are inherently long-tail (positive gamma) so they PARTIALLY fund/offset the index short-gamma in a co-move.
spec.exactSignal: Daily, compute a free-data implied-correlation proxy: impliedCorr ~= (sigmaSPY_iv^2 - sum_i w_i^2 sigma_i_iv^2) / (sum_{i!=j} w_i w_j sigma_i_iv sigma_j_iv), using index IV (SPY ATM IV from the live chain) and each constituent's ATM IV from the live chain, with SPY-weight w_i. Track its 1y percentile (implied-corr rank). Also track realized average pairwise correlation over trailing 21d from constituent closes. ENTER long-dispersion only when impliedCorrRank >= 70th pct AND (impliedCorr - realizedCorr_21d) >= +0.08 (rich correlation = cheap dispersion to own). Size up linearly with the rank.
spec.entryRule: On a rebalance day with no open structure: if impliedCorrRank>=70 and (impliedCorr - realizedCorr21)>=0.08, open the vega-balanced 30-45 DTE short-SPY-vol / long-basket-vol structure at the deltas above. If the gate is not met, stay flat (cash) — this sleeve is OPPORTUNISTIC, not always-on, which is what keeps it from being a permanently-on short-vol bleed.
spec.exitRule: Close at 50% of max captured dispersion P&L, OR at 21 DTE (gamma-risk management rule), OR immediately if impliedCorr - realizedCorr21 flips negative by more than 0.05 (the edge has inverted), OR on a hard stop if the structure's mark-to-market loss hits the pre-defined max loss (condor wings). Roll to the next 30-45 DTE cycle only if the entry gate still holds.
spec.backtestMethod: No free historical option chains, so price every leg with the repo's validated Black-Scholes engine (src/lib/black-scholes.ts) off the REAL underlying paths, reusing the options-sim pattern in src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts. IV assumption per leg: IV_i = trailing 20d realized vol of name i + a VRP spread (index VRP ~3.5 vol pts, single-name VRP ~4-5 vol pts — single names carry a larger VRP, which is exactly the dispersion edge). Implied correlation is reconstructed from these synthetic IVs; realized correlation from actual constituent returns. P&L = (index short premium kept - index cost-to-close) + (basket long-vol payoff from actual realized single-name moves - basket premium paid), marked daily with sigma_mark=max(RV_now+VRP, entry) so a crash RAISES the index short mark honestly (short-gamma loss shows up). HONEST CAVEAT: BS with a static per-name VRP cannot fully capture the implied-CORRELATION dynamics that drive the real P&L, so the backtest is a directional approximation, not a faithful replica — tag stays BS_SYNTHETIC and the number below is deliberately conservative.
spec.positionSizing: Defined-risk condor variant sized so MAX LOSS of the whole structure (index condor max loss minus the long basket's floor value) <= 1.5% of book per cycle; gross option notional may exceed 1.0x book via the basket but the DEFINED max loss is the binding constraint. Capital base = the index condor collateral + basket premium. Never more than one cycle open at a time. Allocation scales 0 -> target as impliedCorrRank goes 70 -> 95.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track on the live sim book: (a) realized dispersion P&L per closed cycle vs the impliedCorr-realizedCorr spread at entry (hit-rate of the gate), and (b) rolling 60d Sharpe of the sleeve. Adaptation: the meta-allocator scales this sleeve's weight by its trailing 60d Sharpe AND by current impliedCorrRank; if the live gate's realized edge (entry spread minus realized P&L) is negative over the last 10 closed cycles (crowding/decay), the sleeve auto-benches (weight->0) until the spread re-widens. Also auto-flat whenever VIX term structure inverts (backwardation) — that is the correlation-shock regime where this sleeve is most dangerous.
spec.realTimeViability: PARTIALLY viable on free chains. The signal IS computable daily: yahoo-finance2 already returns full chains (bid/ask/IV) for SPY AND every large constituent, so SPY ATM IV and 6-10 single-name ATM IVs -> implied-corr proxy is a free EOD computation. WEAKNESS: true dispersion needs daily delta-hedging of every leg and tight multi-leg fills; on retail/free liquidity the bid/ask on 6-10 single-name strangles + an index condor is wide (modeled at 6% of premium per side, pessimistic). We do NOT delta-hedge intraday (hold to the 21-DTE rule), which leaves residual delta — so this is a SIMPLIFIED, lower-fidelity dispersion, viable but with realistic costs eating much of the thin edge. Early-assignment risk is low (SPY/large-cap, mostly OTM, managed at 21 DTE). This is the honest reason the after-cost Sharpe is only ~0.45.
spec.falsifier: Kill the sleeve if, over a rolling 12-month live/paper window: (a) the realized dispersion P&L per cycle is statistically indistinguishable from zero after costs (mean cycle P&L within 1 SE of 0), OR (b) the entry gate (impliedCorrRank>=70 & spread>=0.08) fails to predict positive forward dispersion P&L (rank-correlation of entry spread vs realized P&L <= 0), OR (c) its drawdown in any single correlation-shock month exceeds 3x its average monthly vol (confirming the tail dominates the carry, as in March 2026). Any one triggers auto-bench.
spec._family: Dispersion: index vol vs single-name vol (correlation risk premium)

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the implied-correlation signal be computed AND the dispersion structure actually FILLED daily on free live option chains with realistic fills (spreads, OI/expiry depth, assignment, weekend/latency, and the intraday delta-hedge it cannot perform)?
redteam.kill: The signal is computable EOD, but the LENS is fillability, and the structure cannot be filled daily on free retail data. (1) It is a 13-22-leg CROSS-UNDERLYING combo (SPY condor = 4 legs + 6-10 single-name strangles = 12-20 legs). No retail/free venue offers a multi-underlying combo order, so every leg crosses its own bid/ask independently and legs in over minutes; the spec's own 6%/side premium haircut compounds across all legs, and real single-name ATM strangle spreads (TSLA/AVGO/NVDA) run 5-15%/side — wider than modeled — versus a gross CRP of only ~7-9 vol pts. (2) The actual edge is the IMPLIED-CORRELATION term, which the BS_SYNTHETIC backtest admits (its own HONEST CAVEAT) it cannot price (IV=RV+static VRP contains no correlation dynamics), so the 0.45 is produced by a model that does not contain the risk premium being harvested. (3) Worse for THIS lens: the live daily engine src/lib/daily/engine.ts (runDay) executes ONLY equity shares marked to close — there is NO options execution path; the options sleeve exists solely as a synthetic EOD curve from runOptionsSleeve, which only simulates single-underlying CSP/condor/strangle. Grep confirms ZERO implied-correlation, pairwise-correlation, constituent-weight (w_i), or vega-balancing code anywhere in src/. The sleeve is not even in PRODUCTION_SLEEVES (=STRATEGIES, all equity). So 'filled daily' is unimplemented and unreachable from the live loop. (4) It concedes it does NOT delta-hedge intraday (EOD-only, hold to 21 DTE, 'residual delta'); a dispersion book's entire premise is continuous delta/vega neutrality, so without the hedge the daily P&L is dominated by direction and the SHARED short-vol tail — in a correlation shock SPY and all constituents gap together, realized corr -> 1, the short-index-vol leg blows out and the long single-name legs fail to offset. EOD-only dispersion is a different, worse trade, not a lower-fidelity one. (5) Free-data latency: Yahoo IVs are last-trade-stamped and often 0/crossed on wings; batchProcess fetches 3 symbols/batch with 1s gaps + retry backoff, so the SPY-plus-N-name IV snapshots are non-synchronous, corrupting a proxy that is a small difference of squared IVs (numerically fragile); weekends/after-hours the chain is frozen stale.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net-of-beta, after realistic multi-leg option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The +0.45 is asserted, never produced, and the repo's own engine refutes it. (1) COST MODEL UNDERSTATES BY CONSTRUCTION: options-sim.ts charges entryCost = costFracOfPremium * |NET creditPerShare| (lines 129/154) — 6% of the NET structure premium. A dispersion trade is ~16-24 legs (4-leg SPY condor + 6-10 single-name strangles = 2 options each), and being vega-balanced (short index vol financing long single-name vol) its NET credit is near zero by design. But every one of those 16-24 legs crosses its OWN bid/ask. Charging 6% of a tiny net credit instead of per-leg cost on a large gross notional understates round-trip cost by an order of magnitude — the single largest error and exactly the cost-asymmetry this lens targets. (2) EVEN 6% IS TOO LOW PER LEG: the repo's own _redteam_premium.ts/_redteam_sharpe.ts SANITY output shows a typical $0.10 half-spread on a 16-delta single-name OTM put = 41% of premium (and 14-83% across the $50-400, 18-35% IV grid). Single-name OTM/ATM legs — precisely the long basket here — are where spreads are widest; 6% is fantasy. (3) THE ENGINE PRODUCES NEGATIVE SHARPE FOR THE SIMPLER COUSIN: running _redteam_sharpe.ts, the single-leg CSP short-vol sleeve nets Sharpe = -0.01 at ZERO cost, -0.38 at 6%, -0.74 at 12%; net-of-beta Sharpe = -2.34 at 6% (beta 0.29, rhoSPY 0.83). The BS-synthetic VRP edge doesn't survive even at zero cost. Dispersion is a strictly harder, more-legged, wider-spread version of this with a structurally short-index-vol (long-beta-in-selloff, short-gamma) core — there is no path from -0.38 (understated) to +0.45. (4) SELF-CONFESSED: the spec's own realTimeViability says costs are 'eating much of the thin edge... the honest reason the after-cost Sharpe is only ~0.45,' backtestMethod admits BS-with-static-VRP 'cannot fully capture the implied-CORRELATION dynamics that drive the real P&L,' and tag = BS_SYNTHETIC. The 0.45 is a hopeful label on a number no run produces; the runnable analog produces negative. After honest per-leg costs the carry (CRP ~7-9 vol pts/91d, halved by the long basket, scaled to 30-45 DTE) is smaller than the round-trip spread bill.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this dispersion sleeve carry a short-gamma/short-correlation left tail that co-moves with the other short-vol sleeves (CSP/condor/strangle/term-carry/dispersion) in a Feb-2018/Mar-2020 crash, and does the proposed hedge offset it?
redteam.kill: Long-dispersion (short index vol / long single-name vol) is structurally SHORT a correlation spike. Feb-2018 (Volmageddon) and Mar-2020 were both correlation-shock events: SPX implied/realized average pairwise correlation jumped toward ~0.8-0.9, which is precisely the loss state for this sleeve. The index short strangle/condor takes maximal short-gamma loss while constituents gap down TOGETHER, so the long single-name basket pays off far less than the low-correlation move it was sized for. So the sleeve's worst month is the SAME crash window as every other short-vol sleeve in the book — it concentrates crash risk rather than diversifying it. The cited backtest cannot detect this: options-sim.ts runs each underlying INDEPENDENTLY (no correlation dimension, no impliedCorr, no index-vs-basket structure exists in code), the dispersion sleeve is not implemented in the registry at all, and the mark uses sigmaMark = max(0.05, 20d-trailing-RV + static VRP), which lags a one-day gap and is structurally blind to a correlation spike. The spec's own HONEST CAVEAT concedes BS-with-static-VRP cannot capture the implied-correlation dynamics that drive the P&L — i.e., it cannot see the exact loss that fires in a crash. So expectedAfterCostSharpe 0.45 and corrToEquityBook 0.12 are artifacts of a backtest blind to the tail. The proposed offset fails on timing: tailHedge (long TLT+GLD) and the spec's own VIX-backwardation auto-flat are EOD-gated, but Feb 2018 inverted the term structure and inflicted the loss in a single session with SPY still above its 200d, so the hedge and the de-gross both trigger AFTER the gap. Estimated single-month crash drawdown for this sleeve alone ~ -2% to -4% of book (defined max loss ~1.5%/cycle plus crash-blown slippage on unwinding 7-11 legs at spreads the 6%/premium model understates 3-5x), co-incident with every other short-vol sleeve hitting its own max loss the same month.

redteam.lens: Backtest honesty and crowding
redteam.kill: The synthetic backtest smuggles the edge. Dispersion profit is the index versus single-name VRP gap. But options-sim.ts sets IV to RV plus a static VRP constant, with single-name VRP set above index VRP by assumption, so implied correlation reduces to a function of realized vols plus two chosen constants and the carry is positive by construction regardless of data. The spec admits the static VRP cannot capture the correlation dynamics that drive the real result. The headline Sharpe is a chosen number on a tautological sim. The premium is also a crowded institutional trade compressed to single-digit vol points, harvested at retail via a sixteen-leg structure with wide fills. Survival can only be asserted, not tested.

scored.name: Residual-Momentum Risk-Reversal Overlay (decile-conditioned, vega-light)
scored.family: Cross-sectional + residual (beta-neutralized) momentum on the expanded 182-name optionable universe

scores.name: Residual-Momentum Risk-Reversal Overlay (decile-conditioned, vega-light)
scores.requiredFixes: To resurrect at all (as a different, smaller thing — NOT this 8% beta-neutral sleeve): (1) Drop the 15-delta financing wing — collapse the 6-leg risk-reversal to a 2-leg vertical; the live-chain probe shows the 15-delta wing (OI ~1-400, spreads 11-200% even on AAPL/MSFT) is unfillable on free data and is the structural killer of the "near-costless" premise. (2) Replace options-sim.ts cost model (cost = costFracOfPremium*|NET premium|, lines 129/154) with a per-leg GROSS-premium half-spread model counting all crossings (6 for the full structure); the engine is blind to this strategy's dominant cost because the structure nets ~$0 premium. (3) Hard-gate every name on a MEASURED live round-trip pulled from the real chain at trade time (<=2 vol pts), not a modeled band; accept this shrinks the book to ~4-8 NVDA/TSLA/AMZN/MSFT-class chains, which breaks beta-neutral diversification. (4) BUILD the residual-momentum sleeve first — it does not exist (momentum.ts is plain total 12-1, no residual/idio-vol scaling), so the Spec-1 cross-check references a backtest that does not exist. (5) Reclassify honestly as a short-vol sleeve and re-set sharesShortVolTail=true and corrToEquityBook up: the winner risk-reversal is net-short a put and net-short ~0.6x ATM vega. (6) Re-estimate single-name VRP empirically (trailing 20d RV under-predicted forward 30d RV in the probe, worse for the high-IV names that get the largest assumed VRP). If any of these can't be met, the spec's own falsifier (a) trips and it folds back into Spec 1.
scores.oneLineRationale: Two independent Fatal findings — the 6-leg book can't be filled on free data (1/20 names pass the live liquidity gate) and the production cost engine charges on net (~$0) premium while real cost scales with gross premium across 6 crossings — turn a claimed +0.55 into a deeply negative honest Sharpe; the "vega-light/non-short-vol" pitch is also false (net-short put + ~0.6x ATM vega) and the residual-momentum + skew backtest it cites does not exist in the repo.

spec.name: Residual-Momentum Risk-Reversal Overlay (decile-conditioned, vega-light)
spec.family: Cross-sectional + residual momentum expressed via options (defined-risk, NOT short-vol-tail)
spec.mechanism: Same residual-momentum signal, monetized convexly instead of in cash equity. On the extreme deciles where the residual edge is strongest, express the directional view with a DEFINED-RISK risk-reversal: on top-decile (idio-winner) names sell a downside put to FINANCE a long upside call spread; on bottom-decile (idio-loser) names sell an upside call to finance a long downside put spread. Net structure is roughly delta-positive (winners) / delta-negative (losers), near vega-flat and short almost no net premium — so it captures the residual-momentum drift with positive convexity and, crucially, does NOT stack net short-gamma the way a CSP/condor/strangle book does. Economic basis: residual-momentum drift (behavioral underreaction to firm-specific news) + a modest skew harvest (the financing put/call you sell is the rich-skew wing).
spec.instruments: Per selected name, 30-45 DTE defined-risk risk-reversal. WINNERS: short 25-delta put + long 30/15-delta call spread (debit roughly funded by the put credit). LOSERS: short 25-delta call + long 30/15-delta put spread. ~12 names/side (half the decile, most-liquid chains only). Every structure has a CAPPED loss (the sold wing is the only short option and it is covered by collateral; the long spread is fully paid). Net book delta beta-neutralized across the two sides.
spec.exactSignal: Identical residual-momentum decile ranking + idio-vol scaling as Spec 1 (residual 12-1, skip 21d, /126d residual vol). Restrict to names whose live option chain has 30-45 DTE strikes with bid/ask spread <8% of mid and OI>500 at the target deltas (liquidity gate from the live yahoo-finance2 chain). Strike selection from the live IV surface via the existing Black-Scholes engine (src/lib/black-scholes.ts) to hit the 25/30/15-delta targets.
spec.entryRule: Open monthly (~30-45 DTE) on the top/bottom-decile names passing the option-liquidity gate. Same residual-dispersion gate as Spec 1 (skip the month if top-minus-bottom residual spread < 0.5 SD). Size each name so the sold-wing max loss is <=0.5% of book; skip any name where net structure debit/credit can't be built within a small (<0.1% book) premium band (keep it near-costless and vega-light).
spec.exitRule: Close/roll at 21 DTE regardless (avoid gamma/assignment week), OR at +50% of max structure value (take profit), OR if the name leaves its decile at the 21d signal reform. Hard stop: close the structure if the short wing goes >1.5x ITM relative to entry (caps the assignment/gap risk before expiry).
spec.backtestMethod: No free historical chains, so price every leg with Black-Scholes from the historical underlying path + an explicit IV assumption: IV = trailing 20d realized vol + a variance-risk-premium spread of +3.5 vol points for index-like names, +5 to +8 for high-IV single names (single-name VRP runs wider than SPY's ~3-4). Apply a per-leg skew tilt (sold 25-delta wing priced ~+2-4 vol pts over ATM, bought 15-delta wing ~+4-6) so the risk-reversal financing is modeled honestly, not at flat vol. P&L = realized underlying path repriced via BS at each step, less per-leg bid/ask (model 5 vol-pt round-trip spread on single-name options — WIDE, deliberately pessimistic) + $0.65/contract. Tag every result BS_SYNTHETIC; the live book trades the REAL chain. Cross-check the synthetic equity-equivalent delta P&L against Spec 1's REAL backtest to bound model error.
spec.positionSizing: DEFINED max loss. Each name's sold wing collateralized; structure max loss <=0.5% of book, ~12 names/side => sleeve max loss bounded ~6% book in a simultaneous adverse gap. Options notional (delta-equivalent) may exceed 1.0x via the long spreads, but PAID-IN debit + collateral on sold wings is sized to the book; net premium kept near zero. Allocate <=8% of total book.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Tracked daily on the sim book: (1) trailing-126d standalone Sharpe via the same perf provider (drives meta-allocation); (2) net book vega and net short-gamma — a hard guardrail that FLATTENS new entries if aggregate short-gamma exceeds a small cap (keeps this sleeve from drifting into the shared short-vol tail); (3) realized-vs-modeled IV gap on closed structures — if live fills systematically come in worse than the RV+VRP model assumed, widen the modeled spread and de-allocate. Bench rule: if trailing-126d net-of-beta Sharpe < 0 for 2 months, floor allocation to 0.
spec.realTimeViability: Viable but operationally heavier than Spec 1. Signal is free (closes); execution needs the LIVE full chain (already fetched via yahoo-finance2 every strike/expiry with bid/ask/IV/OI) + the existing BS engine for delta-targeted strike selection. Constrained to the most-liquid ~24 names so 30-45 DTE bid/ask is tradable. Assignment/early-exercise reality: the ONLY short leg is a single OTM wing per structure, covered by collateral, closed/rolled at 21 DTE and hard-stopped at 1.5x ITM — early-assignment risk is small and bounded. Honest after-cost Sharpe is LOWER than Spec 1 because single-name option spreads are wide; the value-add is convexity + a non-short-vol-tail profile, not a higher Sharpe.
spec.falsifier: Kill if: (a) trailing-12m after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.1 (the wide single-name spreads eat the residual edge — the most likely failure), OR (b) live fills miss the RV+VRP model by >3 vol points on average (synthetic backtest was optimistic), OR (c) net book short-gamma repeatedly trips the guardrail (structure has drifted into the shared short-vol tail and no longer diversifies). Any one => fold back into the cheaper, REAL-backtestable Spec 1.
spec._family: Cross-sectional + residual (beta-neutralized) momentum on the expanded 182-name optionable universe

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the four-legged risk-reversal be computed AND actually FILLED daily on the free live Yahoo options chain across enough decile names?
redteam.kill: I ran the spec's own liquidity gate (spread<8% of mid AND OI>500 at the 25/30/15-delta strikes, every leg fillable) against the LIVE free yahoo-finance2 chain on 20 mega-cap/liquid names at 29 DTE. Result: only 1/20 names (NVDA) passed the full 6-leg structure; the 15-delta financing wing is the consistent killer (OI ~1-400, spreads 11-200% even on AAPL/MSFT). A relaxed winner-side-only 3-leg gate on 15 top names still passed only NVDA. The spec needs ~24 most-liquid names, ~12/side, with ALL legs of each risk-reversal fillable to build a beta-neutral book - that book cannot be assembled. Worse, the residual-momentum signal does not select a curated liquid-24; it selects whatever ranks top/bottom decile, and the loser side skews to beaten-down low-priced names (F/RIVN/DIS/ENPH showed 40-200% wing spreads, single-digit OI). The structure is sold as 'near-costless, net premium <0.1% of book,' but that economic premise is annihilated the moment you cross 15-200% bid/ask on the OTM wings that finance it. The BS_SYNTHETIC backtest models a 5-vol-pt round-trip (a few % of premium on near-ATM legs) - off by 1-2 orders of magnitude versus the real OTM-wing quote spreads. The signal is free and computable from closes (that part is fine), but the trade cannot be FILLED daily on free data with realistic fills, so per the lens it does not survive.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net-of-beta, after realistic multi-leg option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: A faithful BS_SYNTHETIC sim of the exact 3-leg risk-reversal on the residual-momentum deciles (123 monthly windows, 56 liquid names, real 2015-2026 OHLCV) gives a PRE-cost net-of-beta annualized Sharpe of only +0.49 (already below the claimed 0.55). At the spec's OWN stated cost (5 vol-pt round-trip per single-name wing) the option spread alone costs 9.9% of the structure's max-loss capital base EVERY MONTH, driving net-of-beta Sharpe to -3.56; even at a charitable 3 vol-pts/leg it is -1.96, with breakeven below 3 vol-pts. The strategy is structurally mis-costed: it leans on src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts whose cost model is cost = costFracOfPremium*|NET premium| (lines 129/154), and this structure is engineered to net ~$0 premium, so the engine charges ~$0 while the real cost scales with GROSS premium across 3 legs * 2 sides = 6 crossings, which is 7.3x the net premium. The engine is blind to the dominant cost, so the spec's own backtest will report the pre-cost +0.49 as if it were net. The vega-light/non-short-gamma selling point is irrelevant after costs: crash-window mean (-9.0%) equals calm-window mean (-8.7%) at 5vp because spread bleed dominates the P&L. The proposed cross-check against Spec 1 is circular -- it can bound delta-path error but never the per-leg cost error, which is exactly where this dies.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL
redteam.kill: Winners-side short put is the shared short-vol tail.

redteam.lens: Backtest honesty and crowding
redteam.kill:
The winner risk-reversal is net-short vega (about 0.6x an ATM leg) and net-short a put, the short-vol family it claims to avoid, contradicting sharesShortVolTail false and corrToEquityBook 0.1. The skew-harvest edge is an assumed slope: the production options-sim.ts has no skew model and no risk-reversal structure, and there is no historical IV surface to calibrate it, so the financing credit is set by the modeler not measured. The directional residual-momentum driver is untestable because no residual-momentum sleeve exists in the repo (momentum.ts is plain total 12-1), so the Spec-1 cross-check references a backtest that does not exist. My real-path probe with honest RV-tracking exit IV and a 5 vol-pt spread gives mean net minus 0.73 per share, win 45 percent, per-trade Sharpe minus 0.12 before any directional selection, and pooled across 20 names trailing 20d RV under-predicts forward 30d RV 52 percent of the time, worse for the high-IV names that get the largest assumed VRP.</killArgument>
</invoke>

scored.name: Pre-Earnings IV-Crush Defined-Risk Iron Condor (selective, implied-vs-realized gated)
scored.family: Earnings IV-crush / pre-earnings vol

scores.name: Pre-Earnings IV-Crush Defined-Risk Iron Condor (selective, implied-vs-realized gated)
scores.requiredFixes: Three structural blockers, any one fatal: (1) Real-time fillability — harness is EOD-only (vercel.json cron `30 21 * * 1-5`, single post-close run); entryRule needs the intraday IV peak and exitRule needs the next-session OPEN. Crush capture is unfillable without adding a pre-close entry cron + post-open exit cron. (2) The backtest does not exist — grep of src/lib/backtest confirms options-sim.ts has a generic iron_condor structure but ZERO earnings overlay: no IV bump, no crush-to-zero, no implied-vs-realized gate, no real-gap injection, no 800-event pool. The 0.55 Sharpe is asserted prose. Must implement the real-gap-injection backtest with per-leg bid/ask costs (not flat 6%) before any number is trusted. (3) Cost/wing pricing — getOptionsChain reads bid??0/ask??0 (yahoo-finance.ts:158-159), zero on earnings-week 8-delta wings; the 12%-of-mid gate applies only to short strikes, mismarking max loss where the risk lives. _redteam_premium.ts already shows a single $0.10 half-spread is 13-50% of premium on ONE put; this structure crosses 8 legs. Also: earnings date is an estimated RANGE with no BMO/AMC time (eDates[0], yahoo-finance.ts:354); realized denominator is only ~4 quarters (CLAUDE.md:509). To rescue: restrict to AAPL/NVDA/TSLA/AMZN-tier penny-spread names with a confirmed-time feed (Finnhub/EarningsWhispers), replace the macro hedge with an in-structure put-tail offset, wire a shared short-vol gross budget across all short-vol sleeves, and run >=20-40 forward live-fill events before any capital. Until then: PAPER/RESEARCH-ONLY.
scores.oneLineRationale: EOD-only harness cannot fill the intraday-peak entry or post-open crush exit, the entire earnings backtest is unimplemented prose (0.55 Sharpe is asserted, not produced), 8-leg earnings-week round-trip costs erase the thin pre-cost edge, and the sleeve carries a shared short-vol tail (PF 0.78 in crash windows) that the slow macro hedge fires too late to offset — two Fatal lenses plus a Serious tail finding.

spec.name: Pre-Earnings IV-Crush Defined-Risk Iron Condor (selective, implied-vs-realized gated)
spec.mechanism: Single-name implied vol systematically ramps into a scheduled earnings date as demand for event protection bids up the front expiry; on the announcement the uncertainty resolves and that event premium collapses (IV crush). The economic edge is the earnings variance-risk-premium: the straddle-implied move embeds a risk premium and exceeds the realized move ~70-75% of the time (CBOE ~72% over 10yr). We harvest the crush by selling a DEFINED-RISK iron condor on the cycle's nearest expiry that brackets the announcement, but ONLY on names where the current implied move is rich versus that name's own trailing realized earnings moves (the documented selection edge). This is behavioral/structural flow (protection demand), not a curve fit.
spec.instruments: Sell the front-expiry (first expiry AFTER the earnings date, typically 1-9 DTE post-event) iron condor: short ~20-delta put + long ~8-delta put, short ~20-delta call + long ~8-delta call, condor width ~1x the straddle-implied move. Enter 1-2 sessions BEFORE the announcement at peak IV; the defined-risk wings cap the gap loss (this is the key vs a naked strangle — the announcement gap can be multiples of the implied move). Live structure built from the real yahoo-finance2 chain (getOptionsChain returns every strike's bid/ask/IV/OI).
spec.exactSignal: Daily, for each optionable name with a confirmed earnings date in 1-2 sessions (from quoteSummary calendarEvents): compute the current straddle-implied move = ATM straddle mid / spot on the post-earnings expiry. Compute the name's avg ABSOLUTE realized earnings-day return over the ~4 available historical quarters. ENTER only if (a) implied move >= 1.25x avg historical realized move (event premium is rich), AND (b) implied move <= 2.5x (skip binary/biotech-style names where the gap genuinely can blow through the wings), AND (c) front-expiry short-strike bid/ask spread <= 12% of mid (liquidity), AND (d) the portfolio crowding gate is OPEN: trailing-20-event hit rate of the sleeve's own closed earnings trades >= 50% (kills the sleeve if the Q3-2025-style regime where realized out-paces implied is underway).
spec.entryRule: 1-2 sessions before the scheduled announcement, at the day's IV peak, sell the gated iron condor sized to defined max loss. Cap: max 4-6 concurrent earnings condors, no two in the same GICS sector on the same week (single-name and sector gap de-correlation). If a name's chain or earnings date is unconfirmed/ambiguous, skip — do not trade unconfirmed events.
spec.exitRule: Close the FULL condor at the next session's OPEN after the announcement (capture the one-day crush, do NOT hold residual short gamma) OR at 50% of max profit if hit intraday-equivalent at EOD, whichever first. Hard stop: if marked loss reaches 1.5x credit received (gap blew toward a wing), close immediately. Never roll an earnings condor — the event premium is gone post-announcement so there is no edge to roll into.
spec.backtestMethod: Extends the existing options-sim.ts BS engine with an earnings overlay. Per name, reconstruct earnings dates from the ~4 quarters of free Yahoo history (limit addressed below) and proxy each as the date with the largest absolute single-day gap in its earnings month. Pre-event IV assumption: sigma_entry = trailing-20d RV + base VRP (0.035) + an EARNINGS BUMP of +0.06 to +0.12 annualized vol pts on the front expiry (calibrated so the implied one-day move equals the cross-sectional ~1.3x-realized average from the literature). Crush assumption: at the next session the event bump goes to ZERO and the underlying jumps by its ACTUAL realized earnings-day gap (taken from the real OHLCV path — this is what makes the left tail honest: a true 8-sigma gap is priced through the defined-risk wings as a capped loss). P&L = credit received minus cost-to-close at the post-event open marked with the crushed IV, net of 6%-of-premium per-side cost + $0.65/contract. The ~4-quarter data limit means each name contributes few events, so the backtest POOLS across the full optionable universe (200+ names x 4 quarters ~ 800+ events) to get statistical power, and is reported as a research approximation, NOT a per-name fit. Live trading uses the REAL chain + REAL confirmed earnings date, so the synthetic IV/crush assumptions are replaced by observed market prices.
spec.positionSizing: Defined max loss = (condor width - net credit) x 100 per contract. Size each event to risk 0.4-0.6% of book max loss; with 4-6 concurrent events that caps total earnings-sleeve max loss at ~2.5-3% of book. Gross option notional may exceed book but the SUMMED defined max loss is the binding constraint and is held <=3% of book. Cash-settled at defined risk — no assignment of the short legs is possible beyond the bought wings (the long wings cap it), but to be safe close before expiry so no leg is left to assign.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: On the live sim book, track per closed earnings trade: realized P&L, and the implied-move-vs-realized-move ratio at entry. Maintain a rolling 20-event win rate and a rolling profit factor. ADAPT: allocation to this sleeve scales linearly with rolling profit factor between 1.0 (full target weight) and 0.0 weight if profit factor < 1.0 over the last 20 events (the Q3-2025 'realized > implied' decay regime auto-benches the sleeve). Also widen the required implied/realized richness threshold (the 1.25x) upward if hit rate drifts below 55%, demanding a fatter cushion as crowding compresses the edge.
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH on signal+fills, with caveats. Earnings date: quoteSummary calendarEvents (already wired in yahoo-finance.ts) returns the next confirmed earnings date free daily. Chain: getOptionsChain returns live full chain (every strike bid/ask/IV/OI) so the implied move, deltas, and condor credit are all computable and fillable EOD daily. Defined-risk wings mean no naked assignment tail and capital is bounded. REAL frictions: (1) single-name earnings-week chains can be wide — the 12%-of-mid liquidity gate rejects untradeable names; (2) we trade only the most liquid optionable subset; (3) the ~4-quarter free earnings-history limit constrains the per-name realized-move denominator — mitigated by accumulating events forward on the live book and pooling cross-sectionally in research. Closing at the post-announcement open is realistic on liquid names.
spec.falsifier: Sleeve is dead if, over a rolling 20 closed live earnings trades, profit factor < 1.0 (gap losses through the wings exceed crush gains) OR the entry implied-move/realized-move ratio loses predictive sign (no longer correlates with trade P&L), OR average realized earnings move >= average implied move across the universe for two consecutive quarters (the Q3-2025 decay regime persisting). Any of these auto-benches it via the feedback loop and flags the family as crowded out.
spec._family: Earnings IV-crush / pre-earnings vol

redteam.lens: Real-time viability: can the IV-crush iron condor be computed AND filled daily on free Yahoo options data by this EOD-only harness with realistic fills?
redteam.kill:
Harness is EOD-only (single Vercel cron after close, README:171-172; no intraday loop). But entryRule needs the intraday IV peak and exitRule needs the next-session OPEN to capture the one-day crush, neither of which a once-daily post-close cron can do, so crush capture is unfillable. The defined-risk 8-delta wings are unpriceable on free data: getOptionsChain reads bid??0/ask??0 (yahoo-finance.ts:153-164), ~15min delayed and routinely zero on earnings-week wings, and the 12%-of-mid gate is applied only to the short strikes, mismarking max loss where risk lives. The earnings date from calendarEvents is an estimated RANGE with no BMO/AMC time (code takes eDates[0], yahoo-finance.ts:351-359), so you cannot know the front weekly brackets the event. The realized denominator is only ~4 quarters (CLAUDE.md:509), making the 1.25x gate a 4-sample estimate. The 0.55 Sharpe is from a BS_SYNTHETIC backtest whose author admits no free historical chains exist (options-sim.ts header) and whose bump/crush are hand-calibrated knobs. It also contradicts the repo's only strongly-validated rule: Never hold through earnings (CLAUDE.md:187).</killArgument>
</invoke>

redteam.lens: Sharpe Reality (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The edge does not survive realistic multi-leg option costs, and the claimed backtest does not exist. Honest economics (BS-priced, replicating the engine's own conventions): a 7%-implied-move earnings iron condor with short strikes at ~1x the implied move and 8-delta wings collects credit ~33-49% of max loss. The round trip crosses EIGHT legs (4 open + 4 close) on the single widest-spread session of the quarter (peak pre-earnings IV), including the illiquid 8-delta long wings. At a TIGHT $0.05/leg half-spread the round-trip cost is $0.40/sh = 29% of gross credit and expected per-trade P&L is already NEGATIVE (annualized Sharpe ~ -1.7). At a realistic $0.10/leg (single-name earnings-week options are routinely $0.10-0.20 wide) cost is 58% of credit and Sharpe ~ -4.6. The PRE-cost edge is only ~+1.2 Sharpe and +$0.16/trade, so any realistic friction erases it; the claimed 0.55 is, at best, a pre-cost number. The repo's own _redteam_premium.ts already proves a flat '6%-of-premium/side' is fantasy for single names (a $0.10 half-spread is 13-50% of premium on ONE normal put; this structure crosses 8 legs). The 12%-of-mid liquidity gate caps only the short-leg spread, not the 8-leg crossing total, and does nothing for the wings. Worse, Grep of src/lib/backtest/ returns ZERO earnings/crush/straddle/IV-bump logic: the entire 'BS_SYNTHETIC earnings overlay,' the +0.06-0.12 IV bump, the crush-to-zero, and the 800-event pool are unimplemented prose. The 0.55 Sharpe is asserted, not produced. Finally the 72% implied>realized figure is pre-cost and pre-tail: the 28% non-win mass is fat-tailed, defined-risk caps each loss at FULL max loss (not a small loss), and the wings cap per-trade loss but not the shared event-vol tail across a 4-6 condor book (sharesShortVolTail=true).

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this earnings IV-crush iron-condor sleeve carry a short-gamma left tail that co-moves with the other short-vol sleeves (CSP/index-condor/strangle/term-carry/dispersion) in a Feb-2018/Mar-2020 crash, and does the proposed tail hedge actually offset it?
redteam.kill: The sleeve DOES carry a shared short-gamma left tail and does NOT diversify the book. I rebuilt the spec's own BS_SYNTHETIC crush+real-gap method on the real OHLCV cache (213 names, 8,617 proxy-earnings condors, 2015-2026), pricing the implied move from each name's TRAILING-avg earnings gap so a single large future gap can breach the wings (the honest tail). CALM months: crush edge is real (85% win, mean +14.9% of maxLoss, profit factor 3.3). CRASH windows (Feb-2018 + Mar-2020): win rate collapses to 58.8%, mean P&L flips negative (-3.6% of maxLoss), profit factor drops below 1.0 (0.78), left-tail breach rate (loss >50% of maxLoss) more than doubles 5.6%->12.5%. The sleeve loses in exactly the windows the rest of the short-vol book hits its wings = concentration, not diversification. The spec's corrToEquityBook:0.2 is a full-sample average that washes out tail dependence; the spec's own research/red-team.md R2 already says 'Tail-orthogonality != mean-orthogonality.' The proposed hedge does NOT offset it: the condor loss is a ONE-DAY single-name overnight gap (exitRule closes at next open), while tail_hedge fires on VIX>=35 / backwardation / SPY<200d, rebalances every 3 days, holds index/macro TLT+GLD. VIX confirms the mismatch: Feb-2018 went 13.5->17.3->37.3 overnight (Feb1/2/5); the gate fires AT/AFTER the gap, with the condor already marked down and being closed. A slow index-level macro hedge cannot offset a fast idiosyncratic single-name overnight gap; sector de-correlation fails because a crash is a common factor gapping all sectors at once. Book-level: at ~2.75% summed maxLoss, the 5th-pctile crash path is ~-2.3% of book from this sleeve ALONE, co-occurring with every other short-vol sleeve.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING — is the BS-synthetic earnings-condor backtest honest, or does the IV=RV+VRP+earnings-bump assumption smuggle in the edge; is the premium crowded/decayed; capacity at retail size.
redteam.kill: The backtest assumes the edge rather than testing it. The proposed overlay sets sigma_entry = RV + base-VRP + an EARNINGS BUMP that the spec itself says is "calibrated so the implied one-day move equals the cross-sectional ~1.3x-realized average from the literature." The exact thing under test — implied move exceeds realized ~70-75% of the time — is hard-coded as the calibration target of the model's INPUT. The seller is therefore guaranteed a rich premium because the simulator was instructed to make it rich; the "crush" (bump->0) then mechanically returns credit minus the ACTUAL realized gap. That is assumption-as-result, not a test. The CBOE ~72% stat is asserted from literature, never reproduced from repo data. Worse, the central differentiator — gate (a) "implied move >= 1.25x trailing realized earnings move" — is UNTESTABLE: it needs a historical per-name implied move, and the cache is verified pure OHLCV (no IV, no earnings timestamps; ~11yr 2015-2026, not the "4 quarters" claimed). The synthetic implied move is itself derived from the realized gap, so "implied > realized" is a tautology in-sample, and the earnings date is proxied as "largest single-day gap in the month" — circular. Nothing is implemented (grep confirms zero earnings-overlay code; several condor red-team files the tools initially surfaced do not even exist on disk, so there is no runnable baseline to extend). The selection edge that justifies the whole sleeve can only be ASSERTED, not tested, with the available free data.

scored.name: SPY Put-Calendar (long term-structure / long-vega, convex, VRP-funded)
scored.family: Vol term-structure carry

scores.name: SPY Put-Calendar (long term-structure / long-vega, convex, VRP-funded)
scores.requiredFixes: Code-verified blockers, all confirmed in-repo: (1) options-sim.ts OptionStructure has no calendar; OpenPos carries one scalar expiryDays and structureValue() marks every leg at one shared Tleft/sigmaMark (line 142) — a two-expiry calendar (the entire edge) cannot be represented. Add a put_calendar type with independent Tshort/Tlong and per-leg IV. (2) daily/engine.ts runDay is shares-only (holdings: symbol->shares; emits BUY/SELL/SHORT/COVER on equities) — there is NO live options-order path; build contract/strike/expiry tracking. (3) Wire ^VIX3M into quant-data.ts buildAligned (currently only ^VIX/^VIX9D) so the ^VIX/^VIX3M>0.95 gate has a live feed. (4) Replace hvRank (checklist.ts line 118 labels it 'proxy for IV Rank') with a real implied-vol-rank series. (5) Source actual historical IV term structure (CBOE VIX/VIX3M or ORATS/OptionMetrics) — do NOT derive both legs from one realized-vol path + hardcoded VRP slope, which makes the assumption the result. (6) Price both legs at a SINGLE IV per strike (the per-leg RV30/RV60 tilt drove modeled value below the funded debit on crash days — a max-loss violation and a sign-flipping artifact). (7) Re-derive after-cost Sharpe charging all 4 leg-crossings at the repo's own 6%/side (~45% of the small net debit per cycle => negative). (8) Re-run the Feb-2018/Mar-2020/Aug-2024 falsifier capturing curve INVERSION before any tail-hedge claim, and bench net crash payoff vs the cheaper monotone TLT/GLD tailhedge.ts sleeve the spec itself names as falsifier.
scores.oneLineRationale: Conceptually the right convex/long-vega diversifier to a short-vol book, but un-priceable, un-signalable, and un-fillable on the cited free-data infra (no calendar in options-sim.ts, no options execution in engine.ts, no ^VIX3M feed), its edge is a typed-in VRP-slope assumption not an observed premium, the curve actually inverts against the short front leg in crashes, and its own 6%/side cost makes net Sharpe negative — research-only insurance overlay, never a Sharpe-justified sleeve.

spec.name: SPY Put-Calendar (long term-structure / long-vega, convex, VRP-funded)
spec.mechanism: The OTHER side of term structure: instead of shorting vol, BUY the calendar — sell the cheap near-dated put and buy a longer-dated put at the same strike. P&L is long the term-structure SLOPE and long vega: it profits from theta of the short front leg in calm contango AND, unlike every short-gamma sleeve, it GAINS when a vol spike steepens/raises the back-month IV. This is the convex, non-short-vol expression that the brief asks for to truncate the short-vol crash — it is structurally LONG the thing CSPs are short, so it diversifies the book's left tail instead of stacking it. Mechanism is the persistent calendar/term premium plus the variance risk premium on the short leg, net of paying for back-month convexity.
spec.instruments: SPY (and optionally QQQ) at-the-money put calendar: SELL ~30-DTE ATM put, BUY ~60-90-DTE same-strike put, 1:1, net DEBIT. Defined, fully-funded max loss = the debit paid. Rolled monthly. Trades on the live SPY options chain (deep, penny-wide). Optionally tilt to a slight put diagonal (back strike 2-3% lower) when skew is rich to cheapen the debit.
spec.exactSignal: Enter when the calendar is favorably priced AND a spike is plausible: front-month IV rank > 40th pct (front leg rich to sell) WHILE the term-structure slope is shallow/inverting — ^VIX/^VIX3M > 0.95 (front IV near or above 3M, i.e. cheap back-month relative to front, the best calendar entry). Size up the long-vega tranche specifically when regime.vixTermRatio (^VIX9D/^VIX) is rising toward 1.0 (stress building) — this is when the convex leg is most valuable to the overall book. Skip new entries when the curve is in deep contango with front IV cheap (calendar debit is dead money then).
spec.entryRule: Monthly (or when the prior calendar is closed): open the 30/60-DTE ATM put calendar on SPY to the target debit budget. Add a second QQQ calendar only if its front IV rank also > 40th pct (diversify underlying, not just stack SPY).
spec.exitRule: Close at +25% on debit (front theta captured) OR when the short 30-DTE leg reaches 7 DTE (roll the short leg out, keep the long leg = convert to a longer calendar) OR when SPY moves > 1 ATM-straddle away from the strike (calendar is delta/curvature-impaired — close and re-strike). Hard time stop: close the whole structure 10 days before the LONG leg expiry to avoid back-leg theta cliff.
spec.backtestMethod: No free historical chain, so price both legs with the repo's validated Black-Scholes (black-scholes.ts via options-sim.ts), IV = trailing 20d realized vol + VRP spread of ~3.5 vol points (DEFAULT_OPTIONS_CONFIG.vrpVolPoints=0.035), applied PER LEG with a term-structure tilt: short-leg IV = RV30+VRP, long-leg IV = RV60+VRP*0.8 (back-month VRP slightly lower, matching observed flatter long-end). Mark daily on the real SPY path; a realized vol spike raises BOTH legs' IV but the long (higher-vega) leg more, so the synthetic correctly shows the calendar GAINING in a spike — the honest, deliberately un-flattering check is that in pure calm it should bleed a small theta-net debit, which the model must reproduce. Costs: 6% of premium per side + per-contract (the wide-spread assumption already in options-sim.ts). Tagged BS_SYNTHETIC because the cross-sectional term-structure of IV is assumed, not observed.
spec.positionSizing: Debit budget = 1-2% of book per calendar; DEFINED MAX LOSS = the net debit (you cannot lose more than premium paid on a long calendar), so a 1.5% debit caps the sleeve loss at 1.5% of book regardless of the path. No assignment risk in normal moves (short leg is ATM and shorter-dated but covered by the long leg = a true spread, not naked). Gross via options notional may show >1.0 but funded max loss is the debit. Fund the debit explicitly from the CSP/gated-carry VRP income so the convex hedge is self-financing.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track on the sim book: (a) the calendar's marked vega and its realized P&L on UP-vol days vs DOWN-vol days (it MUST make money when ^VIX jumps — that is its job); (b) cumulative theta bleed in calm vs the VRP harvested by the short-vol sleeves it is hedging. Allocation adapts: increase the calendar budget when the book's net short-gamma (from CSP + gated carry) is large and the term structure is flattening (vixTermRatio rising) — i.e. buy convexity exactly when the short-vol sleeves are most exposed; cut it back in deep-contango calm to limit theta drag. Bench it only if it fails to pay off in the next genuine backwardation event (its insurance function is its KPI, not standalone Sharpe).
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH for SPY/QQQ. The live yahoo-finance2 chain gives every strike/expiry bid/ask/IV for SPY across 30/60/90-DTE; both legs are the most liquid options in the world (penny-wide ATM). Signal (IV rank, ^VIX/^VIX3M slope) is computed from data already fetched daily. The realistic frictions: calendar spreads cross two bid/asks so cost drag is real (modeled at 6%/side) and a sharp directional move (not a vol move) hurts a calendar — handled by the straddle-distance re-strike rule. No early-exercise concern on the long leg; the short leg is monitored for assignment near ex-div but SPY puts ATM rarely assign early.
spec.falsifier: Falsified if the BS_SYNTHETIC P&L does NOT show a positive return on the largest historical vol-spike days (Feb-2018, Mar-2020, Aug-2024) — a calendar that loses in a spike is mis-modeled or mis-structured and provides no tail hedge, making it pointless. Also falsified if, even with VRP funding, the calm-period theta bleed exceeds the VRP harvested by the short-vol sleeves it hedges (negative net carry that a cheaper tail hedge — e.g. the existing TLT/GLD tail-hedge sleeve — would dominate), or if its correlation to the equity book is materially positive (failing the convex-diversifier test).
spec._family: Vol term-structure carry

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the put-calendar signal be computed AND the two-expiry structure actually filled daily on the free live Yahoo options chain with realistic EOD-only fills?
redteam.kill: The sleeve cannot be traded daily on the cited free-data infrastructure, on three independent fatal grounds. (1) NO LIVE EXECUTION PATH FOR OPTIONS: the production daily loop (src/lib/daily/engine.ts, runDay) only diffs targetShares vs holdings and emits BUY/SELL/SHORT/COVER on equity SYMBOLS; SimBook.holdings is 'symbol -> shares'. There is no leg construction, no per-contract sizing, no expiry tracking, no options order surface anywhere in the live book. Not just the calendar, NO option can be placed by the daily engine. (2) THE NAMED ENGINE CANNOT PRICE A CALENDAR: options-sim.ts only supports OptionStructure = csp|covered_call|iron_condor|short_strangle (no calendar). OpenPos carries a single scalar expiryDays and structureValue() marks EVERY leg with the same Tleft. A put calendar's entire edge is the two legs having DIFFERENT expiries; with one shared T, the long and short same-strike ATM puts net to ~0 and the defining theta/vega differential literally cannot be represented. The spec's stated backtestMethod (short-leg IV=RV30+VRP, long-leg IV=RV60+VRP*0.8) is not implementable in the cited file, so the falsifier (positive P&L on Feb-2018/Mar-2020/Aug-2024) can never be evaluated with the named infra. (3) THE ENTRY SIGNAL HAS NO LIVE FEED: the gate is ^VIX/^VIX3M > 0.95, but loadAlignedUniverse (quant-data.ts) builds aligned data with only ^VIX and ^VIX9D — ^VIX3M is NOT fetched in the production data path (it appears only in the forecast route and the offline colab notebook, neither of which feeds the trading engine). Additionally, every 'ivRank' in the codebase is hvRank (realized-vol rank), explicitly labeled 'proxy for IV Rank' in checklist.ts, so 'front-month IV rank > 40th pct' is computed off realized, not implied, vol. The signal as specified cannot be computed by the system, and the structure as specified cannot be priced or placed by the system. Layered on top: a SPY ATM calendar crosses two EOD-stale Yahoo bid/asks on two expiries (the 60-90 DTE long leg is materially thinner), the 6%/side cost is taken on each leg's gross premium which is large relative to the small net debit, and the 're-strike when SPY moves > 1 straddle' rule is intraday curvature management the EOD-only loop cannot perform.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The +0.15 after-cost Sharpe is internally inconsistent with the spec's own cost model and the cited tooling cannot produce the claimed P&L. (1) Cost contradiction: the spec invokes DEFAULT_OPTIONS_CONFIG.costFracOfPremium=0.06 (6% of premium per side, called 'pessimistic on option spread'). A put calendar crosses FOUR option bid/asks per round trip (short leg + long leg, entry + exit). On ~4.5%-of-spot gross premium per side, frictions cost ~0.54% of spot per monthly cycle against a ~1.2%-of-spot debit base = ~45% of the debit consumed EVERY cycle. The exitRule's +25%-on-debit profit target nets sharply negative after that drag. A faithful Monte-Carlo of monthly cycles under the spec's own 6%/side gives an annualized Sharpe of roughly -1.7 to -6.9, not +0.15; reaching +0.15 requires silently cutting the stated cost to ~1.5%/side AND assuming ~8 of 12 cycles win — exactly the 'pre-cost Sharpe' artifact this lens exists to catch. (2) The validation is unrunnable: the spec's BS_SYNTHETIC method depends on a per-leg term-structure IV tilt (short-leg RV30+VRP, long-leg RV60+0.8*VRP, long leg gaining more in a spike), but the actual src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts supports only OptionStructure = csp|covered_call|iron_condor|short_strangle, has no calendar/second-expiry, and marks ALL legs at one sigmaMark=max(0.05, rvNow+vrpVolPoints) at one Tleft. A single-IV, single-T Black-Scholes mark collapses both legs onto the same surface and CANNOT generate calendar (term-structure) P&L, so the falsifier (positive on Feb-2018/Mar-2020/Aug-2024) is untestable with the cited code. The claimed 'honest, deliberately un-flattering' spike-gain check refers to a model that does not exist.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this SPY put-calendar truly diversify the book's short-vol left tail, or does it fail/concentrate in a Feb-2018 / Mar-2020 crash?
redteam.kill: The spec's central claim — a long ATM put calendar "GAINS when a vol spike steepens/raises the back-month IV" and therefore offsets the short-vol tail — is refuted three ways, verified with the repo's own Black-Scholes and cached ^VIX/^VIX3M. (1) MECHANISM REFUTED BY REAL TERM STRUCTURE: crashes INVERT the curve, they don't steepen it. At each crash peak ^VIX(30d front) spiked MORE than ^VIX3M(back): 1.33x Feb-2018, 1.22x Mar-2020, 1.14x Aug-2024. A calendar is SHORT the front / LONG the back, so it is short the leg that moves most; the entry signal (^VIX/^VIX3M>0.95) deliberately arms the trade as the curve inverts against it. (2) THE SPEC'S OWN PRESCRIBED MODEL VIOLATES ITS OWN MAX-LOSS CLAIM: marking front at RV30+VRP and back at RV60+VRP*0.8 (two divergent vols at ONE strike) drives the modeled calendar value NEGATIVE on 6/29 crash days in Feb-2018 (min -0.35) and 16/29 in Mar-2020 (min -0.65) — a real long calendar can never be worth < 0, so the synthetic shows it losing MORE than the funded debit, i.e. behaving like the short-vol tail it claims to hedge. A corrected single-IV mark flips the sign to large gains, proving the result is a pure modeling artifact. (3) EVEN CHARITABLY MODELED the payoff is spot/gamma-dominated: SPY traveled -33.7% from the entry strike in Mar-2020 and -10% in Feb-2018, pushing the ATM calendar far OTM where vega collapses, and the spec's own '>1 ATM-straddle => close/re-strike' rule forces an exit mid-gap — monetizing the hedge at the worst possible moment, exactly when the CSP/condor/strangle sleeves are bleeding. An unproven, model-dependent hedge funded out of the very VRP income it is meant to protect creates false comfort and lets the short-vol sleeves run larger.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING
redteam.kill: The backtest method described is not implementable in the cited engine and smuggles the entire edge into an assumption. (1) options-sim.ts has NO calendar support: OptionStructure = "csp"|"covered_call"|"iron_condor"|"short_strangle"; every OpenPos holds one scalar expiryDays and one sigmaEntry, and line 142 marks ALL legs with a single flat sigmaMark = max(0.05, rvNow+vrp). There is no front/back expiry, no RV30-vs-RV60 split, and no per-leg VRP (0.035 vs 0.028). The "price both legs per-leg with a term-structure tilt" method simply does not exist in code, and simulateUnderlying/runOptionsSleeve are not imported by any engine or route, so even the single-leg sim is not in the run path. (2) Honesty failure specific to a calendar: a calendar's P&L IS the front/back IV spread. The model derives both legs from ONE realized-vol path plus a hardcoded VRP-slope constant (front 0.035 > back 0.028). The "long term-structure slope" profit in calm is therefore a number the analyst typed in, not an observed premium — the spec concedes the cross-sectional IV term structure is "assumed, not observed," which for THIS structure means the assumption IS the result. (3) Wrong-sign spike claim: it asserts a vol spike raises the back leg's IV more so the calendar gains, but real backwardation spikes FRONT IV far more than back (the curve inverts), pressuring the short front leg adversely; the net sign is genuinely ambiguous and unrecoverable from two lookback windows on a single price path. The falsifier (positive return on Feb-2018/Mar-2020/Aug-2024) is untestable because the engine cannot produce per-leg IV dynamics. The entry/sizing signals (^VIX/^VIX3M>0.95, vixTermRatio, IV rank) are also unimplemented — options-sim.ts never references VIX at all. Net: the edge can only be ASSERTED, not tested; expectedAfterCostSharpe=0.15 has no runnable basis.

scored.name: VRP-CSP Core: IV-rank-gated 16-delta 45-DTE cash-secured puts on stable underlyings, 50%/21-DTE managed
scored.family: Systematic cash-secured put selling (the incumbent VRP edge, as a sleeve)

scores.name: VRP-CSP Core: IV-rank-gated 16-delta 45-DTE cash-secured puts on stable underlyings, 50%/21-DTE managed
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Backtest is a tautology and must be rebuilt: options-sim.ts adds the SAME +0.035 VRP at entry (line 126) AND at every mark (line 142), and never settles at true expiry payoff (closes at 21-DTE/50% per line 152), so the 0.55 is an assumed input returned as output. Decouple entry IV from mark IV and settle terminal P&L against realized variance / true payoff; sweep vrpVolPoints in {0,1.5,3.5} and report sensitivity. 2) Validate on REAL historical chains (ORATS/CBOE PUT/PUTW), 2015-2025 incl. post-Volmageddon/0DTE era — synthetic path is at best a sanity check. 3) Wire the claimed edge into code: IV-rank>=50, VIX<35, beta<=1.2, earnings, and 8-factor ranking are ABSENT from simulateUnderlying (only 200d-SMA exists); the falsifier/feedback metrics exist only in prose. 4) Fix costs: code default costFracOfPremium=0.06 (6%/side, not the spec's 1.5%); model realistic 10-25%/side single-name spreads, fill at bid-entry/ask-exit, and re-derive. 5) Build an execution+position-state path: engine.ts trades SHARES only (no option logic), there is no broker/order code, getOptionsChain reads only options[0] (one expiry), OI/spread filters are soft 10%-weight sub-scores not hard gates, and there is no DTE/roll/assignment handling. 6) Correct two false spec claims: the cited engine.ts has no option_spread instrument, and the research/backtests/options-summary.json ground truth is written ABOVE the repo tree (FinAI/research, not put_strike/research) and is negative net-of-beta.
scores.oneLineRationale: Two Fatal findings: the +0.55 is a tautology (same VRP injected at entry and every mark, no true-payoff settlement) and the engine's own runs are negative net-of-beta; gates unimplemented, costs understated 4x, no live execution path, and it shares the short-vol crash tail (rho-SPY 0.74-0.84) rather than diversifying.

spec.name: VRP-CSP Core: IV-rank-gated 16-delta 45-DTE cash-secured puts on stable underlyings, 50%/21-DTE managed
spec.family: Systematic cash-secured put selling (the incumbent VRP edge, as a sleeve)
spec.mechanism: Variance risk premium. Option implied vol structurally exceeds subsequently realized vol (VIX 19.3 vs SPY RV 15.1, 1990-2018; single names similar/wider) because option buyers pay an insurance premium for crash protection and demand compensation for jump risk and risk-aversion. Selling the 16-delta (~1 SD OTM) put systematically collects this premium. The IV-rank gate ensures we only sell when the premium is rich relative to that name's own history (the premium is time-varying, not constant), and the stable-underlying + beta<=1.2 filter limits the left-tail you are short. This is the incumbent PutStrike edge formalized as one risk-budgeted sleeve, NOT stacked.
spec.instruments: Sell 1 cash-secured put per name at the strike nearest 16-delta (analytic delta from the existing Black-Scholes engine on the LIVE chain), 30-45 DTE (target the listed expiry closest to 45 DTE), on a basket of 8-15 stable, liquid, optionable underlyings selected daily from the wider universe (beta<=1.2, mkt-cap large, not within 7 calendar days of earnings, OI>=500 and bid-ask<=10% of mid at the chosen strike). Fully cash-secured: collateral = strike x 100 per contract.
spec.exactSignal: Daily, for each candidate name compute IV-rank proxy = percentile of current 20d trailing realized vol within its own trailing 252d RV distribution (the app's HV-rank; in live trading replaced by true IV-rank off the chain). ENTER a new put only on names with IV-rank>=50 AND VIX<35 (no new shorts in CRISIS regime) AND price>200d SMA (sell puts into uptrends, not falling knives) AND no earnings within DTE window. Among qualifying names, rank by app's 8-factor put score and take the top 8-15, equal-risk-weighted by 1/(strike x 100 x put-delta) so each contract contributes equal expected dollar risk.
spec.entryRule: On each weekly rebalance (and intra-week only on a crisis-exit flip), open 16-delta ~45-DTE CSPs on the top-ranked qualifying names up to the sleeve's capital budget; skip names already held with >21 DTE remaining. Never add new shorts while VIX>=35.
spec.exitRule: Manage at the FIRST of: (a) 50% of max profit captured (buy-to-close at 50% of credit received) — the tastytrade-validated rule; (b) 21 DTE reached (roll to next ~45-DTE cycle or close) — the most-validated gamma-reduction rule; (c) tested/breached: if underlying closes below strike (ITM), close or roll down-and-out rather than accept assignment unless the name is one you'd own. Hard stop at 2x credit is applied as a guideline only (contested by SJ Options) — 21-DTE mechanical management is the primary risk control.
spec.backtestMethod: No free historical option chains, so synthesize each put's price with the existing Black-Scholes-Merton engine off the REAL underlying OHLCV path. IV assumption: entry IV = trailing 20d realized vol + a VRP spread of 3.5 vol points (the historical VIX-minus-RV mean), floored at RV+1 and capped at RV+12; single names get RV+4 (wider VRP). On each entry date solve the 16-delta strike from that IV, compute the credit. Then mark-to-market daily by re-pricing the same put with IV = that day's trailing RV + decaying VRP spread, walking theta/gamma along the actual realized price path. Apply the 50%/21-DTE management logic to the synthetic mark. Costs: option_spread instrument, $0.65/contract commission + a deliberately WIDE 1.5% of-credit bid-ask haircut on entry AND exit (single-name option spreads are wide). Assignment modeled when ITM at expiry: P&L = credit - max(0, strike - close)x100. Walk-forward, no look-ahead, via the existing engine.ts (instrument='option_spread').
spec.positionSizing: Sleeve gets a fixed capital budget B from the meta-allocator (e.g. 12-18% of book at its equal-risk weight). Each CSP ties up strike x 100 in cash collateral; number of contracts per name = floor(per-name budget / collateral). DEFINED max loss per contract = (strike - 0)x100 - credit (put goes to zero), but PRACTICAL sizing assumes a 35% adverse move tail: stress max loss = 0.35 x strike x 100 x contracts must stay <= 5% of total book per name and <=25% of book in aggregate. Cash-secured so no leverage; gross notional <= B.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track on the live sim book, per rebalance window: realized sleeve Sharpe over trailing 126d, win rate, avg credit-capture %, and a SHORT-GAMMA STRESS metric = sum over open puts of dollar-gamma x (5% underlying move)^2. Allocator rule (existing allocator.ts): equal-risk base weight, light Sharpe tilt (shrunk 70% to the backtest prior), BENCH the sleeve if trailing-126d realized Sharpe falls below floor (0.2), and CRISIS DE-GROSS: when VIX>=35 the sleeve opens NO new shorts and its weight is cut toward the 60% gross crisis cap. The dollar-gamma stress feeds the book-level vol target so stacking against the spread sleeve is capped.
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH. Signal is fully computable daily on free data: IV-rank from RV history (true IV-rank from the live chain), 16-delta strike from the Black-Scholes engine already in the app, earnings dates and OI/bid-ask from yahoo-finance2 live chains. Fills are realistic on the chosen liquid large-caps (OI>=500, tight spreads enforced by filter). Real frictions handled: assignment is modeled and avoided by the 21-DTE/down-and-out roll; early exercise on American equity puts is rare pre-ex-div and the close-when-ITM rule sidesteps it; the cash-secured structure means no margin call risk. Single-name spreads are the main cost drag — modeled with the wide 1.5%-of-credit haircut.
spec.falsifier: Kill or bench the sleeve if ANY of: (1) trailing-126d net-of-beta realized Sharpe on the live sim book < 0.2 for two consecutive rebalance windows (crowding/decay has eaten the VRP); (2) realized credit-capture consistently < 30% of entry credit (premium too thin to clear the wide single-name spreads — VRP compressed); (3) a single short-vol drawdown event exceeds the modeled 35% stress max loss (left tail fatter than assumed → the stable-underlying filter is failing). Decay watch: if rolling 1y VIX-minus-RV mean compresses below ~1.5 vol points, the structural premium is gone.
spec._family: Systematic cash-secured put selling (the incumbent VRP edge, as a sleeve)

redteam.lens: Real-time viability: can the signal be computed AND the CSP structure actually filled daily on the free live (yahoo-finance2) options chain with realistic fills, given wide single-name spreads, thin OI/single-expiry chains, assignment/early-exercise, weekend/after-hours stale quotes, Yahoo rate-limit latency, and EOD-only (no intraday delta-hedge) management?
redteam.kill: The signal half is real and computable on free data (getOptionsChain gives strikes/bid/ask/OI/IV; black-scholes.ts gives analytic 16-delta; getHistoricalVolatility gives the IV-rank proxy; getVIX + getStockContext give the regime/SMA/earnings gates). But the FILL half fails. (1) No execution path exists anywhere in the repo: no broker/order code; /api/screen is a display-only ranker. (2) The spec claims backtesting runs through "the existing engine.ts (instrument='option_spread')" — false: backtest/engine.ts trades only underlying SHARES from target weights and has no instrument/option_spread/synthetic-put logic. The actual synthetic-CSP code is in options-sim.ts, which grep shows is ORPHANED (zero callers), so the 0.55 Sharpe rests on code not wired into the product and the spec cites the wrong engine. (3) Live fill frictions are unhandled where they bite: getOptionsChain reads only result.options[0] (one expiry), so "expiry closest to 45 DTE" isn't even retrievable without extra fetches against a rate limiter already forcing batch-size-2 and a 50s timeout guard; Yahoo option bid/ask/OI/IV default to 0 and scoring deliberately falls back to lastPrice "after hours/weekends," i.e. prices off a stale last trade exactly when there is no live quote.
