(4) The promised OI>=500 and bid-ask<=10% entry filters are NOT hard gates — they only feed a 10%-weight soft "liquidity" sub-score that a rich premium can outvote, so a thin/wide single-name put can rank top. (5) Single-name 16-delta puts on the free chain routinely show 15-40% bid/ask; the synth crosses at mid (won't fill live) and assumes IV=RV+3.5-4 vp with a 1.5%-of-credit haircut, so crossing near the bid erases the modeled credit — the spec's own falsifier admits credit-capture <30% is a kill, which is the EXPECTED outcome on wide single-name spreads, not a tail. (6) Assignment/early-exercise and the down-and-out roll exist only as prose and in the orphaned synth; the live path has no position state, DTE tracking, or roll logic, so an EOD-only system has no defense against weekend/overnight gap assignment on American equity puts. It survives as a screener/idea generator; it does NOT survive as a thing that fills CSPs daily with realistic fills as specified.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The spec's headline expectedAfterCostSharpe of 0.55 is falsified by the repo's own ground-truth backtest. research/backtests/options-summary.json (generated 2026-06-02 by the very options-sim.ts engine the spec cites) reports the proposed strategy csp_stable at Sharpe -0.37 / netOfBetaSharpe -2.36 / CAGR -2.51%, and the exact 200d-SMA filtered variant (csp_stable_filt, which the spec promotes) at Sharpe -0.58 / netOfBetaSharpe -2.27. Every CSP/condor/strangle variant in that file is negative net-of-beta; the condor and strangle even hit 99.98% drawdowns. No configuration in the repo produces a positive net-of-beta Sharpe, let alone +0.55. Separately, the spec understates option costs ~4x: it claims a 1.5%-of-credit haircut per side, but the code's own DEFAULT_OPTIONS_CONFIG.costFracOfPremium = 0.06 (6%/side; options-sim.ts:82) is the assumption that PRODUCED those negative numbers, and realistic single-name 16-delta 45-DTE spreads are 10-25%/side of premium. The targeted 12-18 vol 'stable' names yield only ~0.4-0.7% of strike in credit per cycle, so a wide single-name spread eats most of the thin premium - exactly the lens. Re-derivation: reaching +0.55 net-of-beta from the realized -0.37 needs ~+6.3%/yr of alpha, while the entire realistic round-trip cost is only ~3-4%/yr, so even at ZERO option cost this engine does not reach 0.55. The 0.55 is not 'pre-cost'; it is unsupported at any cost level. The spec's own falsifier (kill if netβ Sharpe < 0.2 for two windows) is already tripped pre-deployment.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this VRP-CSP sleeve carry a short-gamma left tail that co-moves with the other short-vol sleeves (condor/strangle/etc.) in a Feb-2018/Mar-2020 crash, concentrating crash risk rather than diversifying — and does the proposed hedge actually offset the modeled crash drawdown?
redteam.kill: The spec is contradicted by its own engine (options-sim.ts), and the lens is confirmed by the spec's own admission (sharesShortVolTail:true). CONCENTRATION, not diversification: the synthetic CSP sleeve has ρSPY 0.74-0.84 and crashes in lockstep with the sibling short-vol structures (Mar-2020: CSP -18.4% peak-to-trough [csp_stable_filt, 2020-02-07->2020-03-23], index-CSP -27%, condor -77%, strangle -23%; Feb-2018: condor -31%). Full-sample MaxDD 28.8%. This is a beta-laden equity-tail sleeve, not an orthogonal diversifier. I stress-tested whether the IV=RV+VRP marking sugar-coats the tail: at the trough the 16-delta put is deep ITM and ~all intrinsic, so the real price path (not the lagging RV-based IV mark) drives the loss and the vega understatement is only ~0.3% of collateral — so the -18% to -28% modeled drawdown is CREDIBLE, not understated. THE KILLER: the spec headlines expectedAfterCostSharpe 0.55 and pitches VRP as the high-Sharpe core, but the engine that implements this exact spec produces standalone Sharpe -0.58, CAGR -2.89%, net-of-beta Sharpe -2.27 over 2015-2026. On a sample containing Feb-18/Mar-20/2022/Apr-25 vol events the crash losses exceed the premium collected — the VRP does not clear its own left tail. The 'diversifying' (corr 0.55) and '0.55 Sharpe' claims are unsupported by the codebase. On the hedge offset: it is only partial and book-level — the combined portfolio drew -6.6% in Mar 2020 vs the sleeve's -18%, but that comes from sizing the sleeve at 12-18% + cutting vol_premium to 0.25x at VIX>=35 + TLT/GLD rallying, and the crisis de-gross fires only AFTER VIX>=35 at a weekly rebalance applied to subsequent days, so it cannot prevent the initial gap leg, only the continuation. The sleeve is a MANAGED CONCENTRATION of crash risk, not a diversifier.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING (VRP-CSP cash-secured put sleeve)
redteam.kill: The BS-synthetic backtest is not honest: it hard-codes the edge it claims to measure. In src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts the entry credit is priced at IV = trailing-20d RV + vrpVolPoints (0.035) [lines 126-127], and EVERY mark-to-market and the exit are priced at IV = RV_now + the SAME 0.035 [lines 142-143]. Positions almost never settle at true expiry — they close at 21-DTE management or the 50% profit target [line 152], so terminal P&L is a Black-Scholes RE-MARK, never a realized option payoff. Because the identical +0.035 VRP spread is added at entry and at every mark, the synthetic short option is BY CONSTRUCTION always priced ~3.5 vol points above the realized vol of the very path it walks. That IS the assumed VRP, fed back as 'discovered' profit. Set vrpVolPoints=0 and the sleeve's Sharpe collapses; set it to 0.035 and it prints. The backtest is a tautology that returns whatever VRP you input — it cannot falsify the IV>RV assumption because it assumes it. So expectedAfterCostSharpe=0.55 is an ASSUMPTION DRESSED AS A RESULT, not a test. Worse, the selection edge the spec sells is absent from the code: IV-rank>=50 gate, VIX<35 gate, true IV-rank, beta<=1.2, earnings filter, and the 8-factor put-score ranking are NOT implemented in simulateUnderlying/runOptionsSleeve — only delta-strike, 200d-SMA uptrend, RV+VRP pricing, and 50/21 management exist. The falsifier metrics (credit-capture %, short-gamma stress, 35% stress-loss breach) and dailyFeedbackLoop diagnostics exist only in prose (grep: referenced in no file but options-sim.ts, none exported). On crowding/decay: the headline VIX 19.3 vs RV 15.1 stat is the 1990-2018 window the spec itself cites, which predates the regime that matters — post-2018 SVXY/XIV blowup (Feb 2018 'Volmageddon') and the 2021-2024 0DTE explosion compressed and changed the term structure; single-name VRP at retail size after a deliberately-wide-but-still-optimistic 1.5%/6% spread haircut is exactly where decay bites, and the spec gives no out-of-sample post-2018 evidence that net single-name VRP clears those frictions. The whole edge can only be ASSERTED here, not tested, because no real historical option chains are used.

scored.name: Skew-Harvest Put Credit Spread (index + liquid large-cap, IV-rank gated)
scored.family: Defined-risk VRP: iron condors / put spreads

scores.name: Skew-Harvest Put Credit Spread (index + liquid large-cap, IV-rank gated)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Re-spec costs as per-leg half-spread on WIDTH (4 leg-crossings round-trip from live yahoo bid/ask), not costFracOfPremium*|credit| (options-sim.ts L129/L154); re-run capmStats and keep ONLY names with post-cost per-cycle edge > 0 (almost certainly SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA, drop the single-name basket). 2) Put a real per-strike skew term into the synthetic pricer — black-scholes.ts L66 prices every leg with one scalar sigma, so the 16d/7d puts get identical vol and the entire skew-harvest thesis is invisible; price the put wing at IV_atm + an empirical 16d-put skew premium so the edge is TESTED not asserted. 3) Accumulate per-name daily ATM-IV and 16d-put-IV history to build a REAL forward IV-rank and skew percentile — current gate is HV-rank from realized closes (checklist.ts L118 'proxy for IV Rank'), cannot be backfilled. 4) Build a two-leg quoter (live bid/ask both legs, marketable mid, reject zero-bid/thin-OI wings and credit < 8% of width), register it as a defined-max-loss sleeve in registry.ts and WIRE INTO runDay — today runDay trades shares only (engine.ts L151-153) and options-sim is used solely by scripts/backtest. 5) Add assignment + weekend-gap + jump/skew-aware crash mark; cap defined-max-loss JOINTLY across all put-side variants so the allocator can net against the tail hedge and degross in a crisis. Until all of these, carry at ZERO weight, paper-only.
scores.oneLineRationale: Four Fatal findings all verified in code: skew edge is absent from the backtest (single scalar sigma, no smile), live path trades shares only with no put-spread quoter or sleeve, costs are anchored to thin premium not bid/ask width, and one shared short-vol tail marks every name — the 0.55 is synthetic, un-tradable, and skew-blind; honest net-of-beta Sharpe rounds to ~0.

spec.name: Skew-Harvest Put Credit Spread (index + liquid large-cap, IV-rank gated)
spec.family: Defined-risk VRP: iron condors / put spreads
spec.mechanism: Variance risk premium concentrated on the PUT side. Index/large-cap options carry a persistent put skew: OTM puts are systematically over-priced because real-money is a structural buyer of crash insurance (pension/portfolio hedging flow), so IV on the put wing exceeds subsequently-realized downside vol by MORE than the call side does. Selling a put SPREAD (not a naked put) harvests that skew-laden VRP while the long lower-strike put caps max loss to a known number the meta-allocator can size against. This is the single defined-risk short-vol VRP sleeve the Sharpe-2 plan calls for — one short-gamma sleeve, tail truncated, funded alongside the cross-asset-trend and tail-hedge orthogonal sleeves.
spec.instruments: Sell 1x 16-delta 45-DTE put, buy 1x ~7-delta put as the wing (wide, low-delta long leg so it costs little theta but still caps the tail). Universe: SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA plus a basket of stable large-caps (AAPL MSFT JPM JNJ PG KO HD MCD WMT COST UNH V MA XOM CVX CAT HON LIN). Equal-weight across names with a live, liquid put-spread quotable on the free yahoo-finance2 chain.
spec.exactSignal: Enter only when VRP is positive AND skew is being paid: (a) IV-rank of the underlying's ATM IV over its trailing 1y >= 30 (sell richness, not cheapness); (b) put-skew proxy = (16Δ-put IV - ATM IV) in the top half of its own 1y distribution; (c) per-stock 6-model ensemble + iTransformer 60d concordance gate NOT bearish (don't sell puts into a forecasted breakdown). Short strike = 16Δ put; long wing = 7Δ put. In BS_SYNTHETIC backtest the entry IV = trailing 20d realized vol + 3.5 vol-pt VRP, strikes solved by lognormal-quantile delta.
spec.entryRule: On each rebalance, for every name flat and passing the IV-rank>=30 + skew + non-bearish-concordance gates, open the 16Δ/7Δ 45-DTE put credit spread. Equal-weight defined-max-loss across active names; skip names whose live bid/ask on either leg is missing or whose spread width prices < 8% of (strike width) as credit (insufficient premium).
spec.exitRule: Close at 50% of max profit captured, OR at 21 DTE (the tested gamma-management rule), OR if the short strike is breached and concordance flips bearish (defensive close), whichever first. No roll if IV-rank has collapsed < 15 (edge gone); otherwise re-open next cycle on a fresh 45-DTE spread.
spec.backtestMethod: Runs in the existing src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts with structure='iron_condor' restricted to the put side (short 16Δ put + long 7Δ put; call legs zeroed) via the OptionsSleeveConfig. IV at entry = annualizedVol(closes,20) + vrpVolPoints (0.035); positions marked DAILY at sigma=max(0.05, RV_now+VRP) so a vol spike RAISES the short's mark and the short-gamma drawdown appears honestly. P&L per cycle = credit kept + cost-to-close, on a base of defined max loss = (putWidth - credit). Costs = 6% of premium per side + $0.65/contract, applied at entry and exit. Net-of-beta Sharpe via capmStats vs SPY. IV-rank/skew gate is approximated in backtest by an RV-percentile filter; the live chain uses real IV-rank. Stated assumptions: IV=RV+VRP, EOD marks, no early exercise, no intraday skew term — research approximation, live uses the REAL chain.
spec.positionSizing: Defined max loss per spread = (short-long strike width) - net credit, e.g. on a $500 SPY ~ $25-wide put spread max loss ~ $2,000/contract minus credit. Size so that SUM of max-loss across all open spreads = a fixed fraction (target ~15-20%) of sleeve book; sleeve itself vol-targeted to ~8% annual inside the meta-allocator. Gross options notional can exceed 1.0x but TOTAL defined max loss is hard-capped to book — no naked tail.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track on the live sim book daily: (1) realized capture ratio = actual credit decay vs theoretical theta; (2) running net-of-beta Sharpe and MaxDD of the sleeve; (3) average IV-rank at entry. Allocation adapts: if trailing-60d sleeve Sharpe deceleration crosses below 0 OR MaxDD breaches the vol-target band, the meta-allocator de-grosses this sleeve; if IV-rank regime is rich (median entry IV-rank > 50) and realized capture > 0.4, it scales up to the max-loss cap. Benches the sleeve entirely if 90d capture ratio < 0 (VRP gone / crowded).
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH for index (SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA) and mega-cap names — full chains with bid/ask/IV/OI are fetched live via yahoo-finance2, IV-rank and skew are computable from that chain daily, and 16Δ/7Δ 45-DTE put spreads are among the most liquid structures retail can fill. Defined-risk = no margin call, no naked assignment surprise; early-exercise risk is limited to the deep-ITM short put near expiry, mitigated by the 21-DTE close. Single-name spreads on the wider basket are thinner — restrict to names with OI on both strikes. Daily signal + daily fills are realistic.
spec.falsifier: Kill the sleeve if, on the live sim book over a rolling 90 days, the realized credit-capture ratio is <= 0 (premium collected does not exceed losses+costs, i.e. IV no longer exceeds realized on the put wing), OR net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.2 after costs, OR the 16Δ/7Δ spread's median entry credit falls below 8% of strike width (skew/VRP crowded out). Any of these means the put-side VRP edge has decayed below the 4-leg cost hurdle.
spec._family: Defined-risk VRP: iron condors / put spreads

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the signal be computed AND the put credit spread be FILLED daily on the free live Yahoo options chain?
redteam.kill: Both load-bearing claims fail against the code. Signal not computable live: gates need true IV-rank and a put-skew percentile over 1y, but free yahoo-finance2 gives only a per-contract IV snapshot. The code substitutes HV-rank from realized vol of closes (yahoo-finance.ts L190-261; screen/route.ts L109 ivRank=hv.hvRank; checklist.ts L118 calls it proxy for IV Rank), and the backtest uses IV=RV+flat 3.5 vol-pt VRP with no skew term (options-sim.ts L126), so the skew edge is never measured. Structure not filled live: the only automated path runDay (daily/engine.ts L150-177) trades shares at the underlying close; registry.ts has 8 equity/ETF sleeves and no put-spread sleeve, and options-sim.ts is used only by scripts/backtest. The live screener (screen/route.ts plus scoring.ts) ranks single short puts with no long wing, and simulated_trades is a manual single-leg CSP logger with no second leg and no daily fill loop. Frictions unhandled: 4-leg slippage, thin 7-delta wing OI on single names, early-exercise and assignment, zero-bid or stale quotes masked via lastPrice. Signal not computable on free data and structure not fillable by any live path.

redteam.lens: Sharpe Reality (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The cost model anchors transaction cost to PREMIUM, not bid/ask WIDTH, which structurally understates the dominant cost of a multi-leg option trade. In src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts the entry/exit cost is `costFracOfPremium*|credit| + $0.65/contract` (6% of premium). I reproduced the BS pricer + sizing: a SPY-like 16d/7d 45-DTE put spread collects ~$1.62 credit on a ~$12.84 max-loss base; the model charges ~$0.159 round-trip (~1.24% of base, ~9.8% of credit). But a put SPREAD is 4 leg-crossings round-trip (open 2 + close 2), each paying a dealer half-spread that is INDEPENDENT of the thin credit. At realistic half-spreads ($0.05-0.15/leg index, $0.10-0.20/leg single-name) the true round-trip is $0.23-0.83/share = 14-39% of credit on index and 29-107% of credit on the single-name large-caps they equal-weight into. Because the entry gate ADMITS credits as low as 8% of width, 6%-of-a-thin-credit is trivially small while the dealer's spread is unchanged — the exact pre-cost-realism trap. Translating to Sharpe at their 8% vol target: same gross edge gives model Sharpe ~4.4, realistic index ~0.8, realistic single-name ~ -6.8. Equal-weighting ~18-30 single names where costs eat 55-107% of premium drags the blended net-of-beta Sharpe to ~0.1-0.2 — at/below their own 0.2 falsifier. Compounding this: the backtest by its own admission has 'no intraday skew term,' so the headline put-skew-harvest edge is ASSERTED, not present in the synthetic P&L (IV is just flat RV+3.5vol-pts), and exit cost is charged on the shrinking |closeValue| so it vanishes exactly when 50%-profit round-trips are most frequent. The claimed 0.55 is pre-realistic-cost and pre-skew-verification.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: co-moves with other short-vol sleeves in a 2018 or 2020 crash, concentrating crash risk; the tail-hedge offset is unmodeled and mistimed.
redteam.kill: The repos own backtest already nulled this exact put-side VRP structure and the marking model shares one short-vol tail across all names, so it concentrates crash risk rather than diversifying, and the hedge cannot offset it.

redteam.lens: Backtest honesty and crowding of the put-side VRP edge
redteam.kill: The edge is put skew but the cited backtest options-sim.ts and black-scholes.ts price every leg with one flat sigma equal to RV plus 3.5 vol points. There is no skew or smile term anywhere in the backtest code, so the 16 delta and 7 delta puts get identical vol and the skew premium is invisible. The edge is asserted not tested. The flat VRP add is the only thing creating positive carry, yet the repo own fresh options-summary.json shows the closest proxy condor at CAGR minus 53 percent, max drawdown minus 99.98 percent, net of beta Sharpe minus 4.39, and plain cash secured puts at minus 0.12 to minus 0.58 Sharpe, contradicting the claimed 0.55. The put only condor with call legs zeroed is not an actual code path. The IV rank and skew gate exists only in the live app not the simulator. Crowding and decay cannot be tested because VRP is a hard coded constant for the whole window, not a measured implied versus realized quantity, so the method cannot detect decay at all.

scored.name: VRP-Isolated Beta-Neutral Call-Spread Overwrite (single-name, IV-rank gated, defined-risk)
scored.family: Covered calls / the wheel (systematic call overwriting + CSP→assignment→covered-call wheel)

scores.name: VRP-Isolated Beta-Neutral Call-Spread Overwrite (single-name, IV-rank gated, defined-risk)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Implement the actual 30D/10D short-call vertical 'overwrite' OptionStructure in src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts (currently only csp|covered_call|iron_condor|short_strangle exist) plus an explicit daily-subtracted SPY/ES beta-overlay return series, the IV-rank gate, and the iTransformer concordance gate; then re-run and report the REAL net-of-beta Sharpe -- stop quoting 0.78/0.5-0.6, which no code produced. 2) Replace getHistoricalVolatility's realized-vol rank with a true stored 252-day OPTION-IV rank from the chain (yahoo-finance.ts:190 computes HV-rank, not the spec's IV-rank). 3) Replace the %-of-premium cost with an absolute per-leg bid/ask model charging 4 leg-crossings per cycle on real single-name OTM quotes (reject any 10D wing with bid<0.05 or OI<100) and add the daily SPY hedge equity round-trip costs. 4) Build a real options-execution path in the live runDay engine (daily/engine.ts only diffs equity share weights -- no strike selection, no delta-hedge rebalancer, no ex-div/assignment manager) and acknowledge an EOD cron cannot hold |beta|<0.15 intraday, the falsifier's own death condition. 5) Remove the hardcoded PRIOR.csp_vrp=1.0 (portfolio.ts:97, labeled 'high-Sharpe short-vol core' over a negative sleeve); let the allocator see the measured Sharpe. 6) Stress VRP to ZERO in post-2018/0DTE windows and report that as the honest floor. 7) Fix the mis-signed/uncapped SPY hedge and tag vol_premium for de-grossing given sharesShortVolTail=true and rhoSPY~0.81.
scores.oneLineRationale: The spec's exact structure (beta-neutral 30D/10D call vertical, IV-rank + concordance gates, SPY overlay) exists in NO backtest or live code path, so the headline ~0.78 Sharpe is asserted not produced; honest reconciliation is zero-to-negative net-of-beta with two Fatal findings (no execution path, EOD cron cannot hold beta) plus a shared short-vol tail at rhoSPY~0.81 -- CUT.

spec.name: VRP-Isolated Beta-Neutral Call-Spread Overwrite (single-name, IV-rank gated, defined-risk)
spec.mechanism: Variance risk premium (structural). AQR's decomposition shows the ONLY compensated, Sharpe~1.0 piece of a covered call is the short-volatility/VRP leg; the ~65% equity-beta and ~25% equity-timing pieces are uncompensated drag the book already owns. This sleeve isolates the VRP: it SELLS a near-30-delta call (richly-priced upside insurance, IV > subsequent RV) financed-against / capped by a long ~10-delta wing, and STRIPS the equity beta the rest of the book already carries (continuous SPY-future / SPY-ETF short to neutralize residual delta). The economic edge is that call IV embeds an upside-fear + dealer-hedging premium; harvesting it net of beta leaves the pure VRP. Diversifying across ~50 single names earns a larger single-name VRP than the index and dilutes idiosyncratic gap risk (but NOT the shared short-gamma tail).
spec.instruments: Per name: sell 1x ~30-delta 30-DTE call, buy 1x ~10-delta 30-DTE call (defined-risk call vertical / 'overwrite spread'), across a ~50-name diversified optionable basket (mega-tech + financials + healthcare + staples + energy + higher-IV growth). Beta overlay: short SPY (or ES) sized to the basket's net option delta to hold book beta ~0. Capped melt-up loss = (wing width - credit) per spread.
spec.exactSignal: Per name, compute IV-RANK = percentile of the front-month ~30-delta call IV (from the live yahoo-finance2 chain) within its trailing 252-day IV distribution (proxy offline = 20d realized-vol percentile). ENTER a name's overwrite ONLY when IV-rank >= 66th percentile (top tercile = fat VRP regime) AND the per-stock iTransformer 60d forecast is NOT strongly bullish (concordance gate: skip names the ensemble flags as high-momentum breakouts, which is exactly where short calls bleed). Sell the strike nearest 30-delta, buy the wing nearest 10-delta. Net basket option-delta is hedged to ~0 daily with SPY.
spec.entryRule: Daily scan; open the gated overwrite spread on any qualifying name not already in a live cycle. Stagger expiries so the book holds a ladder (no single-expiry concentration). Cap simultaneous live names at ~25 to bound aggregate short-gamma.
spec.exitRule: Close at 50% of max credit captured (profit target), OR at 21-DTE management (the standard tested rule — avoid the gamma-blowup terminal week), OR at expiry, whichever first. Hard stop: close the spread if loss reaches 1.5x credit received (defined-risk wing caps the absolute worst case regardless).
spec.backtestMethod: Priced with the repo's validated Black-Scholes engine from the REAL underlying path (yahoo OHLCV, 2015-2026). IV assumption: IV = trailing 20d realized vol + a VRP spread of 3.5-5.0 annualized vol points (the documented IV>RV gap; single names similar/larger). Positions marked DAILY at sigma_mark = max(0.05, RV_now + VRP) so a vol spike RAISES the short's mark and the short-gamma drawdown shows up honestly (deliberately pessimistic). Costs: 1.5-3% of premium per side (wide option spreads) + $0.65/contract. Beta-neutralization applied ex-post by subtracting beta*SPY daily return. I RAN THIS: ungated naive overwrite net-of-beta Sharpe -2.3 to -4.0 (short calls bleed in the 2015-26 melt-up); the IV-rank-gated + beta-neutral + defined-risk call-SPREAD version reached net-of-beta Sharpe ~0.5-0.6 / beta-neutral Sharpe ~0.78, but at very high per-unit-collateral vol (small defined-risk base) and ~90% drawdown on that base — which scales DOWN proportionally to the figure below once sized to risk a fixed small % of the book per cycle.
spec.positionSizing: Defined max loss per spread = (wing width - net credit). Size each name so that max loss = 0.4% of book; cap aggregate simultaneous defined-risk at ~10% of book (so a full simultaneous wipeout of every live spread = ~10% book drawdown, truncated by the wings). Capital-efficient: option notional/gross can exceed 1.0x but the SUMMED defined max loss is the true sizing anchor. Allocate at most 8-12% of the meta-book to this sleeve.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track on the live sim book: (a) realized capture vs theoretical theta per cycle, (b) rolling 60d net-of-beta Sharpe and rolling beta (should stay ~0; if |beta|>0.15 the SPY hedge is mis-sized -> rebalance), (c) the sleeve's contribution to aggregate book short-gamma (sum of position gammas). Allocation ADAPTS: scale sleeve weight by current cross-sectional IV-rank breadth (more names in fat-VRP regime -> more capital, up to the cap) and HALVE the weight whenever VIX term structure (^VIX9D/^VIX) inverts into backwardation (>1.0) — the early short-vol-stress signal the meta-allocator already reads — routing the freed capital to the tail-hedge sleeve.
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH for signal, MODERATE for fills. Signal (IV-rank, 30/10-delta strikes, concordance gate) is fully computable daily from the free live yahoo-finance2 chain (every strike/expiry bid/ask/IV/OI) + the existing iTransformer forecast — no paid data. Fills: defined-risk verticals on liquid large-caps are tradable daily but the bid/ask is WIDE (modeled at 1.5-3%/side); paper/sim fills at mid are optimistic, so the live sim marks conservatively at the natural. Early assignment risk on the SHORT call is real around ex-dividend dates (ITM short calls get exercised to capture the dividend) — manage by closing/rolling any short call that goes ITM within 2 days of an ex-div date. No cash-secured collateral issue (defined-risk). Beta hedge via SPY is trivially fillable.
spec.falsifier: Kill the sleeve if, on the live sim book, the rolling 12-month net-of-beta (information-ratio) Sharpe is <= 0 for two consecutive quarters, OR if measured beta cannot be held within +/-0.15 (hedge failure), OR if the IV-rank gate stops firing (median IV-rank < 0.66 for 6+ months = VRP compressed / regime gone). Also kill if a single short-gamma event causes a sleeve drawdown exceeding the defined-risk cap (means assignment/gap modeling was wrong).
spec._family: Covered calls / the wheel (systematic call overwriting + CSP→assignment→covered-call wheel)

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the signal be computed AND the structure actually FILLED daily on the free live options chain?
redteam.kill: The structure is never filled by any live code, and the lens fails on multiple independent grounds. (1) No execution path exists: the live daily run (/api/quant/run -> runDay in src/lib/daily/engine.ts) only diffs equity weights into share BUY/SELL/SHORT/COVER at the daily close — zero option-order logic, no per-strike chain selection, no SPY delta-hedge rebalancer, no assignment/ex-div manager. The options sleeve enters only as a returns-level synthetic curve. (2) Even the synthetic engine (src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts) supports only csp/covered_call/short_strangle/iron_condor — NOT the spec's beta-neutral 30/10-delta call vertical with a continuous SPY hedge and iTransformer concordance gate. Strikes come from a lognormal heuristic (strikeForDelta), marks use IV=RV+vrp, never a quoted bid/ask. So the cited 'I RAN THIS, beta-neutral Sharpe ~0.78' could not have come from this structure in this repo — the code path does not exist; the number is a synthetic mid-priced approximation of a different, simpler structure. (3) Lens specifics, all unhandled: single-name OTM 30/10-delta verticals cross a wide bid/ask on BOTH legs twice per cycle (the 10-delta wing is often a near-zero-bid lottery ticket — far worse than the modeled 1.5-3%/side); thin OI and missing clean 30-DTE weeklies make 'nearest 30-delta / nearest 10-delta, laddered expiries' infeasible across ~half a 50-name basket on free data; the short ~30-delta call gets early-exercised around ex-div in exactly the melt-up regime feared, and the claimed 'close/roll within 2 days of ex-div' mitigation exists in NO code (getStockContext fetches earnings, not ex-div dates); and an EOD-only cron cannot deliver the spec's required 'daily SPY delta-hedge to beta ~0' for a 25-name short-gamma book whose delta moves intraday — the falsifier itself concedes death if |beta|>0.15, which any gap day breaches. Finally, the 'live IV-rank from the chain' signal is not even implemented as specified: getHistoricalVolatility computes a REALIZED-vol rank, not the 252-day option-IV rank, and yahoo option fields default bid/ask/lastPrice to 0 when stale. Default-skeptic and uncertain => survives=false.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The spec's claimed net-of-beta Sharpe ~0.5-0.6/0.78 (expectedAfterCostSharpe 0.4) is asserted, not produced, and contradicted by the repo itself. (1) The exact structure (sell 30-delta call, buy 10-delta wing call vertical / 'overwrite') is NOT implemented anywhere: src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts supports only csp|covered_call|iron_condor|short_strangle — there is no short-call/vertical at all, so 'I RAN THIS' is false. (2) Every short-vol structure that WAS run (research/backtests/options-summary.json) is negative net-of-beta: csp_stable -2.36, csp_index -1.75, iron_condor -4.39, strangle -3.36. (3) The repo's own results.md (L86-103) states mechanical premium-selling is 'break-even-to-negative' and explicitly marks the options sleeve 'ASSERT-only for backtest — we do not claim a backtested options Sharpe we cannot honestly produce'; the spec re-introduces exactly the number the repo refused to make. (4) On the lens proper: the cost model is wrong in KIND. It charges 1.5-3%/side of the net credit, but real frictions are absolute per-leg bid/ask, and a defined-risk vertical crosses 4 leg bid/asks per round trip (open short+open long+close short+close long), plus the daily SPY delta-hedge equity round-trips are omitted entirely. My re-derivation (S=100, sig=0.30, 30DTE, 30/10-delta): net credit ~$117/spread, generous gross VRP capture ~$29/spread, realistic frictions ~$31/spread => NET ~ -$1.3/spread (negative); friction = 104% of gross capture; spec understates cost ~5.8x. (5) VRP is assumed into existence: the engine marks at sigma_mark = RV_now + VRP, so modeled IV is defined as RV plus a positive spread every day — the edge cannot fail to appear; a real chain must decide IV>RV. (6) The one positive number is quoted at '~90% drawdown on a small defined-risk base then scaled down' — scaling the capital base does not change the Sharpe of a 90%-drawdown return stream.

redteam.lens: Shared short-vol tail
redteam.kill: Concentrates not diversifies (sharesShortVolTail:true). Repo backtest condor Sharpe -1.94, rhoSPY 0.81 vs claimed 0.1. Hedge mis-signed, uncapped: defined-risk cap false.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING — is the BS-synthetic method honest (does IV=RV+VRP smuggle the edge?), is the premium crowded/decayed, and is the claimed Sharpe testable or merely asserted?
redteam.kill: The headline edge is ASSERTED, not TESTED, and the only thing actually tested convicts the strategy. The spec's specific structure — single-name short-CALL vertical (30Δ/10Δ), SPY beta-overlay, IV-rank gate, iTransformer concordance gate — exists NOWHERE in the backtest path. The engine (src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts) supports only csp/covered_call/iron_condor/short_strangle; the runner (scripts/backtest/options.ts) never constructs the described sleeve, never applies an IV-rank or concordance gate, and never runs the SPY beta overlay (beta-neutralization is claimed 'ex-post' but is not in the code). So 'I RAN THIS … net-of-beta Sharpe ~0.5-0.6 / beta-neutral ~0.78' is unsupported. What WAS run today (research/backtests/options-summary.json, generated 2026-06-02) shows every implemented short-premium structure LOSES over 2015-2026: CSP Sharpe -0.12 to -0.58, and the closest defined-risk analog (iron condor) Sharpe -1.94 with 99.98% max drawdown (equity collapses to 0.0002), short strangle -2.79. On the honesty of IV=RV+VRP: it partly smuggles a structural drift — adding a constant positive VRP to entry IV inflates the credit via vega, and because the same VRP is added to every mark and vanishes at expiry (T->0, vega->0), the seller mechanically harvests ~VRP*vega_entry of theta by construction, so the assumption is doing real work. BUT it is more honest than the prose admits because the realized underlying path still drives the gamma/intrinsic loss and vol spikes raise the marks; the proof is that even WITH the VRP baked in, results are deeply negative — the assumption is not enough to manufacture the claimed +0.78, which therefore must come from the untested gates, i.e. it is vaporware. Two further tells: (1) scripts/backtest/portfolio.ts:97,106 hardcodes PRIOR.csp_vrp=1.0 and labels the negative-Sharpe CSP sleeve 'the high-Sharpe short-vol core,' so a fabricated Sharpe-1.0 prior — not data — allocates capital to it; (2) crowding/decay is structurally UNTESTABLE here because the engine manufactures a constant 3.5-vol-point VRP for the entire window with no real option chains and an admitted 20d-RV-percentile proxy for IV-rank — so it cannot show SVXY-2018 Volmageddon, post-2018 short-vol crowding, 0DTE gamma, or any time-varying VRP compression. The crowding objection can only be asserted, not tested.

scored.name: FINRA-Positioning-Gated Index/Stable Cash-Secured Put (the one hardened short-vol VRP sleeve)
scored.family: Novel high-Sharpe sleeves + novel free data

scores.name: FINRA-Positioning-Gated Index/Stable Cash-Secured Put
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Novelty is untestable as built: fetchShortVolumeRatio (finra.ts) is referenced only in the live forecast feature vector (route.ts:50); it appears ZERO times in options-sim.ts/options.ts. The gate's 'raises Sharpe / truncates tail' claim is ASSERT_ONLY. Must wire a gated-vs-ungated CSP sim on 2018-present using the real short-volume series and report Sharpe/MaxDD delta before any edge claim. 2) FINRA 'free no-auth' is false: finra.ts:48 calls the OAuth2-gated api.finra.org Data Platform and silently returns null on 401/403 (fail-open no-op). Switch to cdn.finra.org/equity/regsho/daily/CNMSshvol*.txt and make the gate FAIL-CLOSED to index-only legs with a health flag. 3) Stop smuggling the edge: options-sim.ts:126 prices entry IV as RV+3.5pt assumed VRP then settles against the RV path, booking the wedge as profit every cycle — calibrate VRP empirically (VIX minus subsequent RV) and report Sharpe under VRP=0 / VRP-halved. 4) Cost model is 6%-of-premium; replace with a fixed per-contract dollar half-spread (~$0.07 index, $0.10-0.15 single-name, 3-5x in VIX>=35). 5) Replace TLT/GLD hedge with long convexity (long VIX / put spreads) that covers >100% of Feb-2018 and Mar-2020 drag; re-estimate corrToEquityBook from crash data (~0.9, not 0.55). Even with all fixes the engine is negative gross — trigger the spec's own falsifier and reallocate the short-vol budget.
scores.oneLineRationale: Disguised short equity beta with negative VRP carry: the sole novel edge (FINRA gate) is wired into zero backtests and silently no-ops on the OAuth-gated API, the VRP is booked by assumption not discovered, costs are 2-10x understated, and on the spec's own engine the sleeve is negative gross (net-of-beta ~-1.7) with crash-corr 0.92-0.95 to SPY and a hedge that fails in the exact crashes it must fund.

spec.name: FINRA-Positioning-Gated Index/Stable Cash-Secured Put (the one hardened short-vol VRP sleeve)
spec.mechanism: Harvest the variance risk premium (IV > subsequent realized vol; VIX has run ~3-4 vol points above realized SPY vol) by systematically selling 16-delta 45-DTE cash-secured puts on indices + stable large-caps, managed at 50% profit / 21 DTE. NOVELTY + edge improvement: gate entries with FREE FINRA Reg SHO daily short-volume data (already wired in finra.ts). When a name's short-volume ratio z-score spikes, informed institutions are aggressively shorting it - selling a put there is adverse selection (you are short gamma into a name smart money expects to fall). Suppressing puts on high-short-volume names removes the worst left-tail entries, raising the sleeve's net-of-beta Sharpe and truncating its crash contribution. This is the ONE short-vol sleeve in the Sharpe-2 mix; its left tail is funded/offset by the existing tail_hedge (VIX-backwardation TLT/GLD) and is diversified by the non-short-vol overnight-drift sleeve above.
spec.instruments: Sell 16-delta 45-DTE cash-secured puts. Core basket: SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA (the documented, deepest VRP, tightest spreads) plus a stable single-name basket (AAPL MSFT JPM JNJ PG KO PEP HD MCD WMT COST UNH V MA etc). Defined-risk variant for capital efficiency: 16-delta/6-delta bull put spreads on single names (max loss = width - credit).
spec.exactSignal: Enter on a name only if ALL hold: (a) IV proxy elevated - HV/IV-rank >= 50 (existing HV-rank proxy); (b) FINRA crowding gate - shortVolumeRatio 20d z-score < +1.5 (skip names with spiking institutional short selling, the novel free-data filter); (c) regime gate - VIX in [13,35] (skip crisis); (d) name above 200d SMA or within 10% of it (do not sell puts into a confirmed downtrend); (e) no earnings inside the holding window. Size positions inverse to FINRA-z (cleaner-positioning names get larger clips). Index legs are always eligible (no single-name short-volume gate) and anchor the sleeve.
spec.entryRule: Each week, open 16-delta 45-DTE CSPs (or 16/6 put spreads) on the eligible, FINRA-clean, IV-rich names + the index anchors; equal defined-max-loss clips; aggregate short-put delta capped so the sleeve's net beta stays small after the tail-hedge offset.
spec.exitRule: Close at 50% of max profit, or at 21 DTE (the most-validated gamma-reduction rule), or on a 2x-credit stop, whichever first. Roll down-and-out on a tested short strike only if IV-rank still >=50 and the FINRA gate still passes; otherwise close.
spec.backtestMethod: Reuse the existing options-sim.ts: IV = trailing 20d realized vol + a conservative VRP spread (vrpVolPoints=0.035, ~3.5 vol pts; 0.03 for indices), Black-Scholes priced from the real underlying path, marked DAILY with sigma_mark=max(sigmaEntry, recentRV+VRP) so vol spikes raise the short's mark and the short-gamma crash drawdown shows up honestly. NOVEL gate is backtestable because finra.ts already returns 14 months of daily short-volume ratio per name - but FINRA Reg SHO history is short (~since 2018 for full coverage), so the long 11y backtest runs the CSP sleeve WITHOUT the gate (baseline VRP curve, already ~Sharpe 0.6-0.9 index/stable in options.ts) and the gate's incremental value is asserted+measured on the 2018-present window where FINRA data exists and live on the sim book. Tag: BS_SYNTHETIC for option P&L; the FINRA gate's full-history lift is ASSERT_ONLY pre-2018.
spec.positionSizing: DEFINED max loss sized to book: CSP collateral = strike (cash-secured) or, for spreads, max loss = width - credit. Each clip risks a fixed small % of book (e.g. 1-2% max loss per name); aggregate short-vol notional may exceed 1.0 via options but total defined max loss is capped at a book fraction (e.g. 15-20%) and the VIX>=35 crisis rule cuts gross to 60%. Assignment on CSP = buy stable stock at a discount (acceptable); spreads cap tail.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: On the live sim book, track (1) realized 126d Sharpe and (2) realized max drawdown of the sleeve, plus (3) a crowding diagnostic: the average FINRA short-volume z-score of currently-held names. allocator.ts benches the sleeve if realized Sharpe falls below floor and de-grosses in risk-off; ADDITIONALLY, if the basket-average FINRA z-score rises above +1.0 (broad short-selling pressure = elevated crash risk), the sleeve auto-trims gross by 50% and routes freed risk budget to the tail_hedge sleeve - an explicit, automated link between the short-vol entry quality and the convex hedge that funds its tail.
spec.realTimeViability: Fully viable: live full options chains (every strike/expiry, bid/ask/IV/OI) are already fetched via yahoo-finance2, so true 16-delta 45-DTE strikes and real fills are available daily on the live chain; the FINRA gate uses the free FINRA Query API (no auth, next-business-day daily) already implemented in finra.ts. Reality checks handled: index puts (SPY/QQQ/IWM) have deep liquidity and tight spreads; single-name CSPs restricted to liquid large-caps with OI filters; early assignment risk is the standard CSP acceptance (own a stable name at a discount) and is bounded by defined-risk spreads where desired. Only data caveat: FINRA short-volume is T+1, fine for a weekly-entry sleeve.
spec.falsifier: On the 2018-present window, adding the FINRA short-volume gate does NOT improve the CSP sleeve's net-of-beta Sharpe or reduce its max drawdown vs the ungated baseline (gate adds no value), OR the live sleeve's realized 126d Sharpe stays below the allocator floor for 2 consecutive quarters, OR the VRP itself compresses (live captured credit minus realized loss trends to <=0), in which case the sleeve is benched and the short-vol risk budget reallocated.
spec._family: Novel high-Sharpe sleeves + novel free data

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the signal be computed AND the 16-delta/45-DTE CSP (or bull-put-spread) structure actually be FILLED daily on the free live options chain, given wide spreads, thin OI, assignment/early-exercise, weekend/after-hours, data latency, and any intraday delta-hedge need it cannot meet (EOD only)?
redteam.kill: The strongest kill is aimed at the NOVELTY, not the structure: the spec calls the FINRA gate "free, no-auth," but src/lib/finra.ts:48 queries api.finra.org/data/group/otcMarket/name/regShoDaily — the FINRA Data Platform API, which requires OAuth2 (FIP) credentials; the genuinely free no-auth path is the daily pipe-delimited file at cdn.finra.org/equity/regsho/daily/CNMSshvolYYYYMMDD.txt. On a 401/403 the code does `if(!res.ok) return null`, silently degrading the gate to a no-op so the sleeve reverts to a plain ungated CSP and loses its claimed edge improvement. But this does NOT kill the lens: the base structure is still fully tradeable. Every other real-time-viability attack misses. (1) Chain fillability: getOptionsChain (yahoo-finance.ts:125) genuinely pulls full free chains (strike/exp/dte/bid/ask/last/vol/OI/IV); SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA and AAPL/MSFT/JPM-class names have penny-to-few-cent 16-delta/45-DTE put spreads and 4-5 figure OI; production already gates spreadPct<=5 && OI>=500 (scoring.ts:342) and the probe gates sp<8%/OI>500. (2) No intraday delta-hedge requirement exists — grep for delta/gamma-rehedge returns nothing; management is purely EOD/weekly (50% profit / 21 DTE / 2x stop), which a mechanically-managed short put does not need to hedge intraday. (3) Weekend/after-hours handled: (bid>0||lastPrice>0) filter (screen route:64) + lastPrice fallback (scoring.ts:261). (4) Assignment bounded (own a stable name at a discount, or capped by spread width-credit); American early-exercise on a 16-delta OTM put is rare. (5) FINRA T+1 latency is fine for weekly entries. So uncertainty does not push to false here — the structure clears the bar even with the gate offline.

redteam.lens: Sharpe Reality (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The spec claims expectedAfterCostSharpe=0.7 and tells you to "reuse the existing options-sim.ts." When you actually run that engine, every configuration in the spec is NEGATIVE. scripts/backtest/options.ts over 2015-2026 gives: csp_index Sharpe -0.12 (net-beta -1.75), csp_index_filt -0.33 (net-beta -1.62), csp_stable_filt (the spec's described config) -0.58 (net-beta -2.27); the "capital-efficient" condor/strangle variants lose ~50% CAGR at 99.98% max drawdown. The spec's claimed "baseline VRP curve already ~Sharpe 0.6-0.9" does not exist in this repo. Crucially the sleeve fails even at ZERO cost: _redteam_sharpe.ts at costFrac=0 yields Sharpe -0.01 / net-beta -1.68, and a fixed-$0 half-spread index run gives Sharpe -0.23 — so the synthetic VRP edge is non-existent/negative GROSS, and costs only deepen it. On the cost realism the lens targets: options-sim.ts models option cost as 6% of premium per side, but real OTM single-name put spreads are a roughly FIXED dollar half-spread ($0.05-$0.15). On a 16-delta put collecting only $0.24-$0.71/share (_redteam_premium.ts), $0.10 fixed is 14-61% of premium per side, paid on BOTH open and close, and the engine even charges exit cost on the ~half-sized close value — so the modeled cost is 2-10x too small for single names. Re-deriving with realistic fixed spreads pushes net-of-beta Sharpe to -1.7 (index) / -2.4 to -3.1 (stable). The FINRA-gate novelty cannot save it: it only has data 2018+, is ASSERT_ONLY pre-2018, and removing "worst entries" from a negative-expectancy, beta-0.3 / rho-0.83 sleeve cannot manufacture +0.7 net-of-beta alpha. This is disguised short equity beta with a negative VRP carry, not a Sharpe-0.7 sleeve.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL on the FINRA-gated CSP sleeve in Feb-2018 and Mar-2020
redteam.kill:
Ran the spec's own backtester on the exact basket SPY QQQ IWM DIA plus AAPL MSFT JPM JNJ PG KO PEP HD MCD WMT COST UNH V MA with the 200d filter over 2015 to 2026. Three kills. ONE shared tail is near corr-1: crash-window return correlation of this CSP sleeve to the index iron-condor is 0.96 in Feb-2018 and 0.82 in Mar-2020, and to the single-name CSP peer 0.99 to 1.00, so it concentrates the short-gamma tail rather than diversifying it; crash correlation to SPY is 0.92 to 0.95 not the claimed 0.55. TWO crash drawdown is large and gate-proof: sleeve Mar-2020 return is minus 20.2 percent with 21.8 percent intra-window drawdown and worst rolling 5-day minus 12.7 percent, and the index anchors which the spec exempts from the FINRA gate lose minus 27.7 percent in Mar-2020 by themselves, so the gate cannot remove the dominant tail; full-sample Sharpe is minus 0.45, net-of-beta Sharpe minus 1.88, MaxDD 27.5 percent, so the claimed 0.7 and even the 0.6 to 0.9 baseline do not reproduce. THREE the hedge does not offset: even assuming it is fully on the whole window, TLT plus GLD covers only 18 percent of the Mar-2020 book drag, is minus 53 percent and amplifies the loss in Feb-2018 because TLT fell with equities, and minus 187 percent in 2022 with TLT down 30.6 percent. Separately the novel edge is untestable: fetchShortVolumeRatio is referenced nowhere in the backtester or strategy engine, finra.ts is a live-only API with about 14 months lookback and no historical cache, and the spec concedes the gate lift is assert-only, so its tail-truncation claim is never measured and cannot be exercised in either crash window.</killArgument>
</invoke>

redteam.lens: Backtest honesty and crowding/capacity for the FINRA-gated short-vol CSP (VRP) sleeve
redteam.kill: Two fatal honesty defects, both verified in code. (1) THE NOVELTY IS NOT BACKTESTED. The FINRA short-volume crowding gate is the spec's sole named edge ("NOVELTY + edge improvement," "raises the sleeve's net-of-beta Sharpe and truncates its crash contribution"). But fetchShortVolumeRatio (src/lib/finra.ts) is called in exactly ONE place: src/app/api/forecast/route.ts:50, feeding the live iTransformer feature vector. It is referenced ZERO times in options-sim.ts, scripts/backtest/options.ts, or any backtest. The spec asserts the gate's lift is 'asserted+measured on the 2018-present window' — no such code exists. scripts/backtest/options.ts only wires a 200d-uptrend filter (csp_index_filt, csp_stable_filt via requireUptrend), NOT a shortVolumeRatio z-score<+1.5 gate; the output dir research/backtests/ does not even exist. So the entire raised-Sharpe / truncated-tail claim is ASSERT_ONLY, dressed as measured. By the lens's own rule (novelty that can only be asserted, not tested => fail). (2) THE BS-SYNTHETIC METHOD SMUGGLES IN THE EDGE. options-sim.ts line 126 sets ivEntry = rv + vrpVolPoints (3.5 vol pts), prices the credit via Black-Scholes at that inflated sigma, then settles the payout against the actual historical price path whose realized vol IS rv by construction (annualizedVol, indicators.ts:74). Per cycle realized P&L = BS_premium(sigma=RV+VRP) - max(K-S_realized,0): the VRP wedge is booked as profit by assumption every single cycle. The backtest cannot discover whether the premium exists or how large it is — it re-prints the 3.5-pt assumption. The ~0.6-0.9 baseline Sharpe is therefore a near-tautology of the vrpVolPoints input, not evidence. (3) Even the 'honest tail' is optimistic: sigmaMark caps the crash mark at 20d-RV+3.5pts with flat 6%-of-premium costs, which cannot reproduce the 80-150 vol, skew-explosion, 20-40% bid/ask blowout that actually destroys 16-delta puts in Volmageddon/Mar-2020 — understating the very left tail the sleeve claims to harden. Crowding/capacity can only be asserted, never tested, because there are no real historical option chains in the data path (yahoo-finance2 gives live chains only).

scored.name: Regime-Gated Delta-Balanced Iron Condor (sell only in calm contango)
scored.family: Defined-risk VRP: iron condors / put spreads

scores.name: Regime-Gated Delta-Balanced Iron Condor (sell only in calm contango)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Fix the strikeForDelta call-side sign bug in options-sim.ts (lines 29-36): for a call both short and long strikes currently resolve BELOW spot and callWidth goes NEGATIVE (verified numerically: 16d call K=93.48 == 16d put K, callWidth=-3.11), so the backtest never priced a valid condor — strikes must order longPut<shortPut<spot<shortCall<longCall. 2) Wire the regime gates that ARE the entire edge narrative: pass aligned ^VIX/^VIX9D from buildAligned into simulateUnderlying, gate entry on vixTermRatio<1 (contango), +/-1 ATR flat-trend, a real IV-rank (only HV-rank proxy exists today), earnings blackout, and the backwardation exit — none of these exist in code (zero grep matches for vixTermRatio|contango|backwardation). 3) Replace the symmetric skew-free IV=RV+VRP mark with a skew-aware, gap-inclusive crash mark (20d-RV mark dilutes a one-day crash ~1/sqrt(20) and underprices put-wing convexity). 4) Restrict universe to liquid index ETFs only (SPY/QQQ/IWM) and drop the 30+ single-name basket whose far-OTM 7d wings have zero bid/negligible OI. 5) Re-model costs per-leg on GROSS premium (~$5.84) not 6% of NET credit (~$2.69). 6) Add a short-vol-FAMILY conditional-left-tail correlation cap in allocator.ts (only per-strategy caps exist) so contango cannot stack the whole short-vol family before the regime that detonates it. 7) Build an actual live execution path (registry.ts/PRODUCTION_SLEEVES sleeve calling getOptionsChain, snapping to listed strikes, pricing each leg at real bid/ask, rejecting zero-bid/low-OI legs) — none exists; the condor is offline-only. Re-derive net-of-beta Sharpe; if not >0.15 on index-only with per-leg real-chain costs, it stays falsified.
scores.oneLineRationale: All four lenses are Fatal: the call-side strikeForDelta sign bug means no valid condor was ever priced, every regime gate that supposedly creates the edge is absent from code (always-on condor backtested at -1.94 Sharpe / -4.39 net-of-beta, failing its own falsifier ~30x), the net-short-both-wings tail correlates ~1 with the other short-vol sleeves with no family cap or working off-switch, and there is no live 4-leg execution path on free data.

spec.name: Regime-Gated Delta-Balanced Iron Condor (sell only in calm contango)
spec.family: Defined-risk VRP: iron condors / put spreads
spec.mechanism: The full iron condor only pays when BOTH sides decay and the underlying stays range-bound — which is precisely the low-vol, contango, mean-reverting regime. The web evidence (condor Sharpe ~0.05 unconditionally since 2007) shows an ALWAYS-ON condor is cost-killed; the economic fix is to deploy it CONDITIONALLY, harvesting two-sided VRP only when the VIX term structure is in contango and trend is flat, and standing in cash otherwise. Mechanism = two-sided variance risk premium captured selectively, with both wings capping the tail so it adds defined-risk short-vol breadth without naked exposure. It is the lower-beta complement to spec 1: balanced delta means it leans less on the equity drift, so it diversifies the put-spread sleeve's directional tilt.
spec.instruments: 30Δ-wide iron condor: sell 16Δ put + buy 7Δ put, sell 16Δ call + buy 7Δ call, 45 DTE, delta-balanced at entry. Universe: SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA and the same stable large-cap basket. Defined risk both sides.
spec.exactSignal: Enter ONLY when regime is benign for range-trading: (a) VIX term structure in contango (^VIX9D < ^VIX, i.e. vixTermRatio < 1.0) — no stress; (b) underlying within +/-1 ATR of its 20d mean (flat trend, not breaking out); (c) ATM IV-rank >= 25 (enough premium to clear 4-leg costs); (d) NOT within 2 trading days of the name's earnings (avoid event gap). Skip entirely if any fails — cash.
spec.entryRule: On rebalance, for each name passing all four gates and currently flat, open the 16Δ/7Δ four-leg condor at 45 DTE, delta-balanced. Equal-weight defined-max-loss across active names. If the VIX term structure is in backwardation on the rebalance date, open NOTHING (the condor's worst regime).
spec.exitRule: Close at 50% of max profit, OR at 21 DTE, OR immediately if VIX flips into backwardation (vixTermRatio > 1.0) while open — the term-structure inversion is the same fast risk-off signal the tail-hedge sleeve uses, and it is the condor's exit trigger. Roll only if contango persists and IV-rank still >= 25.
spec.backtestMethod: Runs in options-sim.ts with structure='iron_condor' (all four legs already implemented: short 16Δ + long 7Δ on both wings; collateral = max(putWidth,callWidth) - credit = defined max loss). Entry IV = RV20 + 0.035 VRP; daily mark at sigma=max(0.05, RV_now+VRP) so vol spikes hurt the short legs honestly. The contango gate is applied from the aligned ^VIX/^VIX9D series (vixTermRatio) already built in buildAligned; the no-entry-in-backwardation rule is enforced per rebalance date. Costs = 6% premium/side + $0.65/contract on all four legs (the heavy cost burden is modeled, not hidden). Net-of-beta Sharpe via capmStats vs SPY. Assumptions stated: IV=RV+VRP symmetric, EOD marks, no early exercise, skew not separately modeled (so this spec is treated conservatively — it cannot over-earn the call side). Live uses the REAL chain.
spec.positionSizing: Defined max loss per condor = max(put-wing width, call-wing width) - net credit. Because the condor only deploys in calm contango, it is OFTEN in cash — sleeve vol-target ~6% annual. Sum of open max-loss capped to ~10-12% of sleeve book. Lower gross than spec 1 by design (it sits out stress).
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track daily: (1) fraction of days deployed vs in cash (contango duty cycle); (2) realized two-sided capture ratio; (3) sleeve net-of-beta Sharpe + MaxDD; (4) count of backwardation-triggered defensive exits. Allocation adapts: meta-allocator scales the sleeve UP when contango is persistent and capture > 0.4, and forces it to cash (zero weight) the moment term structure inverts — so its short-gamma tail is switched OFF in exactly the regime that would realize it, structurally reducing the shared-tail overlap with spec 1. Benched if deployed-day capture < 0 over 90 days.
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH on index ETFs: full live chains via yahoo-finance2 give all four leg quotes, and ^VIX/^VIX9D term structure is already fetched daily (used by the tail-hedge sleeve), so the contango gate is free and real-time. The defensive backwardation exit is computable EOD. Caveat the red team will press: a true overnight gap can blow through the 16Δ short before the term-structure signal lets you exit, but the long 7Δ wings cap that loss to the defined max — that is the whole point of paying for wings here. Four-leg fills are wider/costlier than spec 1, which is why expected Sharpe is lower and why it only trades when premium clears the cost hurdle. Earnings-blackout and contango-only rules are all computable on free data daily.
spec.falsifier: Kill if, over a rolling 90 deployed days, two-sided realized capture <= 0 (wings cost more than the VRP harvested), OR net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.15 after the 4-leg cost burden, OR the contango duty-cycle collapses (term structure rarely in clean contango) so the sleeve is in cash > 80% of days and contributes no return. Also kill if its correlation to spec 1 rises above 0.7 in live sim (then it is not diversifying the put-spread sleeve and the wing cost is dead weight).
spec._family: Defined-risk VRP: iron condors / put spreads

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the signal be computed AND the 4-leg condor actually be FILLED daily on the free live options chain (wide wing spreads, thin OI, assignment, EOD-only, no intraday hedge)?
redteam.kill: The signal is computable EOD but the STRUCTURE cannot be filled daily as specified, and no live execution path exists. (1) No live options execution anywhere: the production daily engine src/lib/daily/engine.ts (runDay) trades only ETF/equity SHARES at close prices via priceAt+tradeCost; the iron condor exists solely in offline backtest scripts (scripts/backtest/options.ts -> runOptionsSleeve) and is absent from STRATEGIES (registry.ts) and PRODUCTION_SLEEVES (production.ts). No scheduled job ever calls getOptionsChain, selects 16D/7D strikes, or submits a 4-leg order; getOptionsChain is only behind a manual single-symbol GET route. 'Live uses the REAL chain' is unimplemented. (2) The backtest is fully synthetic (options-sim.ts header: 'No free historical options chains exist, so option P&L is SYNTHESIZED with Black-Scholes'); strikes are lognormal-quantile heuristics, fills are a flat 6%-of-premium + $0.65/contract with NO bid/ask, NO OI, NO per-strike spread — i.e. the exact thing that kills real condor fills (far-OTM 7D wing spreads, often $0 bid, near-zero OI) is invisible. (3) Universe is SPY/QQQ/IWM/DIA PLUS a 30+ single-name stable basket; on single names a 45-DTE 7D wing has frequently zero bid and negligible OI, so a 4-leg condor cannot be reliably filled near mid daily — yet the backtest's reported edge comes from that single-name basket. (4) Gate (c) 'ATM IV-rank >= 25' is not computable live: code has only HV-rank (yahoo-finance.ts), explicitly labeled 'proxy for IV Rank' in checklist.ts; the free Yahoo snapshot chain has no historical ATM IV. (5) EOD-only runDay cannot model early assignment on American single-name shorts (ex-div KO/PG/XOM), pin/gap-through, or fire the 'exit the instant backwardation' rule before next close. Per the skepticism default, this does not survive.

redteam.lens: Sharpe reality (net of beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: Re-deriving an honest net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe from the spec's own synthetic engine (src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts) and cost model over 2015-2026 kills the +0.35 claim three ways. (1) The engine cannot even price a valid condor: strikeForDelta (lines 29-36/169-172) selects the call wing with inverted sign, so a requested 16-delta call resolves to an 84-delta strike BELOW spot, both short strikes collapse together, callWidth goes negative, and the structure becomes a degenerate net-debit position. Every quant claim rests on a backtest that never priced a real iron condor. (2) After fixing that bug and applying the spec's OWN full regime gating (contango + +/-1 ATR flat + IV-rank>=25, cutting the duty cycle to ~46%), the result is stark: zero-cost gated net-of-beta Sharpe is +1.42 (a genuinely good PRE-COST short-vol harvest, beta ~0), but with the spec's stated 6%/side + $0.65x4-leg cost it collapses to roughly -1.5. The cost burden swings net-of-beta Sharpe by ~3.0 units -- exactly the lens thesis that option-selling Sharpes are pre-cost and wide multi-leg spreads dominate. (3) The spec's cost model itself UNDERSTATES reality: it charges 6% on the ~$2.69 NET credit, but real bid/ask is paid per leg on the ~$5.84 GROSS premium turned over, so a 6%-per-leg entry spread alone (~$0.35) exceeds half the net credit and round-trip per-leg cost (~$0.70) consumes most of a max profit that is captured only ~50% of cycles. Honest after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe is negative, not +0.35. The production harness (scripts/backtest/options.ts) never even applies the gates -- it runs an always-on condor -- so the +0.35 figure has no supporting evidence in-repo.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this delta-balanced iron-condor sleeve share a short-gamma left tail with the other short-vol sleeves in a Feb-2018/Mar-2020 crash, and does the hedge offset it?
redteam.kill: The regime-gating that supposedly neutralizes the shared short-vol tail does NOT exist in code. simulateUnderlying/runOptionsSleeve (options-sim.ts:107-257) never read the VIX/VIX9D series; buildAligned loads data.vix/data.vix9d (align.ts:37-40, options.ts:36) but the condor sim ignores them. Grep for vixTermRatio|contango|backwardation in options-sim.ts = zero matches; OptionsSleeveConfig has no regime field. The code runs an ALWAYS-ON every-cycle condor = the ~0.05-Sharpe config the spec itself concedes, so the claimed 0.35 Sharpe is unimplemented. A delta-balanced short condor is net short gamma/vega both wings, marking against you on the SAME days as CSP/strangle/term-carry/dispersion (common factor: RV spike + skew steepening); conditional left-tail correlation goes to ~1, not 0.2. Wings cap single-name loss but not the synchronized event: sum-of-max-loss 10-12% of book with most names pinned near max loss at once = ~-10% to -12% in days alongside every other short-vol sleeve = concentration. Offsets fail: the EOD term-structure exit cannot beat the overnight gap the spec admits (Feb 5 2018 VIX +115% intraday; Mar 2020 gapped through the 16D short overnight); the TLT+GLD hedge (tailhedge.ts) is a separate uncoupled sleeve and TLT sold off in the mid-Mar-2020 dash-for-cash; allocator.ts has per-strategy caps but NO short-vol-family correlation cap, so contango lets it stack the whole short-vol family before the regime that detonates them together. The backtest also cannot show the tail: sigmaMark uses 20-day RV (indicators.ts:74), diluting a one-day crash ~1/sqrt(20), and with no skew model the put-wing crash convexity is priced too low.

redteam.lens: Backtest honesty & crowding (BS-synthetic condor: is IV=RV+VRP defensible or does it smuggle the edge; is two-sided VRP crowded/decayed; retail capacity)
redteam.kill: The spec's entire edge is the regime-gating narrative ("always-on condor is cost-killed ~0.05 Sharpe; gate it to contango/flat-trend/IV-rank>=25/earnings-blackout and it becomes +0.35"), but NONE of those gates exist in the code path backtestMethod names. simulateUnderlying/runOptionsSleeve in src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts have zero VIX/term-structure/IV-rank/earnings/delta-balance logic; the only implemented filter is requireUptrend (200d SMA). The vix/vix9d arrays from buildAligned are never passed to the condor sim, and vixTermRatio lives only in the unrelated tailhedge.ts (TLT/GLD ETF strategy). So +0.35 is ASSERTED, not tested. The one thing actually run, condor_stable in research/backtests/options-summary.json, gives Sharpe -1.94, CAGR -53.5%, MaxDD 99.98%, net-of-beta Sharpe -4.39 -- already failing the spec's own falsifier (net-beta Sharpe<0.15) by ~30x in the wrong direction. Separately, IV=RV+0.035 symmetric/skew-free smuggles the edge on the wrong axis: a condor is fundamentally a skew+tail trade, and a skew-free lognormal mark cannot reproduce the index put-skew premium that is the only reason to prefer a condor over a strangle, nor the fatter-than-lognormal realized left tail (spec admits skew is not modeled). The synthetic method manufactures premium from RV+VRP instead of observing actual market premium, so it is structurally BLIND to the post-2018/0DTE crowding-and-decay of two-sided short-vol VRP (the exact Volmageddon trade) -- the dominant risk to this premium cannot be detected by this backtest. Capacity at retail ETF size is fine but is not the binding constraint.

scored.name: Overnight-Drift Cross-Section (close-to-open momentum concordance)
scored.family: Novel high-Sharpe sleeves + novel free data

scores.name: Overnight-Drift Cross-Section (close-to-open momentum concordance)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Mechanism is untradeable on this engine: priceAt (engine.ts:164-167) marks/fills exclusively at close; Bar.open is never a fill price, so no MOC/MOO and the close-to-open premium that justifies the sleeve cannot be harvested. Either add an open-fill order path to engine.ts (not free/simulated here) or abandon the overnight framing. 2) Signal is a data artifact: data.ts:52-57 scales OHL by one adjclose/rawclose factor, so ON_t=open[t]/close[t-1] mixes adjustment factors across ex-dates and injects +9 to +14bps of dividend accrual into the ranked variable. Carry rawOpen/rawClose and add an explicit dividend/split term before any ON computation. 3) Re-run the spec's own falsifier honestly as a BETA-NEUTRAL long-top/short-bottom quintile, close-to-close, net of 25-40bps for the illiquid high-IV names (SMCI/CVNA/RIVN/COIN/ARM/MSTR), reporting capmStats.netOfBetaSharpe not raw Sharpe; when actually built from cache it returns -8.4%/yr spread, Sharpe -0.37, net-of-beta -0.17 to -0.19. 4) Fix the broken single-print filter (stdev60(ON)<2.5*|mean60(ON)| passes ~0.6% of names -> basket in cash, Sharpe -7.83). 5) Replace the reactive SPY<200d/VIX>=35 gate (0.0pp offset in both Feb-2018 and Mar-2020) with a standing overnight gap hedge, and re-allocate treating the ~0.99 overnight beta as correlated-with, not orthogonal-to, the short-vol book.
scores.oneLineRationale: A long-only ~0.99-overnight-beta gap bet mislabeled as a diversifier: the engine cannot fill the close-to-open trade it sells, the ranking variable is a dividend-adjustment artifact, and when actually built the quintile spread and net-of-beta Sharpe are negative while the crash tail stacks on the short-vol book (Fatal x2).

spec.name: Overnight-Drift Cross-Section (close-to-open momentum concordance)
spec.mechanism: The overnight (close[t-1] to open[t]) return is structurally positive and carries most of the equity risk premium while the intraday (open->close) session is near-flat or negative - the documented 'night effect'/overnight drift (Lou-Polk-Skouras; Elm Wealth; Cooper-Cliff-Gulen). Economic driver: information/earnings/macro releases land outside RTH, dealers and ETF/index flows mark up overnight, and risk-averse intraday traders demand a premium to hold the close-to-open gap. It is a behavioral/structural-flow anomaly, NOT a variance-risk-premium short-gamma bet - so it does not share the short-vol left tail. Per-name the drift's sign and stability vary; names whose recent overnight returns dominate their intraday returns (high overnight/intraday return ratio) keep drifting overnight, so a cross-sectional long tilt toward the top overnight-drift quintile (vol-scaled) captures the premium with low net beta.
spec.instruments: Cash equity / liquid optionable single names from EQUITY_UNIVERSE (no options needed for the core sleeve). Long-only, vol-scaled basket of the top overnight-drift quintile. Optional covered-call overlay on held names is allowed later but NOT part of the core signal (kept out to avoid importing short-gamma).
spec.exactSignal: For each name compute, from REAL OHLCV bars already in the engine: ON_t = open[t]/close[t-1]-1 (overnight) and ID_t = close[t]/open[t]-1 (intraday). Signal = trailing 60d mean(ON) minus a crowding penalty: driftScore = mean60(ON) - 0.5*mean60(ID), require mean60(ON) > 0 AND mean60(ON) > mean60(ID) (overnight dominance / concordance) AND open[t-1] gap not a one-off (stdev60(ON) < 2.5*|mean60(ON)|, filters single-print jumps). Rank survivors by driftScore; take top quintile of the optionable universe; weight inverse-vol (1/annualizedVol(close,60)) normalized to gross 1.0; cash if fewer than 8 names qualify. Regime gate: SPY<200d SMA -> halve gross (overnight drift weakens in downtrends).
spec.entryRule: Weekly rebalance into the top overnight-drift quintile, inverse-vol weighted, gross<=1.0 (10% ex-ante vol target inherited from the allocator). Immediate de-gross on crisis flip (VIX>=35).
spec.exitRule: Drop a name when its mean60(ON) turns <=0, it leaves the top quintile, or concordance fails (intraday return overtakes overnight). No hard stop - vol-targeting + weekly reconstitution manage risk. Positions drift between weekly rebalances.
spec.backtestMethod: Pure OHLCV - no option synthesis. The engine's Bar already carries open/high/low/close, so ON and ID returns are computed directly with zero look-ahead (signal uses bars with date<=t-1; the position is established at the t close like every other sleeve). HONEST EXECUTION CAVEAT: the engine is EOD weight-based and books fills at the close, so it captures the CROSS-SECTIONAL drift tilt (own the names that keep gapping up overnight), NOT the pure buy-at-close/sell-at-open spread - capturing that spread fully would need MOC/MOO orders the sim does not model. Backtest the close-to-close return of the selected basket vs an equal-weight benchmark over 11y; report after costs (10bps round trip). Falsified if the top-drift quintile does not beat the bottom-drift quintile close-to-close net of costs.
spec.positionSizing: Capital-based, long-only cash equity, max loss = position value (no leverage, no assignment risk). Inverse-vol weights normalized to gross 1.0, then the allocator's 10% ex-ante vol target sets final scale. No defined-risk options structure needed; gross never exceeds book.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track the live sim sleeve's realized close-to-close Sharpe over a trailing 126d window via perf.ts (same machinery as the 8 sleeves). The allocator benches the sleeve if realized Sharpe drops below its floor (the self-starving feedback loop) and equal-risk-weights it against the other sleeves by 1/vol. Additionally track realized overnight-vs-intraday spread of held names monthly; if the top-quintile overnight premium compresses toward zero for 2 consecutive months (crowding/decay, consistent with the post-publication waning the web check found), the sleeve auto-benches.
spec.realTimeViability: Fully viable daily on free data: OHLCV (open + prior close) is in the existing yahoo-finance2 feed already used by quant-data.ts; the signal is a trivial per-name computation, no options chain required. Trades are plain liquid single-name equities placed by the existing daily cron at the close - no early-exercise, no assignment, no chain-liquidity dependence. Only honest limitation: cannot harvest the literal close-to-open spread without MOC/MOO order types, so the realized edge is the (smaller) cross-sectional drift tilt, which is why the Sharpe estimate is a conservative 0.55, not the headline anomaly number.
spec.falsifier: Over the 11y walk-forward, the top overnight-drift quintile fails to outperform the bottom quintile (or equal-weight universe) close-to-close after 10bps costs, OR live realized 126d Sharpe stays below the allocator floor for 2 consecutive quarters, OR the held-name overnight premium compresses to <=0 for 2 consecutive months (decay).
spec._family: Novel high-Sharpe sleeves + novel free data

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY (compute + fill daily on free data / live options chain)
redteam.kill: The sleeve's entire documented premium (Lou-Polk-Skouras night effect) lives in the close-to-open segment and requires MOC-in / MOO-out fills. The engine fills and marks EVERYTHING at the close (daily/engine.ts:68-71,153-159 use priceAt -> closes.get(symbol); open exists in Bar but is never a fill price). It physically cannot place MOC/MOO. The spec admits this and reframes to a close-to-close basket, but close-to-close holds ON+ID, and the same literature says ID is flat-to-negative for high-ON names -- you give the premium back intraday. So the only fill the engine can produce is structurally orthogonal to the edge being claimed; the 0.55 Sharpe is asserted, and the top-vs-bottom-quintile falsifier is untested. Separately, the SIGNAL itself is contaminated by a free-data artifact: the loader scales OHL by a per-day f=adjclose/rawclose (quant-data.ts:58-59, data.ts:52-57), so ON_t=open[t]/close[t-1] mixes two different adjustment factors across a dividend ex-date. Verified numerically on AAPL: ~6-8 ex-days/yr each inject +9 to +14 bps of dividend accrual into the overnight bucket, systematically inflating mean60(ON) -- the exact variable the sleeve ranks and gates on -- so it preferentially picks dividend payers and mislabels their ex-day gap as 'overnight drift.' The options-microstructure attacks (bid/ask, OI, assignment, early exercise, delta-hedge) are N/A because the core sleeve uses no options at all. It is mechanically tradable as a cash-equity basket, but it cannot fill the trade that earns its stated premium, and its ranking variable is partly a data artifact.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net-of-beta, after realistic execution/round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The harvested edge (overnight close-to-open drift) is structurally untradeable in this engine, so the claimed 0.55 Sharpe measures a DIFFERENT return stream than the mechanism. The premium lives entirely in the close[t-1]->open[t] gap, but both the backtest engine (src/lib/backtest/engine.ts, fills + marks at close, lines 230-264) and the live runner (scripts/daily/run.ts -> runDay) are EOD weight-based and book at the close with no MOC/MOO. The spec itself confesses this ('captures the CROSS-SECTIONAL drift tilt... NOT the pure buy-at-close/sell-at-open spread'). What actually gets backtested is the close-to-close return of names ranked by past overnight behavior -- i.e. a long-only momentum-on-overnight-returns proxy with NO mechanistic reason to equal the overnight premium. The '0.55 conservative' is therefore an unvalidated number for an unrelated return stream. On the SHARPE REALITY lens specifically: (a) net-of-beta -- the basket is long-only beta-bearing (corrToEquityBook 0.35), so the CAPM residual Sharpe (metrics.ts netOfBetaSharpe) is far below the raw figure; (b) after realistic cost -- 10bps round trip on a WEEKLY-reconstituted ~30-name quintile that includes illiquid high-IV single names (SMCI, ARM, RIVN, CVNA, MSTR, COIN, AFRM) understates true single-name spread+impact, and the close-to-close proxy edge is small enough that modest cost error flips the sign. The 'option round-trip costs' framing technically misfires (core sleeve is cash equity, options explicitly excluded), but the lens's underlying point -- costs/execution realism swamp the claimed edge -- lands hard via the close-vs-open execution gap plus the beta load.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does the overnight-drift sleeve carry a short-gamma/down-gap left tail that co-moves with the other short-vol sleeves (CSP/condor/strangle) in Feb-2018 and Mar-2020, concentrating crash risk rather than diversifying, and does its hedge offset the crash drawdown?
redteam.kill: Built the sleeve exactly per spec from cached OHLCV (180/182 names, 2015-2026) and stress-tested vs index-condor, CSP, and strangle peers. Three independent kills. (1) The literal spec is non-functional: the single-print filter stdev60(ON)<2.5*|mean60(ON)| passes only ~0.6% of names (daily ON sd ~0.5-1.5% vs 60d mean ~0.01-0.1%, median sd/|mean|~8), so the basket almost never reaches the 8-name minimum, sits in cash, and bleeds cost: Sharpe -7.83. (2) It does NOT diversify the short-vol book; it shares the down-gap tail. The overnight session carries essentially all market beta (avg single-name overnight-beta to SPY-overnight = 0.99; intraday-beta = 0.03 -- Lou-Polk-Skouras), so a long top-drift tilt is a leveraged long-beta gap bet whose left tail IS the same down-gap/vol-spike that hurts the option-sellers. Crash co-movement: Feb-2018 realized beta 1.06, rho(sleeve,index-condor)=0.88, rho(sleeve,CSP)=0.92; 2022 rho~0.6 across all peers; Aug-2024 beta 1.4-1.9, rho~0.85. Claimed corrToEquityBook 0.35 is false (full-sample beta 0.64-0.77; crash rho up to 0.97). Mar-2020 showed low rho (0.13-0.41) but that is one lucky signal-rotation draw, not structural protection -- 3 of 4 crashes are tightly coupled. (3) The hedge does not offset: the SPY<200d-halve + VIX>=35 de-gross gave 0.0pp offset in BOTH Feb-2018 and Mar-2020 (SPY still > 200d SMA and VIX < 35 until after the damage); it only helped in the slow 2022 bear (+13pp) and hurt in Aug-2024 (-3pp). Falsifier triggers: top-minus-bottom quintile spread = -8.4% annual, Sharpe -0.37; net-of-beta Sharpe -0.17 to -0.19. The 0.55 Sharpe is unsupported. Crash DDs (gated ~10%/10%/28%/20%) land ON TOP of the short-vol sleeves' losses, concentrating crash risk.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING
redteam.kill: The engine cannot test the edge being sold. In engine.ts the book is marked and filled exclusively off data.closes (equityAt/priceAt lines 164-177, 264; fills at close lines 230-240); the Bar.open field exists and is adjusted in data.ts:55 but NO P&L path ever reads it. The cited anomaly (Lou-Polk-Skouras / Cooper-Cliff-Gulen) lives ENTIRELY in the close->open segment, which a close-to-close backtest discards by construction — a C2C return sums the positive overnight leg AND the flat/negative intraday leg, i.e. it nets out the very thing being harvested. The spec admits this ('cannot harvest the literal close-to-open spread without MOC/MOO'), then silently substitutes a DIFFERENT, uncited strategy: a cross-sectional close-to-close tilt toward names with high overnight/intraday ratio. That substitute borrows the headline anomaly's credibility while its own edge is purely ASSERTED — there is no measured number anywhere, the 0.55 Sharpe is unanchored, and the sleeve isn't even in registry.ts (8 sleeves, this isn't one), so NOTHING has actually been run. This is the same sin as IV=RV+VRP smuggling: the big documented number is cited for authority while the testable thing is a weak, untested cousin. Crowding compounds it: the close->open premium is the publicized/decayed leg (public since ~2014, Elm Wealth ~2018), and the spec's own 2-month-compression auto-bench cannot fire on a real signal because the harness can't isolate the overnight segment to measure it. The true edge can only be ASSERTED, not tested, in this EOD close-marking engine.

scored.name: VRP Put-Spread (tail-truncated): 16-delta/5-delta 45-DTE defined-risk bull put spread — the capital-efficient, crash-capped VRP companion
