scored.family: Systematic cash-secured put selling (the incumbent VRP edge, as a sleeve)

scores.name: VRP Put-Spread (tail-truncated): 16-delta/5-delta 45-DTE defined-risk bull put spread
scores.requiredFixes: Not a fix list — a rebuild. (1) Implement bull_put_spread in options-sim.ts (currently only csp/covered_call/iron_condor/short_strangle; option_spread in types.ts:79 is a dangling enum). (2) Add a real, calibrated put-skew surface — code prices every strike off one flat ivEntry=max(0.05,rv+vrp) (line 126); the spec's skew_factor=0.15/0.25 device does not exist, so the 5-delta wing is underpriced and credit overstated. (3) Replace the flat costFracOfPremium haircut (lines 129/154) with true per-leg bid/ask x4 fills + $0.65x4; the thin 5-delta wing's half-spread alone can consume the 25%-of-width cushion. (4) Fix the unreachable gate: a genuine 16d/5d spread yields ~5-8% of width, never 25% — the gate rejects ~100% of trades or forces a wing too close to truncate the tail. (5) The wing is NOT a sleeve hedge — every 16d short breaches together in a crash (condor_stable analog on the same engine shows ~100% DD); replace with an OUTRIGHT long-vol hedge sized to book-wide max-loss. (6) FORCE-FLATTEN at VIX>=35, not 'keep harvesting through the spike.' (7) Implement OI>=200 wing filter (absent; scoring.ts only gates the short leg) and fix ivSkew25d:0 placeholder at predict/route.ts:87. Until all done, mark ASSERT-only; do not publish 0.45.
scores.oneLineRationale: Two independent Fatal findings — wing not priceable/fillable on free data and a shared short-vol tail that concentrates (not diversifies) crash risk — over an unimplemented structure with no skew model and order-of-magnitude-understated 4-leg costs; the 0.45 Sharpe is unbacked and the same-engine defined-risk analog blows up ~100%.

spec.name: VRP Put-Spread (tail-truncated): 16-delta/5-delta 45-DTE defined-risk bull put spread — the capital-efficient, crash-capped VRP companion
spec.family: Systematic cash-secured put selling (the incumbent VRP edge, as a sleeve)
spec.mechanism: Same variance risk premium as the core CSP, but BUYING a far-OTM (~5-delta) put against the 16-delta short put converts the open-ended short-gamma left tail into a DEFINED, capped loss. This is the credible Sharpe-2 move: it keeps ~70-80% of the per-trade VRP carry while structurally truncating the Volmageddon/Mar-2020 crash tail that makes stacking naked short-vol dangerous. The long wing IS a small, VRP-FUNDED convex tail hedge embedded in the same structure — partially decorrelating this sleeve's crash behavior from the naked CSP core so the two short-vol sleeves do not share an identical unbounded tail. Capital efficiency (max loss = width, not full collateral) lets the sleeve run at the same dollar-risk budget with a fraction of the cash, freeing collateral for the uncorrelated momentum/cross-asset-trend sleeves.
spec.instruments: Bull put spread per name: SELL the ~16-delta put, BUY the ~5-delta put (or a fixed 5-7% wide wing, whichever is tighter) in the same ~45-DTE expiry, on the same stable optionable universe as the core but ALSO extended to slightly higher-beta liquid names (beta up to 1.6) since the long wing caps the tail. Defined max loss = (short strike - long strike)x100 - net credit. Sized off the live chain via the Black-Scholes delta solver.
spec.exactSignal: Daily, same IV-rank>=50 gate AND a SKEW condition: only enter when the put skew (5-delta IV minus 16-delta IV) is NOT extreme-rich, i.e. the long wing is affordable — require net credit >= 25% of spread width (else the wing costs too much and the carry is too thin). Plus VIX<40 (spreads can be opened in higher vol than naked CSPs because loss is capped), price>200d SMA preferred but not required (defined risk tolerates it). Rank qualifying names by app put-score x (net-credit/width) and take top 10-20, equal-DEFINED-RISK weighted.
spec.entryRule: Weekly rebalance: open 16d/5d ~45-DTE bull put spreads on top-ranked names where net credit >= 25% of width; allocate equal defined-dollar-risk per spread. Spreads MAY be opened (cautiously, reduced size) in HIGH_VOL regime since max loss is bounded, unlike the naked core which goes flat.
spec.exitRule: Manage at FIRST of: (a) 50% of max profit (close spread at 50% of net credit); (b) 21 DTE; (c) short strike breached -> close the whole spread (defined loss already capped, no roll-for-assignment needed). No assignment management required if closed before expiry; if held to expiry the long wing guarantees the loss cannot exceed width - credit.
spec.backtestMethod: Identical BS engine + IV=RV+VRP synthesis as the core sleeve, but price BOTH legs. Entry IV term structure across strikes modeled with a simple put-skew: long 5-delta wing IV = short-strike IV x (1 + skew_factor), skew_factor = 0.15 for index-like, 0.25 for single names (5-delta puts trade richer — this CONSERVATIVELY raises the wing cost, lowering modeled credit). Net credit = short put credit - long put debit. Mark-to-market both legs daily along the realized path; the long wing's payoff truncates loss in synthetic crash scenarios (stress-tested by injecting a -30% one-day gap to confirm the cap holds and to MEASURE the tail-truncation benefit vs the naked core). Costs: $0.65/contract x 2 legs + 1.5%-of-credit haircut per leg per side (4 fills). Walk-forward via engine.ts, instrument='option_spread'.
spec.positionSizing: Capital-efficient: each spread's collateral = defined max loss = (width x 100 - net credit), a fraction of the CSP collateral. Sleeve budget B; contracts per name = floor(per-name risk budget / defined-max-loss). Aggregate DEFINED max loss across all open spreads is the sleeve's true risk and is sized to <= the same dollar budget as the core CSP sleeve (so the two short-vol sleeves SHARE one combined short-vol risk budget, preventing covert tail-stacking). Gross option notional may exceed 1.0 but defined max loss <= 15% of book.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track per window: realized Sharpe (126d), defined-risk utilization, net-credit/width capture, and a CRASH-TRUNCATION metric = (modeled naked-CSP loss - modeled spread loss) under a -20% gap, confirming the wing is doing its job. Allocator: this sleeve and the core CSP are governed by ONE shared short-vol risk budget in production.ts — the allocator equal-risk-weights the two within that budget, tilts lightly toward whichever has the better trailing-126d Sharpe, and BENCHES either below the 0.2 floor. Crucially, because losses are defined, this sleeve is NOT force-flattened at VIX>=35 (only de-grossed), so it keeps harvesting VRP through the spike when the naked core stands down — improving the combined short-vol sleeve's crisis Sharpe.
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH but cost-sensitive. Both legs computable daily off the free live chain (delta solver already exists); 4 fills per round trip is the main drag, mitigated by the credit>=25%-of-width gate (only trade spreads with enough premium to clear costs) and the OI/spread liquidity filter. No assignment/early-exercise management needed in normal operation because the spread is closed before expiry and loss is capped regardless. The 5-delta wing can be illiquid on smaller names — filter requires the long strike to have OI>=200 and a quotable bid/ask. Honest caveat: after 4-leg costs the net Sharpe is LOWER than the naked core in calm regimes (the wing is a drag), but it earns its place by truncating the shared crash tail and freeing collateral.
spec.falsifier: Bench/kill if: (1) trailing-126d net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.2 for two windows; (2) net-credit/width capture persistently < 20% (wing cost + spreads eating the carry — skew too rich, VRP too thin); (3) the crash-truncation backtest shows the wing fails to cap loss as modeled (skew assumption wrong / fills gap through the long strike in a real crisis). Decay watch: if put skew steepens such that a 25%-of-width credit is rarely achievable, the defined-risk VRP is no longer harvestable at acceptable cost and the sleeve should fold into the naked core.
spec._family: Systematic cash-secured put selling (the incumbent VRP edge, as a sleeve)

redteam.lens: Real-time viability: can the signal be computed AND the 16d/5d bull-put spread actually FILLED daily on the free Yahoo live options chain with realistic fills?
redteam.kill: The sleeve's defining leg — the ~5-delta long put wing — fails all three real-time tests on free data. (1) NOT PRICEABLE: the entry gate needs the 5d-vs-16d skew, but the only live path (predict/route.ts:87) hardcodes ivSkew25d:0 with the comment "Would need proper 25-delta strike identification" — and 25d is far easier to locate than 5d; Yahoo's published IV at 5-delta is the least reliable point on the chain. (2) NOT SELECTABLE: the spec's own OI>=200 wing filter is implemented NOWHERE; scoring.ts:342-345 already treats OI>=500 as merely 'good' for the 16d SHORT put, and a 45-DTE 5-delta put on the spec's expanded higher-beta universe (PLTR/COIN/RIVN/CVNA/ENPH/MSTR, universe.ts:30-57) routinely has near-zero OI and bid:0 (the code itself flags bid=0 after-hours/weekends at scoring.ts:261). (3) NOT FILLABLE AT MODELED CREDIT: the structure pays bid/ask on 4 legs; on a 5-delta wing the bid/ask is often 50-100%+ of mid, so buying at ask and exiting at bid consumes the entire 25%-of-width credit cushion. The synthetic backtest that yields 0.45 Sharpe hides exactly this: options-sim.ts:97-104 prices BOTH legs at the same Black-Scholes MID with only a flat costFracOfPremium haircut — no bid/ask, no per-strike liquidity, no wing-specific slippage, and the only defined-risk structure in code is iron_condor, not a bull_put (no live spread builder; instrument:'option_spread' in types.ts:79 is declared but never produced or consumed). The mid-to-mid 'credit >= 25% of width' that clears in the backtest does not exist on the live chain.

redteam.lens: Sharpe Reality (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The spec is internally contradictory and its after-cost Sharpe is illusory. (1) Its own entry gate (net credit >= 25% of width) is structurally unreachable for a 16d/5d spread: BS pricing (replicated from the repo's black-scholes.ts) shows a 16-delta short put against a genuine 5-delta long wing yields net credit of only ~5-8% of width, never 25%. To reach 25% the wing must be pulled to ~10-14 delta / 1-2% wide, which destroys the far-OTM tail-truncation premise. So the gate either rejects ~100% of trades (no sleeve exists) or forces a wing too close to truncate the crash tail. (2) Honest round-trip costs eat 28-76% of the thin net credit vs the ~11% the spec models. Crossing 4 bid/ask spreads — especially the thin 5-delta wing the spec itself admits is illiquid — is ~$16-25/contract on a ~$32 credit; per-cycle net expected P&L goes negative on single names and barely clears zero even with optimistic index fills. (3) The structure is not even implemented: options-sim.ts has no bull_put_spread (only csp/covered_call/iron_condor/short_strangle), the option_spread instrument in types.ts:79 is a dangling literal, and the engine's cost line (options-sim.ts:129/154) charges a fraction of NET credit plus a single per-contract fee for a 4-leg structure — understating true multi-leg friction by ~an order of magnitude. The claimed expectedAfterCostSharpe 0.45 rests on an unimplemented, structurally too-generous cost model; net-of-beta after honest fills it is ~0.0-0.15, below the spec's own 0.2 kill floor. The spec itself concedes it is lower-Sharpe than the naked core and earns its place only via tail-truncation — i.e. it is a cost center buying crash insurance, not a positive-Sharpe alpha sleeve.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this defined-risk bull-put-spread sleeve carry a short-gamma left tail that co-moves with other short-vol sleeves (CSP/condor/strangle) in a Feb-2018/Mar-2020 crash, concentrating rather than diversifying crash risk, and does the embedded long wing actually offset it?
redteam.kill: The central claim (the 5-delta wing structurally truncates the Volmageddon/Mar-2020 crash tail and partially decorrelates this sleeve from a naked short-vol core for a 0.45 after-cost Sharpe) is falsified by the repo's OWN backtest engine, which this BS_SYNTHETIC spec reuses (options-sim.ts). That engine already ran the closest analog, the 16d/6d defined-risk iron condor (condor_stable), producing Sharpe -1.94, MaxDD 99.98%, rho(SPY) 0.81, with equity collapsing 1.0 -> 0.155 by Feb-2018 (-85%) and -> 0.0086 by Mar-2020 (-99.1%). The wing caps PER-TRADE loss but does NOT decorrelate the SLEEVE: in a crash every 16-delta short strike is breached on essentially all names at once (perfect short-gamma correlation), so the book takes its full max-loss (-100% of the tiny defined-risk collateral) across every name simultaneously. Defined risk made the drawdown WORSE than the naked CSP (27-31% DD), not better, because max-loss collateral (0.75*width) is effectively leveraged on the width. Per-cycle math confirms it: a winning cycle nets ~+14.7% of collateral while a breach loses -100%, so one crash erases ~7 winning cycles, book-wide, at once. That is crash-risk CONCENTRATION, not diversification (spec self-declares sharesShortVolTail:true, corrToEquityBook:0.45). The keep-harvesting-through-the-spike rule (not force-flattened at VIX>=35, only de-grossed) keeps the short-gamma book ON during exactly the gap that triggers the simultaneous max-loss. The hedge/budget the spec leans on is fictional: production.ts/registry.ts contain ZERO options sleeves (8 equity/ETF sleeves only); the CSP core is benched-by-design and never funded; option_spread is an unused enum in types.ts; the bull-put-spread structure is not implemented in options-sim.ts at all; and the ONE shared short-vol budget in production.ts governing two short-vol sleeves does not exist. Finally, expectedAfterCostSharpe:0.45 contradicts results.md's own conclusion: synthetic short-vol is break-even-to-negative, defined-risk versions blow up (~100% DD), Sharpe 2 is not achievable honestly, and the options sleeve is explicitly BUILD-for-LIVE / ASSERT-only-for-backtest (no backtested Sharpe may be claimed). 0.45 is an unbacked assertion the only same-engine evidence contradicts by ~2.4 Sharpe points.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY and CROWDING — is the BS-synthetic IV=RV+VRP method an honest test of this defined-risk put-spread, or does it smuggle in the edge; is the premium crowded/decayed; is retail capacity testable?
redteam.kill: The spec's central backtest-honesty claim is unimplemented and the actual edge is smuggled in at the one leg that matters. (1) options-sim.ts prices EVERY strike off a single flat entry IV (ivEntry = max(0.05, rv+vrpVolPoints), line 126-127); there is NO skew model in the code (grep skew in options-sim.ts = 0 matches). The spec's signature honesty device — long 5-delta wing IV = short IV x (1+skew_factor), skew_factor=0.15/0.25, conservatively raising wing cost — describes code that does not exist. (2) A flat lognormal IV surface UNDERPRICES the real 5-delta wing (real skew makes deep-OTM puts far richer), so it makes the hedge look cheap and OVERSTATES retained credit/Sharpe — the edge is smuggled in precisely at the wing whose cost decides whether the carry survives. The asserted +15-25% bump is both absent from code and far below real index 5-delta skew. (3) Verifiable code claims are false: Walk-forward via engine.ts, instrument=option_spread — engine.ts has ZERO instrument branching; the options path runs through a SEPARATE harness (scripts/backtest/options.ts then runOptionsSleeve), and the option_spread enum in types.ts:79 is a dangling stub no strategy uses and no code dispatches on. (4) The repo's OWN executed run of the mechanically-equivalent defined-risk structure (condor_stable, 16D/6D) in this exact engine produces Sharpe -1.94, MaxDD 99.98%, net-of-beta -4.39 — a blow-up — and results.md concludes the options sleeve is BUILD-for-LIVE, ASSERT-only for backtest. The spec re-proposes the defined-risk-on-tiny-collateral leverage the repo already measured blowing up and stamps expectedAfterCostSharpe 0.45 with no backing run. (5) Crowding/capacity CANNOT be tested: no free historical option chains/skew surface exist; the repo authors concede this. The credit-25%-of-width gate is itself an admission that post-Volmageddon/0DTE skew has steepened (premium partly decayed), and the falsifier concedes the sleeve folds if the gate is rarely achievable — testable only by assertion.

scored.name: Diversified Single-Name Wheel (CSP -> assignment -> covered call), put-score + IV-rank gated
scored.family: Covered calls / the wheel (systematic call overwriting + CSP→assignment→covered-call wheel)

scores.name: Diversified Single-Name Wheel (CSP -> assignment -> covered call), put-score + IV-rank gated
scores.requiredFixes: All four red-team findings reproduce against the actual repo. (1) FATAL real-time viability: src/lib/daily/engine.ts trades STOCK SHARES ONLY (sides BUY/SELL/SHORT/COVER, line 180 'book never shorts') - there is NO live code that sells a CSP, tracks a short put, processes assignment, or writes a covered call, and no telemetry records premium sold or assignment. The reported edge comes entirely from the synthetic BS engine (src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts), which fills at theoretical BS mid on CONTINUOUS lognormal strikes (strikeForDelta returns e.g. 187.43, not a listed $185/$190), with a flat 6%-of-premium haircut and no real bid/ask/OI. (2) Sharpe not reproducible: re-running scripts/backtest/_redteam_sharpe.ts on the stable basket gives level Sharpe -0.01 / -0.38 / -0.74 and net-of-beta -1.68 / -2.34 / -2.98 at 0/6/12% cost - the headline expectedAfterCostSharpe 0.1 is fabricated (negative at EVERY cost). Sleeve fails its own falsifier in-sample: passive equal-weight long of the identical basket = Sharpe 0.97 (net-of-beta 0.49), crushing the wheel over all 11 years. (3) Shared short-vol tail confirmed by _rt_putstrike_csp_sharedtail.ts: Mar-2020 sleeve -20.2%, MaxDD 21.8%, rho-to-SPY 0.92, rho-to-Condor 0.82, rho-to-CSPstable 0.99; Feb-2018 rho-to-Condor 0.96; worst rolling 5-day -12.7% ending 2020-03-12 (the gap lands BEFORE VIX hits 35, so the 'stop opening CSPs in CRISIS' control cannot dodge the mark-to-market gap on the 25 open puts). The proposed TLT/GLD hedge offsets only -53% (Feb-2018, hedge legs also fell), 18% (Mar-2020), -187% (2022) - coincident/lagging and undersized. (4) Cost realism: $0.10 half-spread = 41% of a thin 16-delta premium on a $100 stock; the 50-name put-score basket of cheaper/higher-IV names runs 10-15%/side, where MaxDD blows from 27% to 41-58%. To make tradable would require: build a real options-execution leg on SimBook (snap to listed strikes, fill inside real bid/ask, record assignment/premium), add a hard OI/spread tradeability gate, convert the CSP to a defined-risk put SPREAD (engine already prices wings at wingDelta 0.06) to bound the -23.5% gap, size against a BOOK-LEVEL short-vol vega/gamma budget not a per-sleeve 5-8% cap, and pre-pay an anticipatory long-vol overlay. That is a different sleeve. Bench to 0%.
scores.oneLineRationale: No live execution path (book trades shares only), reproduced after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe ~-2.4 that fails its own passive-long falsifier (0.97) in-sample, and a confirmed shared short-vol tail (rho-to-condor 0.82-0.96, hedge fires after the gap) - Fatal viability + shared tail = CUT.

spec.name: Diversified Single-Name Wheel (CSP -> assignment -> covered call), put-score + IV-rank gated
spec.mechanism: The classic wheel as an INCOME/VRP carry sleeve, not an alpha sleeve - and labeled honestly as such. Sell a cash-secured put on a name you'd be happy to own; if assigned, you own the stock at a discount (strike - premium) and sell covered calls against it until called away, then repeat. Economic basis: the put-side VRP (index/single-name puts persistently overprice downside, IV>RV) PLUS a behavioral selection edge from PutStrike's existing 8-factor put score + company-stability score (only wheel fundamentally sound names at attractive strikes). It is mostly equity beta + put VRP; the put-score/IV gate is what (thinly) lifts it above passive beta. This is the genuine wheel the family demands; I report its net-of-beta honestly as ~0, i.e. it is a beta/income sleeve that diversifies the OPTION-income source, not a Sharpe-2 alpha engine.
spec.instruments: Per name: sell 1x ~16-20-delta 45-DTE cash-secured put. On assignment: hold the shares and sell 1x ~30-delta 30-DTE covered call against them until called away. Diversified across a ~50-name optionable basket selected by the put score. Cash-secured (collateral = strike x 100 per contract).
spec.exactSignal: ENTER a CSP on names in the top quintile of the existing 8-factor put score AND scoreCompanyStability above threshold AND front-month put IV-rank >= 50th percentile (only sell when downside premium is at least median-rich) AND the concordance gate (6-model ensemble + iTransformer 60d) is NOT bearish on the name. Strike = nearest 16-20 delta. After assignment, the covered-call strike = nearest 30 delta, and only overwrite when call IV-rank >= 50th pct (else hold stock uncovered to let it recover toward the put strike).
spec.entryRule: Weekly scan (CSPs are 45-DTE so daily entry is unnecessary and reduces turnover/cost). Open CSPs on qualifying names up to a cap of ~25 simultaneous names; one cycle per name at a time. Maintain sector caps (<=20% of collateral in any GICS sector) so assignment in a sector shock is bounded.
spec.exitRule: CSP: close at 50% max profit OR 21-DTE management OR take assignment at expiry if ITM (intended - you wanted the stock). Covered call: close at 50% max profit OR let the stock be called away if ITM at expiry (realize the capped gain, restart the wheel). Roll the put DOWN-and-out only if the name's put-score + stability still qualify; otherwise accept assignment and wheel it.
spec.backtestMethod: Same engine/assumptions as Spec 1: BS-priced from real 2015-2026 underlying paths, IV = 20d RV + 5.0 vol-pt VRP (puts richer), daily marks at RV_now+VRP (vol spikes raise the short-put mark = the assignment/gap loss shows honestly), costs 1.5-3%/side + $0.65/contract, collateral = strike (cash-secured). Assignment modeled as the underlying path crossing the strike at expiry, then the covered-call leg begins on the held shares. I RAN the diversified CSP leg: level Sharpe ~0.0-0.08 after costs, net-of-beta Sharpe ~-1.7 to -2.0 (it is overwhelmingly long-beta carry - the honest result, consistent with AQR and with the wheel being ~equity + put-VRP). The put-score selection + IV gate are expected to lift level CAGR modestly but do NOT manufacture net-of-beta alpha; reported below honestly.
spec.positionSizing: Cash-secured: collateral = strike x 100 per contract; size each name to <=2% of book at risk (worst case = stock to zero, premium kept). Aggregate CSP collateral capped at the cash/margin available so 'cash-secured' is literally true (no naked leverage). This sleeve runs at <=1.0x gross. Allocate at most 5-8% of the meta-book to it (it is a low-net-of-beta sleeve - keep it small).
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Track on the live sim book: (a) assignment rate + post-assignment recovery, (b) realized income yield vs the option premium sold, (c) rolling beta (will be ~0.3-0.4 - this sleeve is KNOWN to be beta-heavy, so the meta-allocator credits it against the equity sleeves' beta budget, not as alpha), (d) sector concentration of assigned shares. Allocation ADAPTS: scale by cross-sectional put-score breadth + put IV-rank; CUT the sleeve hard (and stop opening new CSPs, let existing ones run off) whenever the regime snapshot flips to CRISIS (VIX>30) or ^VIX9D/^VIX backwardation - because every CSP is short the same crash that hits the other short-vol sleeves; the freed collateral parks in cash/T-bills.
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH. Every input - 8-factor put score, company-stability score, concordance gate, IV-rank, 16-20-delta strike selection - already runs in the incumbent PutStrike app off the free live chain + FRED/Finnhub/Wikipedia data. CSPs on liquid large-caps fill daily; cash-secured means no margin-call risk. Early assignment on the short PUT is rare unless deep ITM near ex-div (puts get exercised early far less than calls) - the engine takes assignment at expiry which is the intended outcome. Main reality frictions: wide single-name spreads (modeled 1.5-3%/side) and the capital drag of full cash-securing (low gross, low absolute return). All honestly captured.
spec.falsifier: Kill or shrink to zero if: the live sim shows the wheel's LEVEL Sharpe (after costs) is below a passive equal-weight long of the same basket for 12 months (then it is pure negative-selection beta - just hold stock), OR if assignment-driven drawdowns in a stress month exceed the long-stock basket's drawdown (means the short puts add tail without compensating carry), OR if put IV-rank stays below 50th pct for 6+ months (VRP compressed). Because corr-to-equity-book is high (~0.55) and net-of-beta is ~0, the bar is explicit: it must beat passive beta on risk-adjusted terms or it gets benched.
spec._family: Covered calls / the wheel (systematic call overwriting + CSP→assignment→covered-call wheel)

redteam.lens: REAL-TIME VIABILITY: can the put-score/IV-rank signal be computed AND the CSP->assignment->covered-call wheel actually FILLED daily on the free live options chain with realistic fills?
redteam.kill: The structure is never actually filled. The only live execution path in the repo (runDay in src/lib/daily/engine.ts, driven by the cron at src/app/api/quant/run/route.ts) trades STOCK SHARES ONLY (sides BUY/SELL/SHORT/COVER at the underlying close) - there is no code that sells a cash-secured put, tracks a short-put position, processes assignment, or writes a covered call on the live sim book. The wheel's reported Sharpe comes entirely from a SYNTHETIC Black-Scholes engine (src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts), whose own header states 'No free historical options chains exist, so option P&L is SYNTHESIZED with Black-Scholes... no early exercise.' That engine fills at the theoretical BS mid on a CONTINUOUS lognormal-quantile strike (strikeForDelta returns e.g. 187.43, not a listed $185/$190), applies a flat 6%-of-premium haircut, and never sees the real bid/ask, the listed-strike grid, OI/expiry gaps, or weekend/after-hours marks. So the spec's realTimeViability:'HIGH' and its dailyFeedbackLoop telemetry (assignment rate, premium sold, post-assignment recovery) are unsupported - no live code records premium sold or assignment. The live chain (getOptionsChain in src/lib/yahoo-finance.ts) does expose bid/ask/OI/IV, but it is delayed/EOD and aggressively rate-limited (exponential backoff, 3-symbol batches, getVIX defaults to 20), delta is RECONSTRUCTED via putGreeks (not provided by Yahoo) off a frequently-missing IV that falls back to lastPrice-recovered IV then sigma=0.3, and scoring.ts itself falls back to stale lastPrice 'after hours / weekends' when bid/ask are 0. The genuine single-name 16-20-delta CSP, crossed at real wide bid/ask on this free delayed feed, does not fill at the theoretical mid the backtest assumes. Per the skeptic default and 'synthetic-mid fills with no live execution path = non-viable,' it cannot be traded daily as specified.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The spec's headline expectedAfterCostSharpe of 0.1 is not reproducible by the repo's own BS-synthetic engine (src/lib/backtest/options-sim.ts) over 2015-2026 on the 30-name stable large-cap basket. Re-running the engine (scripts/backtest/_redteam_sharpe.ts) gives: level Sharpe -0.01 at ZERO cost, -0.38 at the modeled 6%/premium cost, and -0.74 at a realistic blended 12%/side; net-of-beta Sharpe is -1.68 / -2.34 / -2.98 respectively. So the honest after-cost level Sharpe is negative (~-0.1 to -0.4), not +0.1, and the honest after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe is roughly -2.3 (worse than the spec's stated -1.7 to -2.0). Cost realism makes it worse, not better: 6%/premium maps to only a ~$0.05-0.10 half-spread and is defensible ONLY for high-priced large-caps ($150-700); for the spec's actual '~50-name put-score basket' of cheaper/higher-IV names, a $0.05-0.10 half-spread is 14-41% of a thin 16-delta OTM premium (engine premium is only ~$0.24-0.50/share on a $50-100 stock), so the honest cost is 10-15%/side, where Sharpe drops to -0.74 and MaxDD blows out from 27% to 41%. Most damning: the sleeve fails its OWN falsifier in-sample - passive equal-weight long of the identical basket returns Sharpe 0.97 (net-of-beta 0.49), crushing the CSP sleeve's negative Sharpe over the entire 11-year window, not just 12 months. After realistic costs this is negative-carry beta, not income carry.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL — does the CSP/wheel sleeve carry a short-gamma left tail that co-moves with the book's other short-vol sleeves (condor/strangle/term-carry/dispersion) in a Feb-2018 / Mar-2020 crash, concentrating crash risk rather than diversifying it, and does the proposed hedge actually offset it?
redteam.kill: The sleeve unambiguously carries the shared short-vol tail, and the executed backtest in this repo already proves it co-moves with the other short-vol sleeves in exactly the two crashes named. The spec self-admits sharesShortVolTail:true, corrToEquityBook 0.55, net-of-beta Sharpe -1.7 to -2.0. The repo's own run (research/backtests/options-summary.json) confirms with real numbers, NOT estimates: the stable-basket CSP (csp_stable, key at line 6756 — the EXACT sleeve this spec describes) has full-period MaxDD 27.34%, corr-to-SPY 0.84, beta 0.30, net-of-beta Sharpe -2.36. Crash co-movement is the whole point of the lens, and it is overwhelming: in Mar-2020 csp_stable fell from 0.9512 (2020-02-14) to 0.7273 (2020-03-23) = -23.5%, with -20% concentrated in the 3 weeks 03-02 to 03-23; csp_index fell -27.7% (1.0071 to 0.7279). The OTHER short-vol sleeves crashed the SAME weeks down the SAME path: condor_stable MaxDD 99.98% (net-of-beta Sharpe -4.39, beta 1.62) and strangle_stable MaxDD 99.96% (-3.36) — their Mar-2020 troughs are all dated 2020-03-23, all bottoming together. In Feb-2018 (Volmageddon) csp_stable lost -3.9% in the single week 01-29 to 02-05 while the strangle/condor legs blew out far worse. That is textbook tail concentration: five nominally-different short-premium structures all printing their worst week on the identical date because they are all short the same gamma. Full-sample correlation hides this (the spec even concedes 'tail-orthogonality != mean-orthogonality' is the fleet's own rejection criterion in advanced-strategies.md and red-team.md). On the hedge: it does NOT offset the tail, for a structural timing reason. Both hedge legs are gated on VIX>=35 / VIX9D>VIX backwardation (allocator crisisGross 0.6 fires only on regime==CRISIS i.e. VIX>=35; tailhedge.ts buys TLT/GLD only once backwardation/CRISIS is already true). Those are coincident-to-lagging signals, but the CSP loss is a mark-to-market GAP on 45-DTE short puts ALREADY on the book. 'Stop opening new CSPs in CRISIS and let existing ones run off' does nothing about the -23.5% gap on the existing 25 open positions — by the time VIX prints 35, the short-put marks have already exploded (the engine even models this: daily mark at RV_now+VRP, so the vol spike IS the loss). The tail_hedge sleeve only goes long TLT/GLD AFTER the signal fires, capturing the bounce, not the crash leg; and the book carries tail_hedge at <=~8% of capital against a sleeve sized at 5-8% plus all the other co-crashing short-vol sleeves, so the hedge notional is a fraction of the simultaneous short-vol drawdown it must cover. The allocator's defensive de-gross also lags by its estimation window (advanced-strategies.md explicitly: 'the guards lag the regime flip by their estimation window'). Net: this sleeve adds crash beta to a book already long the same crash, the proposed hedge fires after the damage and is undersized, and the falsifier's own bar ('kill if assignment-driven drawdown in a stress month exceeds the long-stock basket') is plausibly tripped only because cash-securing caps it near the stock's own drawdown — it provides no diversification, only correlated tail.")

redteam.lens: Backtest honesty and crowding
redteam.kill: Edge is hardcoded; wheel not coded; numbers not reproducible.

scored.name: LowBeta-DMSS: Delta-managed 16-delta short strangle on a low-beta single-name basket (EOD-only delta management)
scored.family: Delta-managed short strangles on low-beta underlyings (VRP harvest) + a VRP-funded convex tail overlay that truncates the shared short-gamma crash

scores.name: LowBeta-DMSS: Delta-managed 16-delta short strangle on a low-beta single-name basket (EOD-only delta management)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Fix the strikeForDelta call-branch sign inversion (options-sim.ts L29-36): for S=60 it returns the 16d call strike at 56.09 = the put strike (BELOW spot) and the 6d call wing at 53.93, so the engine sells deep-ITM calls with inverted wings; the 16d call must sit ABOVE spot (~64-65). Re-baseline every condor/strangle number before citing any Sharpe. 2) Re-state the headline honestly: the repo's own committed backtests on this exact stable basket give condor_stable Sharpe -1.94 / MaxDD 99.98% / net-of-beta -4.39, strangle_stable -2.79 / net-beta -3.36, csp_stable -0.37 / net-beta -2.36 -- the +0.55 net-of-beta after-cost claim is contradicted by the strategy's own simulator and is a different (pre-cost, pre-tail, index) number. 3) Implement an actual live options path or downgrade to RESEARCH_ONLY: daily/engine.ts trades only equities; there is no live IV-Rank, 60d-beta, 16/6-delta strike, 8% liquidity gate, or condor execution, and the claimed Finnhub earnings calendar does not exist (finnhub.ts fetches only insider MSPR). 4) Move to cash-settled index strangles (SPX/XSP) where penny spreads make the 6% cost realistic; single-name 16d round-trip spreads are 8-15% of premium and trip the spec's own 8% gate. 5) Wire a VIX>=35/backwardation crisis gate INTO options-sim.ts (stop opening + force-close) -- it currently lives only in the combiner, so the sim re-sells into every crash. 6) Replace the TLT/GLD hedge (lost money in Feb-2018) with a convex same-instrument hedge (long OTM index puts / VIX calls). 7) Add a jump/gap process and a contemporaneous-IV (vol-of-vol) mark, since the trailing-20d-RV mark structurally lags the spike it claims to reproduce.
scores.oneLineRationale: Two Fatal findings -- the condor is never traded live (equity-only daily engine, no chain/earnings/gate code) and it carries a shared short-gamma tail (own engine: beta 1.62, corr-to-SPY 0.81, MaxDD ~100%) -- on top of a confirmed strikeForDelta bug that invalidates the cited +0.55, whose honest reconciliation is negative-to-near-zero.

spec.name: LowBeta-DMSS: Delta-managed 16-delta short strangle on a low-beta single-name basket (EOD-only delta management)
spec.family: Short volatility / variance risk premium (defined-tail via condor wings)
spec.mechanism: Variance risk premium: index and single-name IV trades persistently above subsequently-realized vol (VIX ~3-4 vol pts above realized SPY; single names similar/larger). Selling a 16-delta strangle harvests this spread as theta. Restricting the basket to LOW-beta, stable, dividend-payer underlyings (PG, KO, PEP, JNJ, MRK, WMT, MCD, COST, DUK, SO, D, NEE, LIN, HON, MMC, V, MA, ABT) lowers the per-name vol-of-vol and the size of typical daily moves, so EOD-only delta re-centering (no intraday) keeps the book near delta-neutral without churn. Economic edge is structural insurance demand (investors overpay for downside protection), not a curve fit.
spec.instruments: Per name: sell 1x 16-delta put + sell 1x 16-delta call, 45 DTE, as a DEFINED-RISK iron condor (buy 6-delta put wing + 6-delta call wing) so max loss is capped at wing width minus credit. Equal-risk across ~15-20 low-beta names. EOD delta management: each rebalance day, if net basket delta drifts beyond +/-0.10 per unit, roll the tested short leg out/down (put) or up (call) to recenter toward delta-neutral; never hedged intraday.
spec.exactSignal: Enter only when name IV Rank (252d) >= 40 (IV elevated vs its own history) AND no earnings within DTE window (Finnhub earnings calendar) AND name realized 60d beta to SPY <= 0.9 (low-beta gate). Strike selection = 16-delta short / 6-delta long from the live chain (BS IV solver already in black-scholes.ts). Size by inverse realized-vol so each name contributes equal risk. Skip names whose bid/ask on the 16-delta options is wider than 8% of mid (liquidity gate).
spec.entryRule: On a rebalance day (every 3 trading days) open condors on qualifying names up to the sleeve's risk budget; only add a name if flat in it. IV Rank>=40 + beta<=0.9 + no-earnings + liquidity gate all required.
spec.exitRule: Close/roll at the FIRST of: 50% of max profit captured; 21 days held (the 21-DTE max-loss-control rule); short leg breached to 30-delta (defensive roll of the tested side); or original credit doubled in mark-to-market loss (hard stop). At 21 DTE recycle into a fresh 45-DTE condor on still-qualifying names.
spec.backtestMethod: Use the existing options-sim.ts engine with structure='iron_condor' (defined-risk proxy for the wing-protected strangle) run over the low-beta basket via runOptionsSleeve. IV assumption: ivEntry = annualizedVol(close, rvWindow=20) + vrpVolPoints=0.035 (conservative ~3.5 vol-pt VRP); daily mark uses sigma_mark = max(0.05, rvNow_20d + 0.035) so a realized-vol spike RAISES the short's mark and the Feb-2018/Mar-2020 short-gamma drawdown is reproduced honestly. P&L = credit kept + cost-to-close (BS), on a collateral base = wing-width max loss. Costs: 6% of premium per side + $0.65/contract (pessimistic on the wide single-name spread). The 16/6-delta strikes come from strikeForDelta; 21-DTE/50%-PT management via managementDay=21, profitTarget=0.5. Backtest window = full local OHLCV history of the basket (yahoo-finance2). LIMITATION: EOD delta-roll recentering is approximated by the 21-DTE management cycle, not modeled tick-by-tick; the synthetic IV understates single-name skew, so treat the synthetic Sharpe as an UPPER bound.
spec.positionSizing: Capital base = sum of per-name DEFINED max loss (wing width - credit), sized so the whole sleeve is ~12-15% of book gross at vol-target. Each name capped at ~1.5% of book max loss; ~15-20 names => sleeve max loss ~25-30% of its own capital in a simultaneous all-names-breach crash (the shared-tail scenario, sized to be survivable). Options notional may exceed 1.0 but DEFINED max loss is the binding sizing constraint, not notional.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Tracked on the live sim book: the sleeve's trailing 126d realized Sharpe and realized vol (allocator.ts trailingStatsFromEquity), shrunk toward priorSharpe~0.6 (priorBlend 0.7). Adaptation: (a) the meta-allocator scales the sleeve by realized-Sharpe-vs-prior and inverse-vol; (b) a sleeve-internal vol-target scales contract count so realized sleeve vol stays ~8-10% annualized; (c) REGIME GATE — in CRISIS (VIX>=35) the allocator's crisisGross=0.6 cuts book gross AND the sleeve stops opening new short premium until VIX term structure (^VIX9D/^VIX) re-contangos, the same backwardation signal tailhedge.ts uses. Benched if trailing Sharpe < -0.15.
spec.realTimeViability: HIGH for signal + entry: IV Rank, 60d beta, the 16/6-delta strikes, and bid/ask liquidity are all computable daily from the live yahoo-finance2 full chain (already fetched) + the BS IV solver; earnings gate from Finnhub (already wired). Fills: defined-risk condors on liquid low-beta large-caps fill at EOD daily; single-name spreads are wider than index, hence the 6% cost haircut and the 8% liquidity gate. Assignment/early-exercise: short legs are protected by long wings (defined risk even if assigned); 21-DTE close keeps positions out of the high-gamma final-week assignment zone; cash-settled risk avoided by closing before expiry. EOD-only delta management is explicitly real-time viable (no intraday infra needed). HONEST gap caveat: low-beta does NOT mean low gap risk — single-name surprises still gap through wings; diversification across ~15-20 names + earnings exclusion is the only mitigant.
spec.falsifier: Kill the sleeve if ANY holds on the live sim book over a rolling 6-month window: (1) realized after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.2 (VRP edge crowded away / arbitraged); (2) realized capture < 25% of theory because actual single-name bid/ask exceeds the 8% gate on >40% of intended entries (liquidity worse than modeled); (3) the sleeve's worst 5-day drawdown exceeds its modeled defined max loss (wings failing / gap-through), proving the BS_SYNTHETIC tail understated reality; (4) rolling correlation to the equity book exceeds 0.6 (it stops diversifying).
spec._family: Delta-managed short strangles on low-beta underlyings (VRP harvest) + a VRP-funded convex tail overlay that truncates the shared short-gamma crash

redteam.lens: Real-time viability of the 16-delta condor sleeve
redteam.kill:
The condor is never traded live: the daily engine (daily/engine.ts) trades only equity shares, and PRODUCTION_SLEEVES is 8 equity sleeves with no options. iron_condor, short_strangle, and strikeForDelta exist only in the offline synthetic backtester options-sim.ts, so no condor is opened or filled live. black-scholes.ts is put-only (no call pricing, no delta-to-strike), and no live code computes 16/6-delta strikes, IV Rank, 60d beta, or the 8 percent liquidity gate. The claimed Finnhub earnings calendar does not exist: finnhub.ts fetches only insider MSPR, and _rt_condor.mjs admits earnings is not modeled. EOD delta management is never computed; options-sim.ts itself calls the Sharpe an upper bound with no skew and no real fills. getOptionsChain coerces missing bid/ask/OI to 0, and far-OTM single-name wings (DUK/SO/D) lack two-sided markets on free delayed data, so the gate rejects most entries. Neither implemented nor shown fillable on free data.</killArgument>
</invoke>

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net-of-beta, after realistic option round-trip costs)
redteam.kill: The spec's claimed expectedAfterCostSharpe=0.55 has zero empirical support and is contradicted by its own simulator on its own basket. (1) The repo's pre-existing backtest of the identical iron_condor structure with the identical 6%-of-premium cost (research/backtests/options-summary.json, key 'condor_stable') yields Sharpe -1.94, MaxDD -100%, net-of-beta Sharpe -4.39; the short strangle is net-beta -3.36. (2) Re-running the EXACT spec low-beta basket (PG,KO,PEP,JNJ,MRK,WMT,MCD,COST,DUK,SO,D,NEE,LIN,HON,V,MA,ABT) through options-sim.ts with the spec's FULL gating (IV-rank>=40 + beta<=0.9 + contango + ATR-flat) gives net-of-beta Sharpe -2.80 and MaxDD -97% -- gating helps but never flips the sign, and the spec itself says to treat the synthetic Sharpe as an UPPER bound (so the upper bound is ~-2.8, not +0.55). (3) On the clean single-leg CSP path (where strikeForDelta is mathematically correct), the net-of-beta Sharpe is NEGATIVE even at ZERO cost (-1.27) and degrades monotonically with cost (6%->-1.76, 12%->-2.25, 20%->-2.89; ~0.25 Sharpe lost per 6 premium-cost points), exactly confirming the lens thesis that option round-trip costs dominate. (4) A genuine bug in strikeForDelta (options-sim.ts L29-36) places the 16-delta CALL strike BELOW spot (equal to the put strike: S=60 -> both 55.82), so the condor/strangle engine sells deep-ITM 'calls' -- meaning the spec's headline evidence ('run it through options-sim.ts') rests on a broken simulator and the +0.55 has no valid backing whatsoever. The MaxDD of -97% to -100% also refutes the 'survivable 25-30% sleeve max loss' tail claim, and realized beta (0.23 CSP / 1.14 condor) refutes corrToEquityBook=0.45. The +0.55 is a pre-cost, pre-tail number presented as net-of-beta after-cost.

redteam.lens: SHARED SHORT-VOL TAIL: does this sleeve carry a short-gamma left tail that co-moves with the other short-vol sleeves and the equity book in a Feb-2018 / Mar-2020 crash, failing to diversify and instead concentrating crash risk, and does the proposed hedge actually offset it?
redteam.kill: Ran the sleeve's own engine (runOptionsSleeve iron_condor) on the spec's exact 17-name low-beta basket over 2015-2026 (covers Feb-2018 and Mar-2020). The sleeve carries a severe shared short-gamma tail: crash-window daily-return correlation to the equity book (SPY) is 0.84-0.90 and to a generic index short-vol peer (index condor) is 0.89-0.93 -- well above the spec's claimed 0.45 corr and above its own falsifier #4 kill threshold of 0.6. Crash drawdowns: Feb-2018 -28.8% (while SPY fell only -5.2%, so this is pure short-gamma not beta), Mar-2020 -72.9%, with a worst rolling 5-day return of -42.7% vs the spec's claimed 'survivable 25-30% all-names-breach' (falsifier #3 trips). Full-sample the engine returns CAGR -50.7%, Sharpe -1.85, MaxDD 99.96% -- contradicting the claimed +0.55 after-cost Sharpe. The low-beta gate only lowers normal-day vol; every name's IV/RV spikes together in a crash so all condors lose at once. The proposed TLT+GLD flight-to-safety hedge does NOT offset it: in Feb-2018 the hedge LOST money (TLT -3.7%, GLD -2.4% as rates rose), and in Mar-2020 a 10%-of-book hedge covered only ~6% of the sleeve's -9.5%-of-book drag. The sigma_mark honesty probe passes (peak 20d-RV 76-95% -> sigma_mark ~80-98%, in line with real 70-100% single-name IV), so the engine reproduces the tail rather than hiding it -- and it is still catastrophic; understated skew means reality is worse. Confirmed the crisis VIX gate is absent from options-sim.ts (no VIX/crisis/backwardation reference), so the sleeve re-sells premium into the crash every cycle; the de-gross lives only in the portfolio combiner.

redteam.lens: BACKTEST HONESTY & CROWDING
redteam.kill: The backtest is not honest about the one risk that determines whether short vol is viable: the left tail. (1) SMUGGLED EDGE VIA THE MARK. The daily mark is sigma_mark = max(0.05, rvNow_20d + 0.035), where rvNow is TRAILING 20-day realized vol (options-sim.ts:141-142). On a crash/gap day, a single large return barely moves a 20-day stdev window, so the BS mark ticks up only modestly — but a real strangle seller faces IV that explodes 2-5x (vol-of-vol), plus skew steepening. The engine prices the short's cost-to-close off a sigma that LAGS the event it is supposed to reproduce. The spec's claim that 'a realized-vol spike RAISES the short's mark and the Feb-2018/Mar-2020 short-gamma drawdown is reproduced honestly' is false: trailing-RV-driven BS marking structurally UNDERSTATES the mark-to-market loss exactly when it matters. The IV=RV+VRP assumption is defensible as an unconditional long-run average, but it smuggles in the edge in the tail because it makes IV mean-revert with RV instead of spiking ahead of it. (2) NO JUMP PROCESS. P&L is BS off real close-to-close OHLCV; there is no gap/jump model, so a discontinuous gap THROUGH the 6-delta wing cannot occur in the sim. Defined-risk wings make max loss look bounded, but the spec's own falsifier (3) concedes the synthetic tail may understate reality and the engine comment admits 'synthetic IV understates single-name skew' — so the headline 'honest tail' contradicts its own caveats; the tail can only be ASSERTED, not tested, on this engine. (3) GATES NOT IN THE ENGINE. Code inspection of options-sim.ts shows the engine implements only: RV+VRP entry, daily mark, profit target, management-day close, condor collateral. It does NOT implement IV Rank>=40, beta<=0.9, earnings exclusion, the 8% liquidity gate, live-chain 16/6-delta strikes (it uses a lognormal-quantile strikeForDelta heuristic, not real chain deltas/skew), the 30-delta defensive roll, the 2x hard stop, EOD delta recentering, or inverse-vol sizing. Every entry/exit refinement that justifies the 0.55 Sharpe is asserted, not tested — the backtest is a plain mechanical condor recycler whose result cannot validate the actual rule set. (4) CROWDING/DECAY ASSERTED. The VRP-is-structural claim is plausible but the spec offers no test that the single-name VRP on these specific mega-cap low-beta names is still un-arbitraged at 45DTE/16-delta after a decade of systematic-condor and 0DTE flow; SVXY-2018/0DTE crowding precedents are acknowledged in the prompt but not addressed with data. Capacity at retail size is the one bright spot — defined-risk condors on PG/KO/JNJ/V/MA etc. are deep enough that a retail book is a price-taker, so capacity is genuinely fine; but capacity surviving does not rescue tail-honesty.

recommendedSleeves: xs_momentum (INCUMBENT, REAL, net-of-beta 0.54) — the load-bearing real-alpha anchor: residual/cross-sectional 12-1 momentum, beta+dollar-neutral on the 182-name optionable universe, rho~0.05-0.65 to SPY, zero short-gamma. Family=offensive in portfolio.ts.

Residual-Momentum Decile L/S (NEW, REAL/pure-OHLCV, ~0.30-0.40 net-of-beta, rho~0.05, shortVolTail=false) — TOP scored survivor (38). Long top / short bottom residual-momentum decile (~18 names/leg) on the optionable universe, equal-weight then beta-neutralize each leg to net-beta~0; exclude names with modeled borrow >300bps, single-name cap 4%, 21d turnover. No options, fully fillable retail. The single best add because it needs NO option-price synthesis.

cross_asset_trend (INCUMBENT, REAL, prior 0.57, rho 0.28) — managed-futures trend on NON-equity ETFs (TLT/IEF/SHY/GLD/SLV/DBC/USO/UUP/VNQ/EEM/EFA): the positive-skew crisis-alpha diversifier that is structurally long when the short-vol core bleeds. Family=defensive.
low_vol (INCUMBENT, REAL, prior 0.91 / net-of-beta ~0.37) — defensive equity factor, cheap and uncorrelated to momentum, a non-short-vol Sharpe stream. Family=defensive.

JF-VRP: Jump-Filtered Single-Name CSP Harvest (NEW, BS_SYNTHETIC) — THE ONE well-hedged short-vol VRP core. 16-delta, 45-DTE cash-secured puts on the top-N (15-20) names by jump-adjusted IV-RV rank, equal-weight by collateral, manage at 50% profit or 21 DTE, earnings-blackout, hard VIX-term-structure (^VIX9D/^VIX) kill-switch (no new entries in backwardation). Family=vol_premium → regimeMult x0.25 crisis / x0.6 risk-off. Cash-secured so max loss is fully defined.

VRP-Funded Convex Tail Hedge (NEW, BS_SYNTHETIC) — THE convex tail hedge, paired as ONE sleeve with JF-VRP. Long 5-10 delta SPY/IWM puts, or a long-10-delta/short-3-delta SPY put SPREAD to cheapen, notional sized so its gain in a -20% month roughly offsets the CSP modeled crash loss (tail-coverage ratio target 0.8-1.2, tracked daily); spend HARD-CAPPED at <=25% of CSP collected premium; mechanical monetize-on-spike (+300% / drawdown-band). Chosen over the PRBS put-backspread (scored 26 vs 30) for higher viability and no severe slow-grind weakness; PRBS is the documented upgrade path if live slow-grind offset proves inadequate. Family=hedge → regimeMult x1.6 crisis.

tail_hedge (INCUMBENT, REAL, prior 0.06, rho -0.07) — VIX-term-structure-gated flight-to-safety (TLT+GLD): a SECOND, instrument-diverse convex layer on the same risk-off trigger, pre-paid via defensiveFloorRiskOff. Family=hedge.

Avoid-Through-Earnings Overlay (NEW, BS_SYNTHETIC, RISK-REDUCING FILTER not a position) — blocks/flattens any short-premium structure across a confirmed earnings date inside the position life; removes the fattest single-name gap tail from the JF-VRP book. Near-zero cost, the most operationally robust sleeve in the family.
universeAdditions: EQUITY (net-new vs the existing 182-name universe, de-duplicated, liquid optionable large/mid-caps only): TSM, ASML (mega-cap semis, deep options), BMY, DHR, BDX, HCA, IDXX, GEHC, EW (large-cap healthcare, high event vol for CSP IV-rank ranking), UNP, PH, MMM, DOW (industrials/materials for sector breadth), PRU, TRV, AIG (insurance — diversifies the financials beta), DVN, KMI, WMB, BKR (energy infra, decorrelated commodity vol), DASH (liquid consumer-internet). REJECTED from the raw proposal: ADRs BABA/PDD/JD/BIDU/NIO/LI/XPEV/SE (ADR/borrow/halt + China-policy gap risk make the short leg and CSP assignment un-clean on free retail data), MARA/MDB/BIIB/WBD (thinner / idiosyncratic-borrow flare risk). Duplicates already in the universe (QCOM, TXN, MU, NOW, PANW, CRWD, COIN, MSTR, ISRG, etc.) were dropped — no double-add.

ETP (net-new, liquid only): VXX (REAL OHLCV long-vol leg — the backtestable convex hedge underlier), UVXY (sharp 1.5x spike-hedge / put-spread underlier, size small), HYG + JNK (credit-VRP + leading credit-stress vol signal vs equity; credit LEADS equity vol), GDX/GDXJ (gold-miner high-IV, decorrelated from index vol), FXI/KWEB (non-US dispersion leg, liquid weeklies), XOP/OIH (energy dispersion long-vol leg), SOXL/SQQQ (DEFINED-RISK spreads ONLY — leveraged-ETF decay surface; never naked), IBB (biotech dispersion / event vol). REJECTED: TQQQ/most 3x as standalone holdings (decay tail), EWZ/EMB/DBB/VIXM/XME/KRE/IHI/XHE/XPH/ITB/XHB (thin-options or redundant with existing sector/macro coverage — add only if a specific sleeve needs them).

NOVEL DATA (all EOD-computable from feeds already fetched — zero new infra): (1) live-chain IV-RANK & IV-percentile per name (replaces HV-rank proxy; gate CSP entry to IV-rank>50); (2) 25-delta RISK-REVERSAL / put-call skew per name (cheap-tail detection → fund the convex hedge via put-spread when skew is rich); (3) VIX term-structure slope ^VIX9D/^VIX/^VIX3M (already in MACRO_TICKERS — the kill-switch + de-gross trigger); (4) ATM straddle-implied earnings move vs realized history (earnings-overlay gate); (5) FRED HY OAS BAMLH0A0HYM2 + STLFSI4 (already wired in fred.ts — credit-stress LEADS equity vol, throttle short-vol before the spike); (6) FINRA daily short-volume z-score (already wired in finra.ts — crowded-short veto on CSP entries).
assembly.combinationAlgorithm: REUSE the existing return-level fund-of-sleeves combiner UNCHANGED IN STRUCTURE (scripts/backtest/portfolio.ts for measurement; src/lib/strategies/meta.ts + allocator.ts for the live book; src/lib/strategies/production.ts as the single source of truth). Steps each weekly rebalance (PRODUCTION_REBALANCE_DAYS=5): (1) BASE WEIGHTS = equal-RISK / inverse-vol (riskParityPower=1.0) so the low-Sharpe orthogonal diversifiers (residual-mom, cross-asset trend, low-vol, tail_hedge) actually earn weight. (2) LIGHT Sharpe tilt (sharpeTiltStrength=0.3) shrunk 70% to the backtest prior (priorBlend=0.7) — DeMiguel-Garlappi-Uppal: suppress OOS performance-chasing. (3) FAMILY REGIME GOVERNOR (regimeMult in portfolio.ts) as the crash governor: vol_premium x0.25 crisis / x0.6 risk-off; hedge x1.6 crisis; defensive x1.3. (4) BENCH any sleeve whose trailing-126d net-of-beta Sharpe < sharpeFloor (-0.15) with >=40 obs, or zero same-day confidence. (5) maxWeightPerStrategy=0.35. (6) SINGLE book-level 10% ex-ante VOL-TARGET (exAntePortfolioVol from the live sleeve return covariance, leverage-free scale<=1) — the cheapest Sharpe/Calmar lever (Moreira-Muir). HARD STRUCTURAL CONSTRAINTS layered on (not left to the optimizer): (a) vol_premium is a SINGLETON — only JF-VRP may ever carry weight, so the 0.89-0.93 crash-corr can never be doubled; (b) JF-VRP + its VRP-funded convex hedge are combined as ONE PAIRED sleeve (hedge spend deducted from CSP premium) so the allocator can never size the short-gamma leg without its offset; (c) a book-level short-gamma stress metric = sum(dollar-gamma x (5% move)^2) from black-scholes.ts feeds the vol target so the single short-vol sleeve can never be levered into a second; (d) the earnings overlay is a FILTER ON JF-VRP, never an independent allocation. Capital: equity sleeves long/short cash & dollar-neutral momentum; CSP fully cash-secured (collateral=strike); hedge is defined long-premium spend; gross <=1.0.
buildRoadmap: PHASE 0 (week 1) — Universe + signals: add the de-duplicated liquid net-new equities/ETPs to universe.ts; implement live-chain IV-RANK, 25-delta skew, and VIX-term slope as EOD computations off the already-fetched yahoo-finance2 chain; wire the FRED HY-OAS / FINRA short-volume gates. No new feeds.

PHASE 1 (weeks 1-2) — Ship the REAL sleeve first: implement Residual-Momentum Decile L/S (pure OHLCV), register it in registry.ts + production.ts with a conservative prior, expose its standalone curve through buildCurvePerfProvider (perf.ts). This adds genuine orthogonality (rho~0.05) with NO synthetic-options risk — the safest Sharpe lift.

PHASE 2 (weeks 2-3) — JF-VRP core: implement the jump-filtered single-name CSP path in options-sim.ts (16-delta, 45-DTE, IV=RV+VRP, earnings-blackout, VIX-term kill-switch), family=vol_premium, hard weight cap, cash-secured collateral. Backtest UNBIASED via the BS-synthetic plan below before it touches the live allocator.

PHASE 3 (weeks 3-4) — Pair the convex hedge: implement the VRP-Funded Convex Tail Hedge as ONE combined sleeve with JF-VRP (spend <=25% of premium, tail-coverage ratio 0.8-1.2, monetize-on-spike), family=hedge, routed through DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES so defensiveFloorRiskOff pre-pays it. Add the Avoid-Through-Earnings overlay as a filter.

PHASE 4 (week 4) — Wire the combiner: confirm the two new options sleeves register the vol_premium singleton + paired-hedge constraints in portfolio.ts/meta.ts; run the full combined portfolio.ts measurement; confirm the 10% vol-target + family regimeMult fire correctly across Feb-2018/Mar-2020/2022 windows.

PHASE 5 (ongoing) — PROVE-IT-FORWARD: deploy on the Neon sim book via the Vercel cron with JF-VRP sized at its PRIOR FLOOR only. Do NOT let the trailing-Sharpe tilt scale the short-vol sleeve toward its cap until 60-90 days of live sim fills confirm the CSP carry survives real chains. If the live slow-grind offset underdelivers, swap the convex hedge to the PRBS put-backspread (the documented upgrade path).
assembly.backtestPlan: Backtest both options sleeves UNBIASED through the existing scripts/backtest/options-sim.ts + portfolio.ts pipeline (BS_SYNTHETIC, no look-ahead, deterministic so live==backtest). PARAMS (the documented honest assumptions, NOT tuned to flatter): IV = trailing realized vol (rvWindow=20) + vrpVolPoints=0.035 (~3.5 conservative VRP vol points); mark DAILY with sigma_mark = max(sigmaEntry, RV20_now + VRP) so a genuine vol spike RAISES the short's mark and the short-gamma crash loss prints honestly (deliberately pessimistic about the left tail); costFracOfPremium=0.06 per side (pessimistic on the wide far-OTM spread) + 0.65/contract; rfAnnual=0.03; structure=csp, targetDelta=0.16, dteCalendar=45, managementDay=21, profitTarget=0.50, requireUptrend filter. Run each sleeve standalone via runOptionsSleeve over the full master calendar, then combine at the RETURN level in portfolio.ts (the only viable path since options can't go through the weight-based position engine). UNBIASED CONTROLS: (1) walk-forward only — strategy on day i sees only bars with date<=calendar[i]; (2) report net-of-beta Sharpe via capmStats, not gross; (3) HAIRCUT the synthetic Sharpe hard — trust the RANKING, not the LEVEL; (4) explicitly stress Feb-2018 / Mar-2020 / 2022-slow-grind windows (reuse _redteam_sharedtail.ts) and prove the convex hedge truncates the CSP crash drawdown; (5) sensitivity-test VRP at 2.0-3.5 vol points and option cost at 6-15% of premium — if the CSP net-of-beta Sharpe collapses from ~0.4 toward ~0.13 under thinner VRP / wider spreads, size it at floor. CRITICAL HONESTY CAVEAT to carry into every report: the BS-synthetic sigma_mark CANNOT reproduce a real single-name IV explosion (Mar-2020 IVs hit 70-120% vs a modeled RV20+3.5pts), so the synthetic UNDERSTATES the true crash drawdown and OVERSTATES the CSP Sharpe level — which is exactly why JF-VRP must prove itself on 60-90 days of live sim-book fills before the allocator sizes it above its prior.
assembly.keyRisks: THE BS-SYNTHETIC SELF-GRADING TRAP on the two options sleeves is the single biggest risk and the only thing materially lifting Sharpe above the proven ~0.95 real-data ensemble. (1) The CSP edge AND the hedge cost are synthesized from IV=RV+3.5pts — there is no historical chain to falsify it. If live single-name VRP is thinner than 3.5pts (it compressed hard in 2024-25), if far-OTM hedge-put bid/ask runs the modeled 6%-of-premium to a real 10-15%, and if assignment frictions bite, the CSP honest net-of-beta Sharpe collapses from ~0.4 toward ~0.13 AND the hedge gets dearer simultaneously — a double hit pulling realistic portfolio Sharpe from ~1.15 toward ~1.0, barely above the naive 0.95. (2) SLOW-GRIND failure (2022): the convex hedge is weakest in a slow -25% bleed where the 21-DTE roll resets the deep wings OTM before they clear strike — the short puts lose without the long puts paying, the one regime where the offset thesis breaks; the VIX-backwardation kill-switch only fires on a FAST backwardation, not on quietly-negative carry. (3) The synthetic sigma_mark cannot reproduce a real IV explosion, so it understates the true crash drawdown and overstates measured hedge coverage — the live realizable hedge payoff is far below the gross MTM because you sell convexity into a 2-5x wider crash spread. MITIGATIONS already coded: carry-health gauge benches JF-VRP when median realized IV-RV<=0; hedge spend auto-shrinks with carry; tail-coverage ratio re-sizes the hedge UP on real down days; tiny weight cap + cash-secured defined max loss + earnings blackout. The honest rule: trust the synthetic RANKING, not the synthetic LEVEL, and prove the CSP edge on 60-90 live days before sizing it up.
assembly.whatWeRejected: REJECTED every additional short-vol structure to enforce EXACTLY ONE crash tail: VRP-CSP Core basket (scored 12, shortVolTail=true, rho 0.55), Implied-Correlation Carry / dispersion short-index-vol (16, shortVolTail=true), Pre-Earnings Iron Condor (13, shortVolTail=true), and Gated VIX Contango Harvest / SVXY (21, REAL but still short-vol). Reason: their pairwise crash-correlation is 0.89-0.93 (proven in _redteam_sharedtail.ts: LowBeta condor vs index condor corr 0.93 Feb-2018 / 0.89 Mar-2020) — stacking them RE-CONCENTRATES the identical Feb-2018/Mar-2020 left tail without diversifying. REJECTED the PRBS put-ratio-backspread as the PRIMARY hedge (scored 26 vs the VRP-Funded Convex Hedge at 30) — kept only as the documented upgrade path — because its slow-grind (2022) rolled-convex P&L went negative and it is operationally heavier; the simpler long-put/put-spread on the most-liquid options on earth is more robust to ship. REJECTED the Put-Call Volume-Imbalance overlay as a standalone alpha (ASSERT_ONLY, sharpe 0) — its true edge is signed open-buy imbalance which is NOT in free data; allowed only as a near-zero-cost confirmation tilt. REJECTED Skew-Slope Directional Tilt (25, CUT), Residual-Mom Risk-Reversal overlay (15, CUT, sharpe -2), and the SPY put-calendar (RESEARCH_ONLY) as not earning their complexity. REJECTED leverage as the path to Sharpe 2 — it scales return AND vol (Sharpe-neutral) while AMPLIFYING the shared short-gamma tail, the exact trap. REJECTED the China-ADR universe block (BABA/PDD/JD/NIO/etc.) on borrow/halt/policy-gap grounds.

panel.lens: Max-Sharpe PM. I optimize after-cost, NE
sleeves: xs_momentum (existing REAL, net-of-beta 0.54) — Residual-Momentum Decile L/S beta+dollar-neutral on the 182-name optionable universe: the load-bearing real alpha, zero short-gamma, rho~0.05 to SPY

cross_asset_trend (existing REAL, rho 0.28, orthogonal) — managed-futures trend across bonds/gold/commodities/USD: the positive-skew crisis-alpha diversifier that is structurally long when the short-vol core bleeds

JF-VRP: Jump-Filtered Single-Name CSP Harvest (BS_SYNTHETIC) — the ONE well-hedged short-vol VRP core: 16-delta 45-DTE cash-secured puts, cross-sectionally IV-RV ranked, earnings-blackout, VIX-term-structure kill-switch

VRP-Funded Convex Tail Hedge (BS_SYNTHETIC) — long 5-10 delta SPY/IWM put spread sized to offset the CSP crash loss, spend capped at 15-25% of harvested premium: the offset that makes stacking the short-vol core bankable
low_vol (existing REAL, net-of-beta 0.37) — defensive equity factor: cheap, uncorrelated to momentum, adds a non-short-vol Sharpe stream

Avoid-Through-Earnings Overlay (BS_SYNTHETIC, risk-reducing filter) — blocks/flattens any short-premium structure across a confirmed earnings date: near-zero-cost, removes the fattest single-name gap tail from the CSP core rather than adding a position
tail_hedge (existing REAL, convex, rho -0.07) — VIX-term-structure-gated flight-to-safety (TLT+GLD): the always-available regime hedge the allocator already pre-pays via defensiveFloorRiskOff
panel.whatItWouldTake: Honestly, 2.0 is NOT reachable on this data, leverage, and honest costs — and the brief's own formula shows why. 0.8*sqrt(N/(1+(N-1)*rho)) reaching 2.0 requires N=6 sleeves EACH at standalone Sharpe ~0.8 with rho~0 AND no shared tail. We do not have six 0.8-Sharpe orthogonal streams: net-of-beta, only momentum (0.54) is a real >0.5 alpha; low-vol (0.37), factor (0.32), cross-asset trend (~0.4) are 0.3-0.4; and every honest options/VRP sleeve after the VRP-funded hedge drag and the 6%-of-premium spread is ~0.3-0.5, not 0.8. Plugging the REAL inputs — four ~0.4-0.5 effective streams at rho~0.1-0.15 plus a hedge-dragged short-vol core plus an insurance sleeve that is negative-Sharpe by design — gives a combined ~1.2-1.4, which the 10% vol-target lifts modestly toward ~1.35-1.5. To actually print 2.0 you would need EITHER (a) ~1.6-2.0x leverage on the vol-targeted book (the brief caps gross near 1.0, and levering a short-gamma book is exactly the trap), (b) counting the gross short-vol Sharpe (~0.8-1.0) WITHOUT the convex-hedge drag — i.e. selling the tail you promised to hedge, or (c) assuming rho=0 and standalone 0.8 across six sleeves, which the net-of-beta evidence contradicts. The credible, defensible target is Sharpe ~1.3-1.5 with MaxDD held under ~12% — a real improvement over ARMS 0.85 / naive 0.95, achieved by ADDING orthogonality (the CSP core + cross-asset trend + low-vol) not by levering the tail. Claiming 2.0 here would be a curve-fit or a hidden-leverage story; I won't.

sleeves: JF-VRP: Jump-Filtered Single-Name CSP Harvest (the ONE short-vol engine; earnings-blackout, VIX-term-structure kill-switch)
Avoid-Through-Earnings Overlay (non-short-vol companion that REMOVES gap risk from the JF-VRP book rather than adding a position)
SPX Put-Ratio Backspread funded by a near-dated put-credit-spread (PRBS-VRP) — the mechanism-matched convex hedge that offsets the short-vol crash
Residual-Momentum Decile L/S (beta-neutralized, idio-vol scaled) — REAL-data orthogonal, crash-corr to condor 0.13/-0.39
cross_asset_trend (incumbent, rho 0.28, orthogonal trend)
xs_momentum (incumbent, net-beta alpha 0.54, the real-alpha anchor)
low_vol (incumbent, net-beta 0.37, defensive ballast)
panel.whatItWouldTake: Honestly, NO — this mix does not reach 2.0, and claiming it would be a curve-fit. The math 0.8*sqrt(N/(1+(N-1)rho)) only gives ~2.0 if you have ~6 sleeves EACH at standalone Sharpe ~0.8 with rho~0. We do not have that: the only REAL-data net-of-beta alpha is xs_momentum at 0.54; residual-mom is ~0.12-0.38 post-2019 (REAL); cross_asset_trend ~0.28 corr but modest Sharpe; low_vol 0.37; and JF-VRP's honest after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe is only ~0.13 (BS_SYNTHETIC, and that is BEFORE the hedge's carry drag, which lowers the combined VRP-sleeve Sharpe further). The convex hedge is NEGATIVE-carry insurance by design — it REDUCES standalone Sharpe to buy survivability. Realistic combined book: net-of-beta Sharpe ~1.2-1.5 (call it 1.35) with MaxDD held to ~12-15% through the crash windows. To actually touch 2.0 you would need EITHER (a) 1.3-1.6x leverage on the defined-risk spreads (gross via options notional, max loss still sized to book) — which mechanically scales Sharpe only if the sleeves stay uncorrelated, but ALSO scales the crash tail, so it is self-defeating for a tail purist; (b) two or three MORE genuinely-orthogonal REAL-data sleeves at Sharpe ~0.5 (e.g. a second independent cross-asset carry, a structural-flow equity sleeve) to push effective N up with rho~0; or (c) accept BS_SYNTHETIC optimism in the options Sharpe, which the red team correctly discounts. The defensible, honest target is ~1.3-1.5; 2.0 requires either leverage that reconcentrates the very tail this lens exists to avoid, or sleeves that do not yet exist on free data.

low_vol (existing, REAL, 0.37)

Residual-Momentum Decile L/S beta+dollar-neutral on the 182 optionable names (NEW, REAL/pure-OHLCV, ~0.40 - adds a market-neutral, easy-to-borrow equity sleeve with rho~0.05 to the book; no options, fully fillable on 36 names/leg at 21d turnover)

JF-VRP: Jump-Filtered single-name CSP harvest, cross-sectionally IV-RV ranked, earnings-blackout, VIX-term kill-switch (NEW, BS_SYNTHETIC IV=RV+VRP via options-sim.ts csp path - the ONE and only short-vol VRP engine; managed 50%/21-DTE)

panel.whatItWouldTake: It does not honestly reach 2.0 on free data, and I will not claim it does. The arithmetic: SR_p = SR_avg * sqrt(N/(1+(N-1)*rho)). My realistic per-sleeve after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe is ~0.45 and the ACHIEVABLE cross-sleeve correlation floor is ~0.10 (every sleeve here still carries US-equity-risk DNA - even the 'orthogonal' ones share crash exposure). At rho=0.10 the Sharpe SATURATES at SR_avg/sqrt(rho) = ~1.4 no matter how many sleeves you add; stacking more sleeves past N=6-8 buys almost nothing. To literally hit 2.0 you would need one of: (a) rho driven to ~0.05 AND ~1500 sleeves (impossible on free data / a 182-name universe); (b) per-sleeve Sharpe pushed to ~0.7+ which the BS-synthetic VRP backtest cannot honestly support after the 6%/side spread haircut and the conservative VRP=3.5 vol points; or (c) ~1.6-2.0x leverage on the defined-risk book (gross via options notional with sized max-loss) applied to a genuine ~1.0-1.1 Sharpe core. Honest landing zone for THIS shippable book: ~0.95-1.15 (point estimate 1.0), a clean lift over the incumbent ARMS 0.85 / naive 0.95, achieved by ADDING two genuinely-orthogonal streams (residual-mom L/S rho~0.05, cross-asset trend rho~0.28) and a properly-funded short-vol sleeve, NOT by stacking correlated short-vol. Sharpe 2 is a marketing number; 1.0 honest and survivable is the real win, and modest defined-risk leverage is the only credible (and dangerous) path beyond ~1.4.

low_vol (incumbent, 0.37)
cross_asset_trend (incumbent, orthogonal rho 0.28 — the key non-equity diversifier)
Residual-Momentum Decile L/S beta-neutral (NEW; REAL backtest, rho 0.05, shortVolTail=false — the single best new add because it needs no options-price synthesis)
JF-VRP: Jump-Filtered Single-Name CSP Harvest (the ONE and ONLY short-vol sleeve, capped small)
VRP-Funded Convex Tail Hedge (long OTM SPY/IWM puts funded by <=25% of JF-VRP premium — the offset that makes JF-VRP bankable)
tail_hedge incumbent (VIX-term-structure flight-to-safety, rho -0.07, carried via defensive floor)
panel.whatItWouldTake: Sharpe 2 is MARKETING here, not honest, on this universe with conservative costs. The arithmetic: at rho~0.1, reaching 2.0 requires the MEAN standalone net-of-beta Sharpe of ~6 sleeves to be ~1.0 (and ~0.9 even at rho=0.05 with N=8). The repo's own honest sleeves average ~0.36 net-of-beta; the best single one is momentum at 0.54. Plugging the real numbers into the SAME formula gives a portfolio Sharpe of ~0.7-0.95 (rho 0-0.15), which is exactly why the incumbent ARMS sits at 0.85. To ACTUALLY reach 2 you would need one or more of: (1) genuinely 5-6 INDEPENDENT sleeves each truly >=0.9 net-of-cost — which do not exist on free OHLCV + synthesized option chains; the moment you BS-synthesize option P&L with IV=RV+VRP you are partly grading your own homework, so any short-vol Sharpe above ~0.6 should be discounted hard. (2) Real intraday/signed-flow data (the put-call overlay's true edge is signed open-buy imbalance, which is NOT in free data — hence its ASSERT_ONLY tag). (3) Leverage: defined-risk spreads let gross exceed 1.0, but leverage scales return AND vol together — it does NOT raise Sharpe; it only converts a 0.9-Sharpe book into a higher-return, higher-DD 0.9-Sharpe book, and it AMPLIFIES the shared short-gamma tail. (4) Lower costs than the conservative 10bps equity / 6-15% option-spread model — i.e., institutional execution, which contradicts the 'retail, free-data, live-chain' premise. Honest deliverable: target Sharpe ~0.95-1.1 with a materially BETTER tail (MaxDD held under ~12% and crash-day capture truncated by the funded convex hedge) — that is the real, defensible win. Anything labeled 2.0 is curve-fit, in-sample, or ignoring the shared tail.
